# Thread: My bumpy road to optimal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-bumpy-road-to-optimal.400/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-24T18:34:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-bumpy-road-to-optimal.400/post-5458>

I feel the need to start this journal to write not only my progress, but also my thoughts and how I feel...And I do I feel today? Awful!!

I feel so bad in my skin, I would love to feel better and lose weight, but I keep messing things up. I sometimes have the feeling that I'm missing the best years of my life. Was so happy to sleep well lately, I had so much energy! I know how to feel better, but why can't I stick to it??

So today will be a new start... I missed the BAB, but will eat protein and fat for the rest of the day. No CT for today. I worked my way up to 50 minutes baths at 50 degrees and I really enjoy it, no problem at all now with cold water.

I'll be back soon...I can do it to myself, I deserve it!!

_Edit: I'm adding here my whole story. Careful, the road is bumpy, long and hazardous_

I'm 32 y/o, from Montreal, Canada. French speaking (keep that in mind as you read and see weird structure and grammar)

Was on the chubby side as a kid. My mom is a wonderful cook, and loved to feed us with deserts at every meal, and we would also consume a lot od processed food, few fruits and veggies. The normal average diet in the 80's... Even as a kid, I had problem sleeping. I remember having difficulties falling asleep, and alwayd waking up to early, driving my mom crazy

Many years later, I gained a few pounds when I changed job, and the parade of diets started. I lost those extra 15 pounds pretty easily, kept exercising moderately and was eating heatlhy (as healthy as a SAD can be...). Meantime, I quit my job and decided to go back to school. I was 27. I had to go back to my mom's for the first year, studying to get accepted at university. I started couting calories, but would let go if I went overboard and wasn't to worry about it. The next year, I had to move away for university, getting in a pretty demanding program. Lots of studying, exams every week, it was very crazy and I wasn't used to all that by-heart (?). Hell began. I found out that I was a pretty anxious person, and I had a really hard time staying focused and studying for hours. I've always been a sugar lover, but it became a love/hate relationship. Everytime I was studying and finding myself stressed out, I would end up in the pantry, binging on whatever was avalaible.

At that moment, I was weighting 126 lbs. I was obsessed to lower that weight at 120. Couldn't do by dieting, especially that I was binging more and more often, so I began purging. The first year, it would happen only once a week or once every two week. Nothing to worry about, right? I know it wasn't good, but I could stop whenever I would like...Second year, I was missing working out, which I stopped the previous year because of school. I do road biking at summer, and I discovered spinning classes. I was hooked. I could attempt 4-5 classes a week. I became very strict on my diet. It was easy on week days, with school and everything, but I would blow it up every week-end. Every monday morning was a new beginning, more strict than the previous one. And every week-end was worst, with more food, more sugar, more everything. I was miserable.

My third year of university was the worst. I was now 30. I don't even know how I could have succeed in all my classes. My sleep got worst also. I would binge at night when I could not sleep, week days or not, showing up at school the next morning exhausted, with huge dark circles under my eyes and feeling dehydrated. I begged a friend of mine to prescribe me Ritalin so I could study better (and knowing it's also an appetite suppressor), but sleep got even worst, so I didn't use it long. Then I got Wellbutrin for anxiety, and as it was also affecting my sleep, I went on sleeping pills. Was sleeping, but was never feeling rested in the morning. I don't really like to use medication, and after 3 months I stopped all that cold turkey. This was the end of third year and I was wondering if I would ever feel better and if my life was meant to be a constant battle with my weight, my mind and food. At that point, I was avoiding all social life, especially going out to restaurants or having a drink with friends. Everything would trigger a binge and a night in hell.

4 months prior to end my degree, I had enough and was tired of harming my body that way. Up to then, I saw a few therapists, but it didn't really help. A friend of mine told me about hypnosis and I decided to try it. It changed my life around. I loved it, and it helped me change some behaviors related to stress and low self-esteem. I still do it from time to time, with therapist or by my-self, it,s really relaxing. I then decided it was time to stop purging. I knew the binges would probably go on for a while, and I was ready (well, I thought I was) to accept gaining some weight through the process. At the same moment, I tried cutting out all wheats because I was feeling bloated all the time. Good move, but every week-end I would still binge on pastry, sugar, cake and ice cream. During that time, sleep got even worst, and I barely sleept for 3 months. And was binging at night... I put on 10 lbs in 4 months. And was still feeling miserable, really vulnerable with food and couldn't see the end of it.

Last August, I discovered Robb Wolf and read The Paleo Solution. That's when I understood I was a true sugar addict and that I had to stay completely away in order to avoid binges. They became less frequent (maybe once a month, sometimes twice), with a week in and there where I completely lost it and hit bottom again. I also knew I had Adrenal fatigue, and thought it would go away with time, supplementing and lowering stress. It did lowered because now I sleep a little better, but as I'm wrinting right now, It's 6 in the morning and I've been up since 3 o'clock...Still a little work to do here obviously...

A little before Christmas, I saw Dr kruse's blog, but wasn't thinking much of his BAB and no snack at night...It's only at the beginning of this Febuary that I went back and read a little more...And decided to try it. Not long after, I started CT. I was not losing weight (I don't weight myself much, too much stress for me), but was feeling leaner and sleep was an hundred time better. I never recall sleeping that well my entire life!! Had so much energy too!!

And two weeks ago, thinking I was doing good, I went out with a friend, had a little (too much) wine, and that was it, i was started for two weeks... I hit bottom again. It's been a really long time being so bad. Usually my feet slide and the next day I'm up and I go on, but I got a lot of stress at work, sleep became bad again and was having mini-binges here and there, having a hard time to come back on the right track. I even had a bagel, after MONTHS of not having any gluten. Stupid me!! And of course comes with it this feeling of self disgust and low esteem. I can be quit a drama queen also, calling my sister in tears, telling her I'll never do anything good with my life and that I'm tired to put all my energy on my weight lose goal. My sister is an angel, BTW. And so are my friends. i don't know how I would have been through this without their support. Love you all s the more difficult, insulin being high and sugar calling me everywhere. Then I start to feel better and want to keep this feeling...

Next week I'll see my new doctor here in Montreal and will ask him blood testing. Can't wait to see how much of a mess I am. I gained another 20 lbs since last summer. Seems to be upping 10 lbs at the time. I don't feel pretty good in my skin, but I know weight isn't the goal here (or should not be anyway). I want to get healthier, to feel better, and I know weight is gonna drop as a secondary effect. I don't have a weight goal. I know I will never go back to low 120's, I think it was to hard to maintain for me. I'll leave that to my body to decide where optimal is. I'm harmed it a lot, and now I have to focus on healing it and trying to love it on the way.

I know hormonal imbalance are responsable for the mess that I am, but that a psychological problem started the machine. Low self-esteem, wanting to be perfect, not good at managing stress...Over exercising, anerexia/bulemia...adrenal, thyroid and sexual hormones imbalance...Have I said how glad I am to have discovered Jack kruse?? That forum is going to help me much, too! I'm sadly amazed to see that lots of you have an history similar to mine. I thought that I was the only one constantly fighting with food...Your support is already helping me a lot. Thank you my virtual friends

Will try to go back to sleep now an hour or two

### Slone07 — 2012-03-26T21:34:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-bumpy-road-to-optimal.400/post-6523>

I hope by the time you read this you feel better. I understand the cycle you're in and it sucks. My best advice is to do one thing at a time. Do the LR eating protocol for 8 weeks and THEN add CT. It may not be a popular opinion around here, but I \*get\* what's going on with you because I go whole hog at first, too. If you look at the POST-LR Support Thread, you'll see that many of them started having problems when they added in CT. I'm not saying you shouldn't go through with CT, but going through that as well as the issues of getting settled into the LR protocol and making it habit is a LOT.

Sleep is your friend. Get some melatonin and 5-HTP and/or GABA to help you stay there and get regulated on your sleep cycle. You don't have to take them forever, but you owe yourself a good, SOLID night's sleep.

I've done HCG very successfully...but this LR has me much healthier mentally, which allows me to stick to it better. The LR is less crazy making. So far, just focusing on the LR with the understanding that I may gain weight (I started off expecting to and made sure I was okay with that), has led to 100% eating 100% of the time. I'm only a week in, but that's still pretty big for me. Personally, and I'm a huge HCG fan, I'd do the LR before you do anything else, given my research on leptin.

You've got this. You can totally do it...if you stay away from the second week "comfies," where you get lax. I know, I'm really one to talk as I'm still in the first week, but I've been there with everything else before. Even HCG. I feel for you. I get it. A lot of other people here do, too...and look how successful they are! Let's do this.

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-27T02:47:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-bumpy-road-to-optimal.400/post-6709>

All right...The week-end went ok, but still struggling to follow the prescription...No problem with the BAB, but if I wait to much for lunch, I find myself eating what ever's on sight: chocolate, nuts, cocco manna. Yesterday I binged on cocco manna!! That thing is so expensive, I ate half of the jar in one sitting!! Ewww

I have to stop foccussing about weight. Everytime I do, I put pressure on myself (ok, I HAVE to loose weight, my clothes doen't fit, summer is coming, blah, blah, blah), I start to binge. Have to come back on earth...What is the goal here? Getting optimal, feeling better, sleeping better and eventualy lifting heavy things again. I love reading Jason Seib posts on Everyday Paleo. He wrote on Scale Addict 2: "Losing weight is not what you want and pursuing a number on your scale is not the path to success....Change your perspective to something healthier. Forever!"

Will go to bed early tonight and will rock this week

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-28T16:25:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-bumpy-road-to-optimal.400/post-7701>

Yesterday went pretty good. The most difficult is not eating at night. I'm having a BAB (up to 75g of protein), light lunch and a decent dinner. I est until I am feel full, and sometimes a little beyond at dinner to make sure I'll be fine in the evening, but yesterday I was hungry a few hours after dinner. Thought about eating crap for a minute, but talked myself out of it and had water instead. Went to bed still hunggry and tried to sleep anyway, but couldn't...

I got up around 11:30 to get a few bites of salmon, and fell asleep as soon as I went back to bed. Got up once in the night and woke up with the alarm at 8. I usualy wake up by myslef around 7, so I was pretty surprised to hear the alarm!! I feel great this morning, after 2 nights sleeping really good

I miss my cold baths...Might go back to CT tomorrow (working late tonight). I was doing pretty good, could stay in 50-55 degrees water for an hour without shivering and I was warming up in a few minutes. I'm feeloing so sleepy after those sessions, it might help me to sleep even better...

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-30T03:34:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-bumpy-road-to-optimal.400/post-8546>

Today went ok...BAB ever: 85g of protein!! Trying to kill the hunger I'm having at night. Ate little for lunch. Wasn't hungry but since I wanted to get in a cold bath, had sardines and avocado and was really full.

Spend at hour in the tub at 60 degrees, feet in, but was freaking a little after I got out cause my toes where numb, white and really cold! Will go colder next time to go back at 50-55 degrees like last week, but will be more careful with the toes...

Had curried veggies and shrimps for dinner, ate til full, hopefuly enough to go throught the evening and the night this time. I always tell myself I'll be fine, but find myslef hungry around 9... We'll see tonight how it goes...

Fifth day with no binging, I feel much better than last week. My mom is coming to visit Sunday and will stay at my place the entire week. She's gonna think I'm really weird eating all that food and taking cold bath... She doesn't know about my bulemia history and all the crap I went through. I feel a little bad not telling her, but I don't know how she would react...  It's almost all behind me now, but sometimes I'd like to reach out to her to get some support.

Oki, so it's 8:30 now, lights are down, I'm gonna relax with a book and some ice packs on my belly, while the AC is on (its 39 out...my mom will definetly think I'm crazy! lol Won't be able to turn the AC on while she's here, she's always cold)

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-30T15:28:55+0300

The bad news: went to bed around 10:45, aslept by 11:30, and wide awake at 3:00  And that was it. 3 and half an hour of sleep. I was feeling so sleepy after CT yesterday afternoon!! I know cortisol is the culprit and wreck all the party. Anyway, I'll survive I guess.

### MamaGrok — 2012-03-31T19:15:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-bumpy-road-to-optimal.400/post-9280>

How's the fat in your BAB?

Are you doing some natural cortisol things, like sunlight on the eye first thing, total darkness at night, bright sun in mid-day if possible, even if just for a minute, ...? It definitely takes time and those mid-night awakenings can be the suck till it fixes itself. Some people also notice certain supplements at the wrong times can keep them awake, like Vit D at night.

> **AbetterME;5492 wrote: I'm sadly amazed to see that lots of you have an history similar to me. I thought that I was the only one constantly fighting with food...Your support is already helping me a lot. Thank you my virtual friends
>
>
>
>
> It's really amazing, isn't it? This is something you really just aren't supposed to talk about, even more so than depression, I think. Good grief, I had binge eating disorder for TEN YEARS before I realized it was something other than a problem with self-discipline. !! I still haven't told any of my family, not even dh really knows that it was to the extent of a true eating disorder. I don't have any of the usual history, either - distressed childhood, mother fixated with food or weight, shame, overweight child, obsessed with size as a child, nothing.
>
>
>
> How many women (men, too) are out there suffering, alone, with no hope for change because they've been battling this for years and see no way out; doctors only want to drug them, if they even know there's a problem.

> THERE IS HOPE! There is a way out!!!
>

> > AbetterME;6745 wrote:
>

>
> > No problem with the BAB, but if I wait to much for lunch, I find myself eating what ever's on sight: chocolate, nuts, cocco manna. Yesterday I binged on cocco manna!! That thing is so expensive, I ate half of the jar in one sitting!! Ewww
>
>
>
> I binge on coconut stuff, too, except for oil. Totally can't handle it. And I also can't be late for meals, especially at the beginning of my LR journey, or I'd be like a starved wild animal, wild-eyed and desperate for anything. (All the more a miracle that I can now skip lunch! It took MONTHS to get there.)
>
>
>
> I have to stop foccussing about weight. Everytime I do, I put pressure on myself (ok, I HAVE to loose weight, my clothes doen't fit, summer is coming, blah, blah, blah), I start to binge. Have to come back on earth...What is the goal here? Getting optimal, feeling better, sleeping better and eventualy lifting heavy things again.

Just an idea that came to me, and this may not work for you, but I wonder if it might help to give yourself permission to have a \*little\* focus on weight. What if, instead of expecting to lose weight \*now\*, you make it a goal to lose 10lb in 6 months? (I know it's always hard for me to do that, b/c I want to have that "moderate, reasonable" loss of 1-2lb/month "they" always talk about, but that never happens for me.)

That way, you do have weight as a (background) focus, and can maybe then give yourself permission to quit expecting it today. After all, my weight loss happens in stalls & spurts - nothing for a month, then 3lb, then nothing for a month, then 2lb ... so I can't expect to lose this week, or next (but I might), but by the end of six months, something will probably happen.

LOL, if I had ever known, two years ago at nearly 190lb, that it would take TWO YEARS to lose 50lb, I would have cried my head off, but ... it's better than two years going by with the same old same old, not losing, and more importantly, not having control over binges, not sleeping well, not able to go 2hrs without eating, not able to follow the nourishing diets I believe in, not having energy to play with my children, not being able to hike, not being able to get off the couch, still having dry rough skin, still melting down every evening around 6 o'clock ...

(MammaGrok, I don't know how to respond with quotes!!)

I cook eggs in CO, and that's about it...It is enough??

About the cortisol issue things...Yes...doing all that...Vit D at morning also...I've seen great improvement in the last months. Not still quit there, but it's coming.

I could try it your idea on mini-focussing on weight...Weighting myself is a huge stressor for me, I get frustrated and it has often lead to binges in the past. I try not to concentrate on aesthetic goals, telling myself I'm not doing this to lose weight but to feel better. If the weight comes off, I'll be happy, and if it doesn't, well, I'll have to accept myself like that. I have focussed so much on my weight on the past, I don't want to define myself as a number on a scale anymore. Of course, I want to lose weight, but the journey here should be about treating my body the way it deserves it, and once everything falls into place, the weight should come off. I wish I could be, let's say, at 145 by my birthday (beginning of June), but don't want to think about it too much cause if it goes up, I'm just gonna be mad at myself

### MamaGrok — 2012-04-01T15:23:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-bumpy-road-to-optimal.400/post-9740>

>
> Probably not. Unless you intentionally use a lot, it's not enough. I cooked my eggs in a whole tablespoon of CO (more than most people use for cooking) and intentionally stirred it all in so it all got used, but I still saw better satiety when I upped it to two, then three tablespoons (slowly - runs can result if done too fast!).
>
>
>
> IIRC< you said you're using 85g sometimes for BAB? I suspect more CO or tallow or lard (just drippings from your pastured meat) or butter with your BAB will help you be able to have less protein, and will create better health by avoiding overdoing the protein.
>
>
>
>
>
> I try not to concentrate on aesthetic goals, telling myself I'm not doing this to lose weight but to feel better. If the weight comes off, I'll be happy, and if it doesn't, well, I'll have to accept myself like that. I have focussed so much on my weight on the past, I don't want to define myself as a number on a scale anymore.
>
>
>
> This is a great place to be, and better than what I suggested before. I never could accept the possibility of staying at the weight I was, to my fault. If you can embrace what you just said above, you may have no need to give a mini-focus on weight.
>
>
>
> I've got a question for you...I'm curently reading your whole journey on MDA (now on page 32), it really encourages me. Even with your struggles, you're always improving and you don't give up!! I read that you don't use shampoo at all...What are you cleaning your hair with? Only water??

LOL - I discovered that people, like animals, have self-cleaning hair, for the most part. Animals have sebum which coats their fur and if you never wash them, they will self-clean. My cat is a great example, as were the dogs I had growing up. . They were never, ever bathed, yet if they got dirty, they'd be clean in hours, b/c the sebum is an oil that repels dirt. Cloth diapering showed me this, too - I used wool covers, and they repelled dirt & even urine, and even got the smell out if you aired them out for a few hours. Every few months you'd have to apply new lanolin (sheep sebum), since, of course, the diaper doesn't make its own.

So I learned that people didn't wash their hair with soap for most of history, either, and found a group of people also trying a water-only method of hair care. (Some people use conditioner only, or apple cider vinegar & baking soda only.) I rinse my hair with water on the weekend and never in between. I haven't used shampoo more than twice in 4.5 years. (Once I was going to some place fancy and wanted straight hair - my hair has had a natural wave in it ever since I stopped using shampoo, and no way was I going to put a hot iron on my hair. I ccan't remember the other time.) Even when I was in my sister's wedding and had tons and tons of hairspray, or went to a smoky bar, all the crap was out by the end of my water-only shower the next morning. Sebum sheds it.

I can't wear makeup for long, either - sebum sheds it after about 6hrs, lol. (No soap on my face.) I don't use soap anywhere but my pits, and since they've been stinking less in the last few weeks, I may stop that soon, too.

There's a transition period that I can tell you more about if you're interested.

### [email redacted] — 2012-04-05T15:21:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-bumpy-road-to-optimal.400/post-11690>

11 days binge-free!!

And I weighted my self this morning, I was pretty confident I had lose some...Nop...Gained two...Whatever

BAB: raw game meat (tartare), eggs, CO (60g protein)

Lunch: a few macadamia nuts

Dinner: more nuts (too much), salmon and a small piece of chocolate...finally, I had the whole bar

Went to bed at 10:30 last night, it took maybe half an hour to fall asleep. Got up at 3 as usual, and slept til 7:30...And I feel so good this morning! It's been a while since I had a real good sleep night, feeling rested and refreshed in the morning. I want that feeling every morning!! What did I do differently? I was a little hungry last night (as usual), and had some coco manna before bed...I knew when I went to bed that I was going to have a good night...Funny...Since I have adrenal fatigue, is not eating while hungry adds more stress and have a nefative impact on my sleep...Will try again tonight...

Going to my Dr this morning, I hope he's going to be open and helpful! Will record back.

CT: 35 minutes bath after BAB. Don't feel like another deep tonight, so I'll just do ice packs as long as possible.

Appointment with Dr was great. He's into paleo and thinks binges are a symptom of allergies or some intolerance. he was pretty amazed by all the information I already knew. I told him abour Dr Kruse and the cold baths I'm having. I have to go for blood tests and I got out with a prescription of progesterone and some supplements: adaptogens, Advanced B-Complex, Ortho-Adapt (adrenal support), Mag clycinate and melatonin. I'll to go back next week to get some estro-detox (he was out), and I also have to take Vit C and GABA, which I already have. He didn't think pregnenolone is a must, so I'll wait for the blood tests to come back and to ask again.

Was late for an appointment in the afternoon, so had only a few nuts for lunch. It was fiea, wasn't hungry anyway, but when I got home at 4 I was ravenous. Had nuts, chocolate, salmon. I wasn't hungry anymore, but came back for more!! Had some hazelnuts, finished the chocolate bar and threw away the rest of macadamia nuts. I Know!! Shame on me!! But i better like it in the garbage than in my body. I said last week that I wasn't going to buy those anymore...And I did...Didn't binge on them as usual, I had a few here and there all week, but tonight has proven that I can't control myself with those...Lucky for me, I didn't have much!!

Should I treat them the same way I do with wheat and dairies? Am I allergic or intolerant to nuts...Will ask Dr next time I see him (only in 3 months).

Note to myself: NO NUTS and NO CHOCOLATE and also: No skipping meals for the moment!!

Went for a 45 minutes walk at night...This was part of my goals for the month (moving a little every day), and I promised my Dr to go out everyday...Wasn't feeling like it but did it at 9:00.

Went to bed at 10:30, it took a while to fall asleep. Woke up at 3, at took really long to go back to sleep. Finally woke up at 7, a little tired, but not like usual. It's easier to get up, I have more energy and mood is better (is it because period began yesterday?)

### [email redacted] — 2012-04-11T14:30:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-bumpy-road-to-optimal.400/post-13692>

Thank's Darllen!!

I gave up coffee because of sleep problems and adrenal fatigue...I first switched to deca, so it was easy to give up completely. I don't really miss it now, but I might try the Bullet Proof Coffee (with deca), everybody seems to love it!

I hear you, it can be difficult to travel and to eat at relatives...I used to tell people I have some kind of allergy to gluten and dairy and I feel really bad when I eat them. People are less likely to insist. Or I would also tell them that this "diet" has been prescribed by my doctor to fix such or such thing... Once YOU are convinced that is the way you should eat and it does good to YOU, you won't let anyone poison you  My friends and familly know why I'm doing it, they don't try anymore to convince to have "only one bite", and often I bring my own food. No one is offended, and I get to eat what I want...

Supplements: Vit B, C, D, E. Estro-detox (2), ortho-adapt (2), maca powder

Lunch: pork and cauli-flower at 1:00

Supplements: Vit B and C

Dinner: eggs and guacamole

Supplements: Vit B, C, E, K, selenium

Supplements before bed: melatonin (4), GABA, Mag (5) and Relora-Plex

Ct: did an hour ice packs before work. Was too rushing to have a bath.

Energy: pretty good, but I had a little low around 2:30...Would have gone for a nap but went walking instead to enjoy the nice sun we've got here.

Daily walk: 45 minutes this afternoon

Just had dinner and I so don't feel like having a cold bath...It much easier for me to do it in the morning or after lunch. Still wondering if I'll do it or not. I'm so tired, all energy is gone! I feel I could be a little sentitive to a binge tonight, I have to change my mind!! Gonna have some GABA right now directly on the tongue, its supposed to help to calm down.

Ok, gonna get in that tub, aiming for a 20 minutes, and once in there, I might stay longer. Go, Kat, Go!!

\*Edit: just realized (from FitDay) that I only had 46g protein this morning...It that why I was having a hard time at night and was feeling like binging??...

### MamaGrok — 2012-04-13T21:29:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-bumpy-road-to-optimal.400/post-14820>

Ugh, no kidding having to reset is a motivator! I HATED going from 22 to 0 last Feb or 33 to 0 last summer, and a couple more time sof getting up there, and then SPLAT!

Heard a nice nugget from Dr. Kruse on one of the podcasts: If you fall, make sure you fall forwards.

This puts into words what I have felt so strongly is a key point for those of us battling the sugar/binge demon. For me, that means making sure I \*never\* go back to the way I was before, even if I screw up. So early in my journey, if I was bingeing on cookies every other day before, well now I'll make sure I'm not doing it more than once every four days. Or I'll make sure I'm bingeing on organic cookies, at least! If I was able to do without sweets at all for a month and I know I \*have\* to fall, I'm going to fall \*after\* eating a huge meal of fat & protein and it's going to be 100% fruit organic jam, or PB & honey, or homemade yogurt cream ... followed by as much water as I can stuff to fill me up. And then I'm going to forget about it all and eat my BAB the next morning. And not skip any meals.

Each binge was "better" than the last ... well, most ... I still ate half a cookie cake some time last summer, hiding in a dark grocery store parking lot ... but the progression was always FORWARD. I celebrated every step forward, and every "better than last time" binge, and just kept dusting off and going forward again, and continuing to do the things that brought me to where I was, which was always better than the past ... fat, protein, BAB ... fat, protein, BAB ... rinse & repeat.

### [email redacted] — 2012-04-14T00:41:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-bumpy-road-to-optimal.400/post-14896>

> **MamaGrok;14889 wrote:** Heard a nice nugget from Dr. Kruse on one of the podcasts: If you fall, make sure you fall forwards.

Good advice...

If I look at myself compare to last year at the same time, I see a complete new person. I was such a mess, but gradually, the binges would be less frequent...Instead of every Saturday and Sunday, it would only be Saturday. And then instead of every Saturday, it was one out of 2... I slowly gave up wheat and would binge on things that would be less harmful for me....Now when I want to binge I want 90percent chocolate and fruits!! I've come a long way!! I try to look at all the positive changes I made and don't pay too much attention on the negative "details". No matter what we do, just look forward and continue from where we left.

When I started this journal, I was feeling like my old self... I was caught in the binge circle for 2 weeks and though I came back in time and ruined all the efforts I made in the last years. Now I know I didn't, but I also know the battle is not won. Thank's to the Leptin Rx and the BAB, the cravings are at bay and the one that still manage to taunt me are manageable.

"Fat, protein, BAB...Fat, protein, BAB...rinse & repeat" Love it

### [email redacted] — 2012-04-14T21:55:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-bumpy-road-to-optimal.400/post-15119>

20 ays binge-free!!

Went to bed at 11:30 last night, slept til 5, and tossed and turned til 8. Once I wake up in the middle of the night, the sleep I can after is definitly not good quality...Hope it will get better soon...

BAB: game meat, eggs, kale, coffee with CO and dark chocolate (66g protein)

Lunch: smoked salmon. BP coffee (was supposed to go w/o butter today but couldn't help it). Had lunch pretty early, was hungry at 11, and at 11:30 I couldn't wait anymore

Dinner: saussages and veggies. I almost forgot: had a few tortillas chips before dinner, maybe 5...

It 3 o'clock right now and energy is so loooowww. I had a nap after lunch (didn't sleep though), I'm feeling nauseus and dizzy. Trying to convinced myself it is some kind of detox...

CT: 60 minutes cold bath after BAB. 1 hour and a half ice packs.

Daily walk: Will kick my butt and will go right now, and it is super nice out today....Went for 30 minutes...

Went to buy some Macarons for a friend (the French ones, made with almond flour and tons of sugar). I usualy have one (only one!) for myself, but not this time. It was so hard, those things taste like heaven! But on my way back I got some dark chocolate (99pourcent). You know, a kind of reward because I've been doing so good. I didn't have any. I realized, once home, that it could easily lead to a binge. This is not a good day to put anything dangerous in my mouth!! I spent the night at a friend's house. we had dinner a little late, but at least it changed my mind.

### [email redacted] — 2012-04-15T16:17:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-bumpy-road-to-optimal.400/post-15294>

21 days binge-free!! I can't believe it!! No binge, and I did stick to the Rx for 3 entire weeks!!  Thank's to that journal that helps me not to slack off!! And thank's to all of you encouraging me and showing me it going to be better one day! I was feeling so out of myself yesterday, this is better be worth it!! lol

I slept very bad last night. Went to bed at 11, and woke up at 3. Sleep was really bad and light after that.

BAB: game meat, eggs, kale (68g protein). And I had that chocolate bar I bought yesterday...It didn't last long!! Bad girl!! It's gone, so I won't have to think about it anymore!

Lunch: shrimps with curry, turmeric and coconut milk and avocado

Dinner: eggs and veggies

Ct: 75 minutes at 10C and 2 hours ice packs in total (on belly, as always).

Energy: like yesterday, very, very low, especially in the afternoon. Wasn't feeling like doing anything, nauseous and hungry. Once I had dinner, I was feeling better...Hope that is not going to last too long, I need my energy back at work tomorrow!! I wish I'm gonna have a good night sleep too, that would be more than welcome!!

Daily walk: went for 45 minutes walk tonight.

Went I first start the Rx at the beginning of Febuary, I was sleeping like a baby after 2 weeks only. And I also cut back on meet. I was only having fish and seafood since I can't get any grass fed meat here and I thought it might add to the inflammation and contributing to a bad sleep. Right now I'm only having red meat in the morning, and fish and seafood for lunch and dinner (unless I'm not home). Any thoughts, anyone? Should I give up red meat again?

### MamaGrok — 2012-04-15T16:17:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-bumpy-road-to-optimal.400/post-15295>

Lunch: salmon, avocado

Dinner: shrimps, veggies, CO and raw chocolate

Sleep: so, so. falling asleep is easy now. Went to bed at 10, woke up at 11:30. At 12:30, I got up to take more supplements (Relora), and went back to sleep. Woke up tired this morning. I so need a good night sleep!!

### [email redacted] — 2012-04-17T15:05:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-bumpy-road-to-optimal.400/post-15860>

23 days binge-free!!

BAB: liver, eggs, CO and raw chocolate

Lunch: salmon and avocado, Co and raw chocolate

Dinner: scallops and asparagus, CO. Feeling like I ate too much.

Sleep: it's getting worse every night  Was in bed around 10:15, fall asleep quicly, and turned and tossed from 2 o'clock. What's wrong with me??

CT: an hour 12-13C bath after BAB. Another hour after dinner (10-12C). As the day goes on, it seems that I'm colder and I have to talk myself into CT, much more than in the morning.

Energy: low in the morning, and very low after lunch. It's 6 pm as I'm writing and energy is up a little

Daily walk: 1 hour in the afternoon, enjoying the sun

As I sat in the tub after dinner, it rang at the door. Had to get out and run down the stairs, because I knew what it was: my orange glasses were delivered!!  I'm so tired of not sleeping (!!), and couldn't find any in store, so yesterday I ordered them on Amazon, paid 20$ for quick international delivery, and here they are!! Can'y wait to see if it's going to help at all.

### MamaGrok — 2012-04-17T17:01:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-bumpy-road-to-optimal.400/post-15884>

I didn't have big problems with DHEA, melatonin, or other things that usually plague those with sleep problems. My sleep was perfect before (albeit TOO LENGTHY, but solid), and is now again (still too lengthy! Tired of waking sleepy after 9+hrs!).

### [email redacted] — 2012-04-18T19:24:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-bumpy-road-to-optimal.400/post-16240>

24 days binge-free! Can't believe I'm not hungry at night anymore and that the cravings are so rare now.

BAB: game meat, eggs, CO. Total of 74g protein

Lunch: salmon and avocado, CO

Dinner: salmon and veggies, CO

Sleep: was better last night. Wore the oranges glasses all night, I think it helped. Supplements don't seem to be as effective as before though, I don't fall asleep that quicly. Woke up a few times during the night and sleep was very light, but I feel better this mornig.

CT: 1 hour ice packs this morning. Will do some more tonight. No cold bath today as I'll return late from work. \*total 2,5 hours ice packs\*

Energy: so far so good. Maybe 7/110.

Daily walk: will do it tonight. (45 minutes)

### [email redacted] — 2012-04-20T03:39:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-bumpy-road-to-optimal.400/post-16850>

25 binge-free days

BAB: game meat, eggs, CO (64g protein)

Lunch: small piece of salmon

Dinner: shrimps and veggies, CO

Sleep: slept from 10 to 1:30, and only went back to sleep at 5, til 7. The morning has been difficult!!

CT: 50 minutes bath at 11C. 1 hour ice packs tonight. Started icing upper back yesterday.

Energy: still good considering the night

Daily walk: 1 hour this afternoon under the sun

Went to a market today and found a butcher with organic and grassfed beef. Was so glad! People are not into that at all in here, and this is the first place I find with any. I always ask when I go to a new butcher, and they give me strange looks...I guess they never heard of that. There's a few farms an 100 miles from here where I can order online, but you can't choose the cuts, they only sell pre-esthablished packages. It was a bit expensive, I'll do more research to see if I can find something more affordable.

### [email redacted] — 2012-04-20T17:26:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-bumpy-road-to-optimal.400/post-17068>

26 days binge-free. It's getting so much easier, binging seems like an old memories now. I can see the 30 days mark coming and so exited about it!

BAB: grassfed beef (it was sooo good!!), kale, CO (past 9)

Lunch: saussages, CO. Not enough, I was hungry early

Dinner: too lazy to cook...eggs and CO

Sleep: better. Went to bed at 10, slept til 2, and then again from 4 to 9. Felt pretty good when I woke up. Took 2 x 500mg Gaba to help me go back to sleep. Only thought about it after 2 hours, but I think it helped. I've read that waking up at 2 is a sign of elevated cortisol (or adrenaline?). Is it also associated to insulin problem?

CT: in the bath right now, aiming for an hour. (did 70 minutes) Will try to take T down a little (now it's 11C/52F ) and will try to have another one after lunch (did not happen, wasn't feeling it. No spot icing as well).

Energy: ok...maybe 7/10

Daily walk: rainy day here...not sure. Went for 45 minutes at night.

Got part of my labs back yesterday. Cholesterol seems to be fine, B12 is in the roof. CRP is < 4, that's all that I could get...Will ask my Dr if I can test for HS CRP. Waiting for sexual hormones results next week and VitD. Upped my vit D yesterday and could almost feel it instantly. Went from 10 000 IU to 35 000. Started progesterone 2 days ago, can't really tell the difference right now. My Dr prescribed it to me especially for PMS and told me to use only on the second half of my cycle. I see here that a lot are using it all month... PMS are usually pretty strong the week before my period, so I'll see next week if it helps (I'm taking 0,1mL BID).

### [email redacted] — 2012-04-21T22:13:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-bumpy-road-to-optimal.400/post-17515>

27 bingre-free day and week 4 is almost completed!!

BAB: beef and eggs, CO

Lunch: sardines, avocado, CO

Dinner: snails and scallops

Sleep: had a difficult time to go to sleep, and only slept til 4

CT: did 35 minutes after BAB at 10C. Couldn't do it longer, had to run out. Did another 35 minutes after lunch while listening to the webinar...Huge motivator for CT! Was shivering and couln't stay longer.

Energy: bof...

Daily walk: went out this morning for maybe 20 minutes? Raining hard... don't think I'll go out beside walking to grocery shopping 2 blocks away.

Since I re-started CT 3 weeks ago, I usualy sit straight up in the tub, and trying to lay back from time to time....It's time now to get more efficient and to get the BAT in the water. I fit perfectly in the tub even if it's kind of small. When it's really full, I can lay back with my feet out and have water up to my neck. I find it easier if I ice spot my belly prior to get in the tub.

It's been pretty cold here yesterday and today. I left the windows opened for a few day. It's now 6C/43F out, and kind of cold inside too. I'm shivering big time right now!!

Just took some pictures of me so I can compare down the road. Didn't really like what I saw: another motivator to get in the cold tub!!

Went to the market just before dinner to get some stuff, and I was HUNGRY. I grabbed some almonds thinking I could have a few while getting dinner ready....Yeah right...I ate them all!! STUPID ME!!! I know this stuff is not good for me, and I buy it anyway...No more of that. To make sure that I wouldn't be hungry in the evening and that wouldn't lead to a binge, I still had my dinner with plenty of protein. i noticed that I'm way more vulnerable to bad stuff when I'm really tired....

### PaulaRichards — 2012-04-21T22:22:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-bumpy-road-to-optimal.400/post-17519>

Doing good girl!! I need to catch up with you on the CT. I purchased the webinar today only to find out I couldn't be home for it  Then thought I'd view it from an ipod & there was NO connection available where I was. Oh well! So, is it a "shock" everytime you get in that cold tub?? The two times I tried it I had to put an inch or so of warm water, sit, then add cold. But we had bathfitter install a new tub/shower a couple of months ago (before I even new about CT) and now I don't have a spearate cold tap & can't get just cold water so I have to get that adjusted. ### [email redacted] — 2012-04-21T22:57:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-bumpy-road-to-optimal.400/post-17533>

lol No, that's not a good excuse!!  I don't have separate cold tap neither, I just turn it a little to get only cold water. It takes very long to fill up the tub, but I better like to sit in it once it's full. I started with warmer water (maybe 18C/65F), and once I felt confortable in that temperature for 30 minutes, I gradually decrease the temperature. It didn't take that long though. Just do it. It's not really pleasant at first, but if you do it at your rythm, it's gonna get easier that you thought. And it seems to be THE WAY to speed healing, to balance hormones and to lose weight.

This morning the T was 10C/50F and it felt a little cold, but berable. This afternoon the T was 14C/57F and it actually felt pretty warm!! Especially compared to the ice packs I had on my belly while having my lunch! That helps a lot too...

I'm sorry you missed the webinar! It was pretty interesting. Next one will be about Factor X...You don't want to miss it!!

### PaulaRichards — 2012-04-22T02:36:52+0300

### [email redacted] — 2012-04-22T16:25:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-bumpy-road-to-optimal.400/post-17716>

Beginning of week 5!!  And 28 days binge free!

BAB: beef and eggs, CO, raw chocolate

Lunch: avocado, sardines, BP coffee (decaf)

Dinner: eggs, CO

Sleep: I slept through the entire night!!! Went to bed at 11 and first woke up at 6. 7 hours of sleep! Last time it happened was 2 months ago. I feel so good this morning. Was still tired so I stayed in bed til 8:30. I'm taking Relora-Plex every night (blend of plant extracts to lower cortisol) and last night I doubled the dose. Don't know if I slept well because I didn't sleep much the night before or because of that, but I might do it again tonight...

CT: Got out of bath 20 minutes ago and shivering like crazy, I have a hard time to type! Stayed in for 65 minnues at 10C/50F. Another cold bath after diner for 45 minutes. Was harder to warm up. Went to bed with window open (3C/37F)

Energy: Seems pretty good so far...Have been good all day

Daily walk: nop...It is starting to look more like a weekly walk than a daily walk, will have to move a little more...

I think I souldn't have lunch. It's been like that for a few days. I'm not hungry a lot, but feel like I should have something. Its usually really small, but once I'm done I feel too full for a while. I'm a little worry about skipping it though...Wil it stress my adrenal? Will I get to dinner ravenous and wanting to eat everything in sight? Will experiment in the next days...

At 6 pm, I was still full from lunch! What the heck? I had a little dinner anyway to make sure I could go through the night, but I def ate too much today. Feel really full and stonac is a little upset...

### [email redacted] — 2012-04-24T01:22:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-bumpy-road-to-optimal.400/post-18194>

29 days binge-free

BAB: game meat and eggs, CO

Lunch: avocado and sardines

Dinner: shrimps in coco milk

Sleep: slept good til 5, and not so much after. But good quality sleep

CT: an hour in tub after dinner. Cold tolerance def lower at night, especially with less sleep...

Energy: Good all day

Daily walk: nop

4th left toe didn't like yesterday's CT...Was blue/purple and swollen yesterday, and was stil like that this morning when I woke up. Had to work in running shoes cause I couldn't tolerate normal shoes, it was way too sensitive. It happened many times in the past, but mostly in winter time. It's pretty cold in my place, I guess it didn't help neither. Might also be a reaction from the nuts I had saturday... Looks a lot like Raynaud (especially a few years ago, not the white phase is pretty rare, it looks more like veinous stasis all the time), and that toe is much more affected than the other one. It's way better since going Paleo. Dr Kruse sais Raynaud is a ratio O6:O3 to elevated...Might be that...

Wanted to skip lunch today. Wasn't much hungry, but was feeling low energy. I ate at 1:30...Would probably could have wait til 5 or 6 for dinner, but wasn't sure, and I'm afraid I might stress my adrenals and up cortisol.

### [email redacted] — 2012-04-24T16:33:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-bumpy-road-to-optimal.400/post-18344>

30 days binge-free!! Record broken!! I'm so, so, exited about this!! It's becoming easier everyday!!

BAB: game meat, eggs, CO and raw chocolate (total 72g protein)

Lunch: no

Dinner: scallops and veggies, CO

Sleep: None. Nada. Niet. Rien!!!  I was tired (and really cold!) but couldn't fall asleep. I turned all night, and I'm miserable this morning. What is wrong? Better be detox. I just want to sleep, pleeease.

CT: 50 minutes after BAB in a 8C/46F. Couldn't stay any longer, it felt so cold! Doing the same after dinner (50 min again, same T).

Energy: loooow

Daily walk: no excuse today, I'm off and it's nice out. Might help me to sleep too...Went for 30 minutes this afternoon and another 30 minutes in the evening.

4th left toe is better this morning. It's now red and still swollen. Hopefully it will get better soon.

Wasn't hungry for lunch, didn't have any, but still not sure if it's a good idea...Had dinner early. Was hungry but not ravenous. I thought that going longer between meals could help the liver function properly again, but not sure...Just want to make sure not to stress my adrenals further...

### [email redacted] — 2012-04-25T17:12:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-bumpy-road-to-optimal.400/post-18699>

31 days binge-free

BAB: beef and eggs, CO and raw chocolate

Lunch: beef, avocado

Dinner: salmon, CO, blueberries and strwberries. First time having fruits in a very long time.

Sleep: Tried EFT, that really helped me to relax, but it took me a while to fall asleep. Slept til 2:20...and went back to sleep around 5. I was getting anxious! I know it doesn't help me, but I so need to sleep. I slept til 9:30 . Not the best sleep quality, but not to bad. Just finished BAB and feeling like going back to bed!

CT: I take a break today...Can't tolerate any cold right now

Energy: Do I need to elaborate?

Daily walk:

I've seen better days. I'm pissed off today. I'm 32 y/o, I worked really hard to go back to school and to get my degree, I have a very good carreer ahead of me, but...I can't get out in the morning, I have NO energy at all, every single task looks unconquerable, even cleaning my place is depressing and I don't know how I can handle it. I don't even feel like giong outside. This isn't me! It seems that my life is passing me by, and I don't see the end of it. Fortunaltely, I don't always feel this way. A good night sleep finds me with great energy and a good mood. But it's so rare. And as long as I won't sleep, I can't solve my problems. High cortisol don't allow me to sleep, and sleep deprivation increases my cortisol at night. And we are not talking about other hormones who are probably in the toilet. Still haven't got my labs back. I'm supossed to see my dr only at the end of june but I'll change it to see him sooner.

Had increased energy around 5 for 2 hours or so...It's like my day was beginning! Going out for a walk. No more TV or computer for the night.

### [email redacted] — 2012-04-27T01:04:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-bumpy-road-to-optimal.400/post-19168>

32 days binge-free!!  Was seriously thinking about that number this afternoon when I was cravings macaroons...

BAB: beef, eggs, CO

Lunch: salmon

dinner: sashimis

Sleep: took a pill to make sure I'd sleep, and even with that it took me a while to fall asleep. I have to do something about the stress, this is spoilling my life, literaly. I was crying in bed last wondering when I would have my life back and be normal again....Impressive what a good night sleep can do. Waking up with the alarn was difficult, but once I got going, I was fine.

CT: no

Energy: was ok this morning. Finished work early and went shopping. Trying a few pairs of jeans after walking to the store found me exhausted. Crazy.

Walk: around an hour to go and come back from the store.

Called my Dr to see if i could see him sooner. Nop. I'll be seeing him at the end of june  He got my labs back, but the lab didn't do E, T, DHEA and Vit D. Was pissed off. I contacted a friend of mine (a Dr) to see if he can take me as a patient or if I can a reference from him. Fingers crossed!! There is basically NO func doctor here. I just need to find someone who can prescribe me blood tests again and willing to help me with hormones.

Will also order some 7-keto DHEA. This isn't avalaible OTC in Canada, will have to have it ship to a friend in the US.

No orange glasses tonight, no CT. I'm going to meet a friend for an happy hour (sparkling water for me) for a change. I need a life

### MamaGrok — 2012-04-29T18:38:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-bumpy-road-to-optimal.400/post-19834>

I felt that way Friday and needed sleep all day, but it didn't make sense ... I had had enough sleep. I started DHEA two weeks ago, then after a few days found myself not able to get to sleep at night, so stopped it. Don't know how it may all be tied together.

### [email redacted] — 2012-04-30T02:52:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-bumpy-road-to-optimal.400/post-19970>

MG, I know what you mean because I was feeling like that when I first started CT. I knew it was some detoxing, so I could live with it and actually told myself it was a good sign. But those last few days, it was clearly linked to the lack of sleep and high stress. It's been years with so-so sleep, and when I'm just not sleeping at all, it's like a switch turns on and I go nuts!

I took some PC today along with L-Theanine, and I can really feel the difference. I'm more calm and the "noise" in my brain, as Souldanzer would say, has decreaded a lot. I know it's not the best, but I'll use the sleeping pills for a few nights, to make sure I sleep well and rest a little. And in 2 weeks I'll get out of the city to visit my parents and relax, that should help a lot too.

### [email redacted] — 2012-04-30T03:02:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-bumpy-road-to-optimal.400/post-19973>

35 days binge-free

I'm restarting this...Felt off the bandwagon those last few days...Had a glass of wine, some cheese and some yogourt, and ate too late. Also had some fruits and sweet potatoes, which made me feel real sick.

BUT the good news: I didn't binge!! Despite the lack of sleep and not following the reset as I should have, I didn't binge! I did not even think about it! I had some sugar cravings because I didn't eat enough for breakfast yesterday and because of the fruits I guess, but nothing like before. Alchool is usually a BIG trigger for me, and that's why I didn,t want to have more than 1 glass of wine. When I go out, I used to head to the grocery store on my way home (thank's to those 24/7) and binge before going to sleep. This time, I got a flat tire on the highway in the cold rain, was dawn stressed out, and it didn't occur to be that I could stuff myself with sugar. I was so, so glad when I got home and realized that!!  Small victory!!

So today I'm back on track, and back on CTing also after a 5 days break. Water is a little warmer than it was last week, but I'll get it down again this week. I'm in the tub right now and it's really relaxing

### [email redacted] — 2012-05-01T15:04:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-bumpy-road-to-optimal.400/post-20339>

37 days binge-free!!

I've noticed that appetite has diminished a lot lately. Hungry at meals time, but can't have much. Maybe because I slept? However, last night it was really bad sleep. I'll try to go without the pills tonight. I'm not home a lot, always in and out. My sister had her first baby last week, and parents are exhausted, so I'm going over a lot to help with food and the house. This little boy is the most beautiful thing I've ever seen. I'm in love

So for today:

BAB: liver

Lunch: salmon, avocado

Dinner:eggs, CO

CT: 30 minutes after BAB.

Daily walk: an hour this morning

I eat a lot less those days. Not cooking much neither, always in and out and getting something quick for my meals. And tonight I realized that I didn't have any veggies for a couple of days. I always buy too much and waste like half of it, and didn't buy any this week. I should at least get some kale. I also often forget to add some fat with my meals, and I get hungry soon after I finish. I sometime can wait to next meal, and sometime I'll just dig into CO jar. Only CO I have left is the oone with full coconut flavor, and as much as I loved it, I can't stand it anymore. I'll finish it off course, but can't wait to get the gallon I bought from the states!! I'll finish it using it for cooking and adding butter when I'm hungry.

I'm more relax tonight, it feels good. I'll go to bed with no pills, only supplements to see how it goes....

> I'm really exhausted today, I'm starting again to cry for a yes or a no. I went to my Dr office, and they have had a cancellation for tomorrow, so I won't have to wait til the end of June!! I was so glad I showed up there. They have a list of patients waiting like me, but I guess crying and asking to see someone put me on the top of it
>
>
>
> I'm not working much those days. Makes it hard finacially, but I the same time I don't know how I could keep up working full time. This has to get better soon. I'm self employed and can't rely on anybody....And speaking of that, I have to do some major cleaning in my personal life, I'm sure it's adding on my stress. Actually, I don't think. It IS adding big stress on me. Enough of this, I have to get my life back.
>
>
>
> Sorry about that, I'm rambling
>
>
>
> Have a good day everyone

Great on being able to see the doc and congrats on your new nephew. Very happy for you! You will get this worked out all in time. Patience is very hard as I very well know, but I think I am finally on the other side of the mountain and have only been doing this for 9 full months. Geez, I coulda had a baby in that time! Not really, just sayin. There is hope. You and I have a lot of the same crap so I know you will get better- remember you have age on your side. Your hurdle is a good doc and that was mine too even though I am in the states. You do have some new supps on the way yes? I hope you have success tomorrow. The best of luck to you! I will be reading to see how you fared.

V, thank's to you too.

I know, time and patience are the keys...I've had bad sleep for years and could do ok with it, but now it's just too much. I can't barely take care of myself, I don't know where I could find the energy to take care of a baby.

I'll report back after my appointment with doc tomorrow. Can't wait to see what is gonna tell me...have a nice evening everyone.

### [email redacted] — 2012-05-02T13:18:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-bumpy-road-to-optimal.400/post-20628>

38 days binge-free

Didn't sleep last night. At all. I was expecting that a little since I didn't take the pills, but was still hoping to get a few hours in since I was pretty calm last night and feeling sleepy. Didn't happen, but I stayed in bed and relaxed. Got up around midnight, was hungry and ate  Was about to panic a little around 4 when I saw I wouldn't sleep at all, but remained calm and got up at 5:30. Took the extra time to get in the tub before going to my sis who needs help with baby to go to Dr since her bf can't be there.

I'm having a hard time for a few days now post meals, and yesterday I realized it's mostly with eggs. Had some at dinner last night, and had stomac pain after. Not feeling as bad this morning, but have a little heart burn and purping (how nice to write about that!!). Or could it be the butter?? Have had quite a bit since monday.

BAB: game meat, eggs and butter

Lunch: brocoli soup, some butter

Dinner:

CT: 40 minutes after BAB at 58F

Daily walk: maybe 30 minutes or so

Energy: even with no sleep at all, I feel pretty great and have good energy. Went to my sis, went to my Dr, ran errands...Pretty stressed out though, I constantly have to remind myself to breath slow and deep. I'm getting pretty good at this, trying to change my thinking and control my breathing as soon as I feel I'm losing control of them and that my brain is full speed. When very stressed, my intercostal muscles are pretty thight and it makes it difficult for me to breath deeply.. Did some yoga/streching while listening relaxing music prior to go to bed.

### [email redacted] — 2012-05-02T23:56:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-bumpy-road-to-optimal.400/post-20832>

Just got back for the Dr office...In French, we say it was whatever...N'importe quoi!! I got out with few prescriptions, but he didn't seem to see what was the interest for testing any hormones...Oh well...

I already take progesterone for the second half of my cycle (bio-avalaible), and now I'll be adding oral progesterone for the entire month. I'll also be using DHEA (cream) and T3 (also cream I think, I need to get them tomorrow). And he gave me Elavil if needed to sleep...

I'll be seeing my friend doctor this week-end, he's going to prescribe me the blood tests I want, but I still don't know if the lab will do it. If not, then I'll order the saliva hormones profile through Canary Club. I prefer not paying for that, but I don't want to mess around with my hormones, I thing they already have been enough maltreated...

Oh yeah, and he also told me I should eat more carbs, like quinoa...Thank's, but no thank's. The first time I say him, he was talking about adrenal issue, and today he was thinking I'm fine about that....Whatever

### PaulaRichards — 2012-05-03T01:03:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-bumpy-road-to-optimal.400/post-20851>

I hear you on the "whatever"! I have a feeling I'm going to be leaving my appt tomorrow with that same feeling, even though I'm seeing a naturopath. I've heard she's into "healthy" grains! We'll see!!

A few years ago when I was going through a really stressful time my doc gave me Elavil for sleep. He knows I hate taking any medication but assured me it wasn't addictive & it was a really low does. I have to admit the first night I took it I fell in love with the stuff. I slept so well. I took it for months and gradually weaned myself off with no problems (at least none that I know of!). I'd be interested to know what others think about it. Back in November, because of another period of stress and being at my wits end with the type of sleep you described I asked for a script for it again. It's such a comforting feeling knowing that you're going to sleep! Good luck & if you decide to take it; have a good sleep!

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-bumpy-road-to-optimal.400/post-20862>

If I can get some of them with my friend that's fine, but he can't prescribe cortisol salavi tests anyway, and that way I'll make sure I have all the information I need asap.

I hope it's gonna be better with your doc tomorrow! Good luck!!

### [email redacted] — 2012-05-03T14:53:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-bumpy-road-to-optimal.400/post-20933>

39 days binge-free

Took the sleeping pills last night (2). Was pretty relaxed when I got to bed- orange glasses, relora, yoga/stretching plus relaxing music, and the pills. It took around 30 minutes to fall asleep, I woke up a couple of time and it was difficult to get up in the morning, but at least I slept. Will try to go no pills tonight.

Forgot to mention...My doc thinks I was so desperate last week when I wasn't sleeping because of pms. Because Tuesday night I didn't sleep at all and was fine yesterday (mood and energy), he might be right... I have irregular cycles, so it's hard for me to see where i am in the month. So maybe it was psm aggravated by CT? Didn't think of that by myself, I really thought it was just because of bad sleep...MG, maybe you were right  Will see at the end of the month how it goes...

BAB:game meat, eggs, BP coffee (decaf) to get my fats

Lunch: veggies and butter. Wasn't hungry for more

Dinner: steak and veggies

CT: 30 minutes after BAB and 1 hour ice packs

Daily walk: 30 minutes

Energy. A little low as I got up (bc of the pills). Felt kind of foggy for a couple of hours. Energy was good in the afternoon and stil good in the evening!!

Have you ever heard of the yoga pose called "legs up a wall"? If you Google it, you'll find an entry on Yoga Journal Magazine showing it. You can even do it on your bed, as long as your legs are pretty straight (not too big of a headboard, but I've never seen a too big one not in a museum). It helped my insomniac cousin tremendously after we learned about it in a yoga class. Do it for 15-30 min. It doesn't even matter what you do when you do it, though I'm sure getting into a fight or bathing yourself in blue light doesn't help.

I have often put the soles of my feet together and make a triangle or diamond shape with my legs because straight isn't as comfortable.

Thanks for reminding me of this pose, I need to do it so I get to bed earlier and have better sleep. Let me know if you have any success after trying!

sent from my phone while wrangling at least 1 toddler...expect typos, smile when you see them...the toddlers or the typos

42 days binge-free!!

Used the computer late at night yesterday, maybe it didn't help with the sleep...It's sunny and warm out, and i'm sitting here not even wanting to get out...Usually when 1 get up like that, I get better around lunch time, but it didn't seem to happen today

Dinner:

48 days binge-free!

Slept much better last night, but still with the pills. It's taking me half an hour or so to sleep, I got up once and stayed in bed til 9 this morning. I was really surprised, I usualy get up by myself around 7. Will try to sleep tonight without the pills to see how it goes...

BAB: game meat, eggs, coconut milk

Lunch: beef, brocoli and a little rice

Dinner: salmon, brocoli, coconut milk

Energy was very good in the morning, and went downhill at the end of the afternoon. I just finished dinner and I'm so tired I could go to sleep. Usualy I have higher energy at this time of the day and feel like my day is starting. Maybe it's a good sign...

### [email redacted] — 2012-05-17T17:55:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-bumpy-road-to-optimal.400/post-24916>

53 days binge-free

Barely slept the last 3 nights... Did not so bad Tuesday night, but was very tired yesterday to drive to my mom's (9 hours...). Did not slept last night neither. No problem at all falling asleep, woke up around 3 and that was it. I'm supposed to stay here for up to 2 weeks. It's really great to be out of the city. My moms lives in a beautiful place, its really calm and there's a big river at the front door. It's pretty deep thought and the flow is pretty strong. Not sure I would have the courage to go in it, because of that and because the outside weather is only 57 degress...Will think about it

Meanwhile, I did a great CT session this morning. The tub here is huge and so confortable, I want one like this too!!  Only sitting in it, I had water up to the middle of the girls!! The tap water is going out at 44F, so no need to add ice at all. I stayed in almost 45 minutes. Did it just after BAB. Will do the same for a week to see if my sleep improves, and if not, will switch the CT at night to see if there is any difference.

Still ok with the food. I brought with me meat because I can't find any grassfed here. Forgot my magnesium home, will you buy some at a natural grocery today.

Spent the whole day yesterday in car with my mom...And told her about my past ED...I was a little emotional! I wasn,t sure if I should tell her because sometimes she is not pretty open, but she has been awesome, really supportive. It was a big relief for me to talk about that with her. I decided to tell her because she couldn't understand why I have so bad sleep. So I told her the whole story...I'm glad I did

### [email redacted] — 2012-05-18T20:33:23+0300

54 days binge-free!

Slept so much better last night!  That was about time!! Went to bed at 10, woke up at 1:30 to pee, and again at 5, but I could fall back asleep. Woke up around 6 and got up at 7. Still tired today but energy is much better. I didn't take supplements yesterday but HCL and DIM, and after reading Glam having trouble to sleep with CO, I didn't have any after lunch. I also didn't take progesterone cream...Last month 2nd end my cycle was awfull sleep wise, and that's also when I started using the cream...I took the pill anyway, but will try without it tonight to see how it goes and will add them back in a few nights.

Did Ct in the tub this morning. Started at 44F and finished at 55F. Lasted an hour.

I'm doing good with food. Had a chocolate craving yesterday, but didn't have any. It is usualy pretty challenging to stay at my mom's, she loves to cook, especially cakes and deserts. But now that she knows about my past, she doesn't push anymore so I have any.

57 days binge-free

Week 4, already? Wow, where is the time going?? I started leptin protocol beginning of Febuary...Messed up big time for two weeks in March and have been doing pretty good now for almost 2 months

Slept like a log all night! Didn't even get up once to pee! Didn't happen in a while. Didn't wear the glasses last night (having some familly member over...with my mom and step-dad I don't mind, but I'll keep them away again tonight and tomorrow). Doubled the dose yesterday morning with DHEA (cream). 2 mL only has 0.5g, so I used 4 mL to see if it would be any difference. Did also use progesterone before bed (pill) and omit the cream I'm supposed to take. Was feeling so crapy yesterday, I'm glad I slept.

I did split up a little with food...Was craving fruits, so I had a banana in the afternoon... Had some fish for early dinner, and an hour and a half later was still hungry. Made myself 2 eggs and was fine for the evening and the night. Will try to eat a little more at dinner from now on because I'm often a little hungry when I go to bed and when I wake up in the night. Not that much, but that might be stressing my body...?

Didn't do CT this morning. Might go in the river, but my mom is freaking out about it, she sais is too dangerous. I'd love to try it anyway

### [email redacted] — 2012-05-23T23:34:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-bumpy-road-to-optimal.400/post-26831>

Got my saliva test results this morning...And I don't know what to think. I was expecting something wrong with the cortisol since my energy is really low in the morning and because of bad sleep (and also increased waist line), but it appears to be pretty normal. Was also expecting higher estradiol (fat in thigh and hips).

Estradiol: 0.8 (1.3-3.3)

Progesterone: 210 (30-300)

Ratio Pg/E2: 263 (Optimal: 100-500 when E2 1.3-3.3 pg/ml)

Testosterone: 134 (16-55)

DHEAS: 4.0 (2-23)

Cortisol morning: 9.1 (3.7-9.5)

Cortisol noon: 3.6 (1.2-3.0)

Cortisol evening: 0.6 (0.6-1.9)

Cortisol night: 0.7 (0.4-1.0)

And no surprise DHEA is in the toilet...Hello insomia...Is high testosterone a problem??

If cortisol is ok and it's not adrenal fatigue, then what's wrong with me?? Any input is welcome, as always

If that can be useful: I started supplementing with those in the last month:

Pregnenolone: started with 50 mg and went up with 100mg after the testing

7 Keto DHEA: 50 mg per day. Started 1 week before testing.

Taking adrenal support for 6 weeks now

Small dose of DHEA cream (1 mg per day)

Prometrium 100mg at night

sleep has improved in the last week. Not always good quality sleep, but at least I'm sleeping. Always getting up low energy though and felling better at night.

One last thing...Since I'm in Canada, I took longer to send the kit back to the lab. The shipping took 2 days...Could that have any incidence on the results??

> Glomerular filtration 107 (42-89 umol/L)
>
>
>
> Sodium 139 (135-145 mmol/L)
>
> AST 25 (13-39 U/L)
>
> ALT 33 (8-31 U/L)
>
> Phosphatase ALC 46 (36-110 U/L)
>
> GGT 19 (7-33 U/L)
>
> Serum Iron 11.0 (5.6-31.6 umol/L)
>
> Transferritine 2.21 (1.85-3.51 g/L)
>
> Iron/Trans 0.20 (0.06-0.44)
>
>
>
> Trigly 0.24 (0.43-2.69 mmol/L)
>
> Cholesterol 5.27 (3.40-7.30 mmol/L)
>
> HDL 2.81 (0.90-2.38 mmol/L)
>
> C-T/HDL-C 1.9
>
> LDL-C 2.35 mmol/L
>
>
>
> Testosterone T. 1.3 (0-2.9 nmol/L)
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
> If that can be useful: I started supplementing with those in the last month:
>
> Pregnenolone: started with 50 mg and went up with 100mg after the testing
>
> 7 Keto DHEA: 50 mg per day. Started 1 week before testing.
>
> Taking adrenal support for 6 weeks now
>
> Small dose of DHEA cream (1 mg per day)
>
> Prometrium 100mg at night
>
>
>
> Sleep has improved in the last week. Not always good quality sleep, but at least I'm sleeping. Always getting up low energy though and felling better at night.
>
>
>
> One last thing...Since I'm in Canada, I took longer to send the kit back to the lab. The shipping took 2 days...Could that have any incidence on the results?? \*\*Edit: called the lab and they said the samples are stable for 30 days, so that is not an issue...**

**

And got this from JanSz, which I find really helpful:

JanSz;27488 wrote:** I am not a doctor.

I think that you have low testosterone, but do blood test again.

Test also, blood

total testosterone

free testosterone

SHBG

\--------------------------------

You are low on estradiol, may loose bones.

Consider working on more complete

Steroid Hormone Panel.

You have most tests already, you are missing pregnenolone.

Likely your pregnenolone is low.

Your current use of 100mg supplemental pregnenolone should help some,

but

in my experience

all kinds of pregnenolone pills, creams, drops were rather ineffective.

When I argued I was given runaround that it does not show in blood or saliva for x or y reason.

Miraculously when I eat

Nutricology Micronized Lipid Matrix MLM pregnenolone, 150mg pills scored

I am able to find my pregnenolone in the blood tests.

I suggest first blood testing, then eating plenty of above kind of pregnenolone.

First thing pregnenolone does is convert to progesterone (according to body requirements).

Look at the chart,

[http://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/c/c8/Hormones\_estradiol%2C\_progesterone%2C\_LH\_and\_FSH\_during\_menstrual\_cycle.svg](http://http://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/c/c8/Hormones_estradiol%2C_progesterone%2C_LH_and_FSH_during_menstrual_cycle.svg)

first 13 days of menstrual cycle progesterone level look real low.

Please note that men live with that level and it is very important for them to have this level (and not less).

I am a men. 72yo

I started with almost non existing pregnenolone and progesterone.

I am now using two tabs of above pregnenolone (150mg/wakeup, 150mg/4hr latter)

and

my preg and prog are at good levels.

See my supplementation and resulting blood levels here:

[http://www.musclechatroom.com/forum/showthread.php?15433-JanSz-Labs-amp-Tests&p=166492#post166492](http://http://www.musclechatroom.com/forum/showthread.php?15433-JanSz-Labs-amp-Tests&p=166492#post166492)

pregnenolone & progesterone are highly influencing cortisol levels. They down-metabolize (also) to cortisol.

This may help in your currently weak cortisol in afternoons.

In the future you would have to mind your menstruation cycle and do blood draws on 3-4 day and 19-21 day.

When posting results always provide information about when you have drawn your blood in relation to menstrual cycle.

You would have to stop progesterone supplementation

hopefully all together

but at least in first 14 days

\------------

If you would study major Steroid Hormones, assuming their content in the blood as 100%

you would find that

DHEAS participation (when in healthy level) is 94.99%

[http://img824.imageshack.us/img824/1103/image83k.jpg](http://http://img824.imageshack.us/img824/1103/image83k.jpg)

That is a lot.

You are not going to accomplish much if you do not attend to this elephant.

Good way to do it is to first take 100mg/wakeup of 7ketoDHEA.

(I take 100mg/wakeup and 100mg/4hrs latter).

Then you take plain DHEA pills, enough of them to see results in blood.

If able, always do blood testing at LabCorp.

Desirable DHEAs, Women 275-400 μg/dL

Aim at the top levels, as long as you are not getting excessive

testosterone (currently low)

hair

acne

Some people are looking at chart and see that all starts cholesterol----pregnenolone----everything else

ans are hoping to raise DHEA by supplementing pregnenolone

that is not happening. (one explanation is in the above table that says DHEAs=elephant, all else are ants)

I have not figured convenient way to stay in touch with this board.

Drop in here in totally random ways that are hard to reproduce next time.

Can be PM-ed here

and always include links to the stuff you want comments on.

[http://www.musclechatroom.com/forum/showthread.php?20061-Dr.-Patricia-Kane&p=171942#post171942](http://http://www.musclechatroom.com/forum/showthread.php?20061-Dr.-Patricia-Kane&p=171942#post171942)

You may find out there how I supplement my Fatty Acids (and why).

The Omega6/Omega3 stuff

/////////////

At wakeup look (almost) straight at the sun for 15 min, or high intensity light.

...

I also asked in the Ask Jack section what I should do about testing Testosterone (saliva or blood), and he told me he prefer AM fasting blood test.

Mornin'. My experience with this whole thing is some days I feel good and some days I just don't. I keep doing mainly the same things. As for the mag, what kind are you taking and how much? Dr. K recommends that new mag theonate (sp). I still have to order it. Although, sleep does have a lot to do with it. Sounds like your sleep is finally a little better. I also think with CT'ing that it just keeps fixing things which tax our bodies in ways we can't see. Might be why some days are good and some are bad. How much energy is being used to fix something we are unaware. Anyway, that's my "best" guess! We gotta just keep rollin' with it. I also think people like you and me who really need some fixin' will just take a long time to get better and as long as we make some progress, even if small that is a win and we are in the right direction.

75 days binge-free

Did back off on the BAB in the last few days, but I find myself hungry at night, so I'm upping it again. This morning I had:

\- game meat and mackerel (total of 67g protein)

\- BP coffee with CO and ghee

Slept very bad Tuesday night. Had half a glass of wine in the evening, went to bed late, took a very long time to fall asleep, and when the alarm went on at 6:30, I was drained. Went to a spa last night (yeah, I know, hot water is not optimal ). I was staying in cold water very long, people were loooking at me as a weirdo  Also got a massage, went back home at eleven, went straight to bed and slept til 9 this morning  That feels so great!!

Went to a new gyn yesterday to get a IUD. She didn't want me to get it before I go for an echography. She sais since I have abondant menses, hormone-free IUD is gonna make them worst. Don't know if that echography is really helpfull...She wanted to do it at her office for 150$, but I didn't want to, so I'll go at hospital to get it done for free.

Need to get going...Have a good day all.
