### [email redacted] — 2012-06-10T18:30:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-bumpy-road-to-optimal.400/post-30419>

Yes, sleep is getting better and better!!  I can now sleep through the night, every night! I don't even get up anymore to use the bathroom! I generally wake up pretty early to do so (around 6), but I can go back to sleep after. I'm still going to bed too late though.

In the last week, one day out of two I was feeling like crap and was very tired, and the other one was pretty good. Had a good day yesterday, and so far, today will be another great day!! Two in a row!!  Feeling great this morning, good energy and good mood. Still tired, but I can what I have to do, get going and see my friends without wondering how I'll be able to get through the day.

Friday has been a really bad day emotionally speaking. Was feeeling really depressed. I'm not working right now, and it stresses me a bit. Was feeling like cheating at night...Ended up having sushis, which I consider ok. It was good, but not as good as I was expecting. The good news is that even beeing in a stressful place in my life, feeling like crap and very tired half of the time, I still don't feel like binging. Ok, the thought of having a whole box of cookies friday night came in my mind, but didn't last. I don't have to fight against myself anymore. Thank's for this Dr Kruse!!

I'll go the an no-appointement clinic this week to get a prescription for labs again. My friend who Rx it to me last time doesn't want to do it anymore since he doesn't understand what my functional doc is doing and he's sure it doesn't make sense. Well, thank's for your help, but it does lots of sense for me and I'll try to get some help elsewhere. It's pissing me off. I haven't binge for close to 80 days, I'm sleeping way better, and my labs show that my hormones are a mess. What else do you want for you to make sense?? What ever....

I'll foccus on this beautiful sunny day, enjoy it and have fun

80 days binge-free!!

Feeling better everyday  Woke up once the last two nights to use the bathroom. Was very tired yesterday, but not as crapy and depressed as usual on a "bad day". Went to bed at 10:30 last night and slept til 9 this morning!

I've had BAB a little late the last few days, but had it as soon as I got up this morning. Had 40g of protein with tons of fat and blueberries. I'd like to re-introduce egss to see how I'll react...Still miss them!!

Ice spotting at least an hour everyday on belly and upper back, and started to sit on ice packs when working at my computer on watching TV. And cold shower too, but they are not that cold. Can,t wait to winter to come back!

Have a good day all!!

### [email redacted] — 2012-06-14T22:26:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-bumpy-road-to-optimal.400/post-31224>

Last too night have been so, so...Waking up at 3 and having mediocre sleep until I get up. It is the sushis I had last Friday, plus the few french fries on Saturday, plus the Mijito on Sunday?  I KNOW I shouldn't have those, but it's only when my body react to them by refusing to sleep that I truly understand the impact of bad choices... And that's only what I can see/feel!! My poor cells must be in such a mess!!

So yesterday I went back to keto diet: no fruits anymore, no carbs and more CO. And made sure BAB is big enough. Don't feel like eating much, I have to force it down, but its better than reaching for carbs later in the day. Ate very little for lunch and same for dinner. I was actually really surprise, because I lelt more than half of what was in my plate last night. It's really hard for me not finishing my plate. Bad habit from childhood... I know I'm not hungry anymore but I eat anyway. I just pusehd away my plate and ate the leftovers this morning.

Did a little w/o monday night, Ã  la BodyRock.Tv. Can't believe I was doing that EVERY DAY last year (in addition to the no-sleep). No wonder I was so exhausted all the time....Hello!! Did only a few exercices, mainly for abs and shoulders, and was feeling sore the day after. Went kayaking last night with friends, it was pretty relax, but I can feel it in my arms today!! I loved it! What a great way to relax and enjoy nature  I shoul ddo that more often.

### [email redacted] — 2012-06-16T17:01:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-bumpy-road-to-optimal.400/post-31540>

Felt asleep real quick last night, slept all night, got up once at 4, and woke up very tired. I hate that. But the good thing is that I'm sleeping at least. It pisses me off that disgressing only a little is affecting my sleep that much!! But the good news it that I can totally control that and its pretty easy actually. Can't wait to wake up again fresh and rested!!

Got the book "Weight Loss Apocalypse" this week and read it all. Interestimg. I like the idea of eating only when you are hungry, just like kids before we tell them to eat only because it's time to eat and its more convenient...Reminds me of a book i read when I stop binging, to help me stop categorize food as "good" or "bad". It was from a French author and it was called "Maigrir sans rÃ©gime". The principle was the same: eat whatever you want, but only when hungry. It helped me a lot to renew with hunger and satiety signals.

I'm really thinking about doing a round of HCG...I want to fix myself as much as possible before, but from what I read, it could help rewire a few things (sorry this is not clear!! lol My English skills doesn't allow me to explain it in my own words. I can read it and understand it, but putting it down myself is another thing!! ) I should get "Pounds and Inches" this week. I'll think about it...In "Weight Loss Apocalypse", she explain why disgressing, especially in P3, is so harmfull and can lead to gain all the weight back again. So it's not only a 40-days trip, it's 40 days + 3 weeks, so it's almost 9 weeks. I would do it only once since I have around 20 pounds to lose. Even if i can't lose it all in one round, I'll live with it. Will talk about it to my doc in August to see what he thinks about it.

### allison09 — 2012-06-17T04:59:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-bumpy-road-to-optimal.400/post-31694>

I love Lisa too!! She is in such great shape, holy cow! But when I see people that look like her I always wonder things like, how did she achieve that body? Is she ruining herself from all the cardio/working out/ eating 5 small means a day/eating grains? Is she inflamed? Does she menstruate? Is she _really_ that peppy all the time? (I bet she is!) There are people that do everything the opposite of what \*we're\* all doing, which is supposed to be correct for our bodies in an evolutionary/ancestral sense, but yet how does she look like THAT?! \*sigh\* I'll never understand.

You're so right about kids and their hunger signals. I've worked with young children a lot, and it always amazes me how they only eat when they are hungry. Well, most of them. I've definitely witnessed young children who already have severely warped relationships with food, and that is very sad, but the majority of them only eat when they are hungry. I could be offering the most enticing treat, and they just say nah, I'm not hungry. It's so simple, yet so amazing! I wonder if I was ever like that...

### [email redacted] — 2012-06-22T16:51:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-bumpy-road-to-optimal.400/post-32937>

88 days binge-free

Wow, almost 8 weeks completed on the leptin with no derailment!!

Slept badly again last night. Went to sleep around 3 and woke up at 9 tired and drained, with D. Was hungry around 1 and had coconut milk...could it be that? Was also feeling bloated and my legs were sensitive. They still are this morning.

BAB: 1 cup coconut milk. Couldn't have more, was feeling nauseous and full

Lunch: 4 squares of dark chocolate and a coffee

Dinner: game meat and veggies

Was a little hungry before bed but didn't have anything.

Read "Natural Hormone Balance for Women" by Uzzi Reiss as recomended by Darleen. That book is a must read! So many useful information!! He's talking about estrogem, progesterone, testosterone, HGH, DHEA, melatonin and pregnenolone: symptoms if those are too low or too high, who should take them and how.

I've learned that my low estrogen can cause difficulties falling asleep, GI discomfort, mental fogginess, sense of normalcy during second week of cycle (that means I would do better on high estrogen). Very interesting...

I've stopped Prometrium a couple of day before my labs were showing it's high, but I went back on it since I had problem sleeping. After reading the book, I understand that I might be too low on that too:

\-Heavy and frequent periods (with large clots)

\-Spotting a few days before the period

\-PMS for a few days

\-Difficulty sleeping

\-Water retention

Last time I tested for testosterone, is was low, but I'm waiting for the last lab results. Low testosterone means:

\- Flabiness and muscular weakness

\-Loss of hair

\-Lack of energy and stamina

\-Loss of coordination and balance

\-Loss of sense of security

\-Indecisiveness

\-Decreased sex drive

\-Poor body image

\-Decreased body air

I recognize myself in many of those...

Benefits of HGH:

\- Better muscle tone

\-Improved elasticity of the skin

\-Higher bone density

\-More vigor and energy

\-Greater sense of inspiration, zest, creativity and positivity

\-mental clarity

\-Reduced irritability

\-Can do attitute

\*\*Those with adreanal fatigue should not use HGH

Signs of DHEA deficiency:

\-Stressed

\-Lack of stamina

\-Intolerance to loud noise

\-Constant fatigue

\-poor mood

\-decreades immunity

\-memory loss

\-Lack of pubis hair

\-Poor abdominal muscle support

\-Dry skin and eyes

\-Poor sex drive

I'll do testing again next wedensday. I would have prefer to do it today as I'm on day 3, but I'll get my prescription only Tuesday. My friend finally agreed to help me again with that. Hopefully I'll get most of the results back for my consult with Dr K.

Will try to get myself going now...The day is going to be long  And a have a Spanish lesson this afternoon (the first one). Hopefully my brain will wake up sometimes today

Have a nice Friday all!!

### DarleenMB — 2012-06-24T18:58:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-bumpy-road-to-optimal.400/post-33308>

Heavy and frequent periods with large clots could also be a sign of fibroid tumors. THAT was what I had. When they finally yanked my parts the doc was in near hysteria ... he came barreling into my room the day after just AGLOW with the news that I had a kilo's worth of fibroids ... 2 fist sized and dozens of thumb sized inside and outside of the uterine wall. he TOOK PICTURES for me! It was so deformed it didn't even LOOK like a uterus. He told me, with great glee, that his fellow OB/GYN who assisted in the surgery said, with a great deal of sarcasm, "Well, THAT didn't need to come out, did it?" MEN.

You may want to have your GYN give you an ultrasound to be sure. That was how mine were finally discovered. stupid doctors. supid PA. I complained for 25 years about heavy periods. EVERY year that got worse. It was finally so bad I became anemic which I discovered only when I went to give blood and they kicked me out the door.

IF it turns out you have fibroids PROGESTERONE should fix them. They are (according to the books I read by the late Dr. John R. Lee) a result of estrogen dominance. In my case, I am CONVINCED, it happened because I blew out all my eggs at once in my late 20's. I got THAT bit of info from "Sex, Lies & menopause" the book I've been recommending (not the one by Uzzi Reiss ... I've never read that one). When that happens you do not produce progesterone to balance out the estrogen and the body is ALL ABOUT BALANCE. I love the word "homeostasis" because it says it all. The body wants/needs to stay in balance. We ruin that balance in so many ways in our current lifestyle.

The more I (try) to read and understand what Jack is saying the more I embrace that. We cannot deny our biology without serious, damaging, life destroying consequences.

Ok, this morning's rant is now complete.

Thank's Darleen for your message!!

Tumors? Thai is scary!! I saw my gyn 3 weeks ago and she gave me a prescription for an ultrasound. I wasn't planing do go, but now I will!! I will read that book from Wiley. I thought you also recommended the one from Reiss, sorry about that. It's really well written, telling what are the symptoms if you are low or too high for each hormones, what forms are better and how too adjust.

As I read Simeon "Pounds and Inches", I read that we can not apply any cream or oil on the skin and I thought that the hormones were in this category, that's why I asked on your journal if you were going to continue or not.

I'm seriously thinking about doing a round of HCG too, but still don't know if I should try bio-identical hormones first or wait til I'm done to do so. I'll wait until I've got my consult with Dr K before I make a decision.

### DarleenMB — 2012-06-25T18:06:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-bumpy-road-to-optimal.400/post-33463>

Well I'm not giving up my hormones.

I think it's very important to remember that creams today are not the same as the ones back in the 50's. Neither was tuna or beef. People are either too young to know that or have forgotten it. I don't think tuna even came water-packed back in the 50's. And they've fiddled with beef so much to make it "low-fat" that honestly I don't see how eating either could be a problem. We'll see. If my allergy panel turns out like I'm afraid it will I may have no choice in the matter.

### [email redacted] — 2012-06-26T01:06:59+0300

91 days binge-free

Slept good last night, but very little. Went to bed late, had to get up at 4 because my friend who was visiting was leaving for airport, ans then I had to get up at 8. Too little hours, and was very tired today. Just took a nap and I wish I could have stayed in bed and get up only tomorrow morning.

BAB: game meat and coconut milk

Lunch: eggs

Dinner: mackerel, dark chocolate

Congratulations on 92 days binge free. If you had to pinpoint little things that have made a differencea long the way, what would they be?

### [email redacted] — 2012-06-27T18:22:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-bumpy-road-to-optimal.400/post-33961>

Thank's PCG!

It's still a work in progress, and I guess it will always be, but if I could tell you what helped me, it would be those points:

1) BAB. At first, it was 85g of protein to make sure I would have any cravings. Then it was 70-ish grams, and not it's somewhere between 50 and 60g, but sometimes higher.

2) Enough fat. I can feel it when I don't have enough. I can't find any lard here, and sometimes I'm just tired of CO. I do my own ghee too, and the BPC if the best way to get my fat. When I was still craving sweet at the beginning, I was having CO even if that wasn't meal time. It's better than sugar even if I was off with the schedule...

3) I can't have nuts, coconut manna in the house. Everytime I buy some telling myself I'll be fine this time, I'm not. I bought almonds last week-end and ate them in 2 sittings. I don't consider it as binging (because before I would have ate tons of food after without being able to stop myself), but I know it's a no-go. I'm getting better with dark chocolate. I don't feel the urge to eat it all when I have some. But if I do, I don't feel bad, it's sugar free and full of fat

4)I also have a difficult time with fruits and sweet potatoes. When I buy berries, I eat them all in no time. I bought cherries yesterday. I choose the smallest size to make sure, but they're all gone. Everytime I tell myself I shouldn't but any anymore. Same with sweet potatoes. If I start and have a few, I'll want more and more.

5)Over the last year and a half, I've learned to listen to satiety and fullness signs. This one is difficult and it takes times. Most of the time I still eat 3 meals a day, but I listen to myself especially at lunch time and dinner. Lunch is always pretty small (protein and avocado), but I have it anyway because if I get too hungry in the afternoon, I might eat whatever come in sight and eat beyond fullness. You're hungry when you feel it in your stomac, not when you want to eat. If you are not sure, you can have water and wait a little. If you are not hungry for a steak, you probably are not hungry, period. When you eat and food doesn't sound as tasty as it was when you began, it's a sign you are not hungry anymore. You have to listen carefully and not eat too fast.

6) I'm not as hard on myself as I used to be. That feels good  I'm not perfect, and I don't have to be. Sometimes I eat stuff I shouldn't, but that's ok, I'll go back on track on the next meal. I've always had a hard time not eating at night when not sleeping. Those last 2 weeks, I can't go to sleep before 3 o'clock in the morning, and I get hungry at midnight or one. Sometimes I eat, sometimes I don't. I now I shouldn't, but I don't beat myself up with that and I get up with my BAB the next morning.

7) I stopped weighting myself. Scale is only a number, I don't want to focuss my life on that anymore. I want to be healthy and strong. I go by how I feel, and not by how I weight.

8) Learning that binging is not psychological or emotional, but physiological. If I want to binge, it's not because I'm stressed/sad/angry. It's because there is something unbalanced inside me. I wish I knew that before!! Usually if I want to binge it's because I lack sleep, I haven't had enough fat or I had sugar and I want more and more!! Understanding what is happening is helping me a lot!

9) Journaling. It help me a lot. And couting the binge-free days too when I started. I wa smotivated by the number going up and didn't want to go back to zero. Now I've lost count, I have to go to previous messages to do the count. I know I'm approching the 100 days mark, and I'm so proud of it. I know however that I'm still vulnerable to a binge, and I'll probably will be for a while, but seeing the progress is really great.

It's not vain to want to fit in tiny clothes or be as thin as your friends. I know you didn't chose that over health and having a baby. But I know it's a constant battle. When I was in that circle, I thought I was having the control on my body. But at the end, I was always losing. I wasn't happy even if I was thin, because I was obsessing with food, the scale, staying away from social events, isolating myslef from my friends and familly. I was always feeling tired, stressed, puffy and gross. Talk about having control! I was also ao angry at myself, I coudn't see the end of that. I just wanted to be normal again! I sometimes wonder where I would be now if I didn't start the leptin reset protocol. I would be in some miserable place beating myself up and praying for some help. If you change your goal from "being thin" to "being healthy", at some point the size of your clothes won't matter to you anymore because you'll feel good about yourself and your body.

If I think of something else, I'll come add it. I'm with you with all my heart, I know it's hard but I know you can do it and you will do it.

### [email redacted] — 2012-06-27T18:29:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-bumpy-road-to-optimal.400/post-33965>

I've had some treat here and there in the past weeks (glass of wine, nuts, fruits) and I want to go back to a keto diet now. It makes it difficult with all the parties with friends, but I know I can do it.

I'll keep the chocolate since it's 99% and sugar-free.

Crapy sleep again last night. Slept from 5 to 10  I'm having my BAB pretty late, then dinner, and often I'm hungry in the evening. I try to have a bigger dinner, but I din't like to eat past full. Was hungry a little last night and ate all the cherries I bought yesterday at the market... So no fruit anymore, no wine, no nuts!!

BAB: veal liver, avocado and kale.

Lunch: had to have one, I was hungry. Had 3 eggs in CO

Dinner: cod. Didn't eat much, I was full fast.

I just realized that I'm not having as much fat as I use to. Could it have any incidence on my sleep? I'm not having BPC anymore, so CO and ghee are limited.

This is amazingly helpful...you have no idea. The last paragraph about fighting yourself and needing control and isolating yourself is exactly what I was doing! I'm going to repost this in my journal, so I don't lose your advice. Actually, I'm going to print it out and hang it on my fridge as a constant reminder. THANK YOU!

### [email redacted] — 2012-06-28T15:44:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-bumpy-road-to-optimal.400/post-34139>

Had my first consult with Dr Kruse last night. Wow!! He's really great! I'm glad my history and labs were almost finished written down so I could book the consult. I wish I was better in English though, it would be easier to exchange, but I really enjoyed it anyway.

First, I need more labs. I'm waiting for some results from the last time I tested a month ago (IGF-1, hormones, rT3), but I also need LH/FSH/prolactine and CRH. Those could reveal HA  If not, it would simply be peri-menopause (simply because it's more easy to fix).

Even if I do have menses every month, it could be HA (I had amenorrhoea while at school 3 years ago and Dr put me on BCP to fix it). All mu hormones are very, very low. Probably just enough so I still get menses, but to low anyway. As he explained it to me, it's like having a little gas in the tank: the car is still gonna work, but it won't go far...

Since the main problem in my life right now is the lack of sleep, I have to increase DHEA. And I mean really increase it. I'll use the regular DHEA since testosterone is low and DHEA probably won't be conversed to T. And then I'll have to monitor the level through blood test and try to keep it in the top range.

I have to be really careful with the light and circadian rythm. My room is already dark, and I might want to add a mask on my eyes. No more getting up a few times when I can't sleep, I have to stay in the dark. That's what I did yesterday. I went to bed with some relaxing music. It took me maybe an jour to fall asleep, and I slept all night! Woke up around 6:30 this morning. I went outside to expose myself to the sun light a few minutes and then made BAB. I have to be really strict with the light and wear my orange glasses.

Seafood, seafood, seafood. I need more of the good stuff. And seafood over fish. And more fat. Butter is ok. I should skip the coffee and chocolate.

CT is ok. And he told me that going swimming would be the best for me for the summer. In the morning to not impact my cortisol. I looked around my place and there is no outdoor pools opened in the morning. There's an indoor one opened from 6:30 to 8:00, so I might try to go there. If not, I'll go back to my tub.

I'll get my testing done and book another consult and the end of the summer.

Oh, and I forgot: I got to love my fat!  The weight I gained in the last year may actually be a protection for now... I'm glad I know that because I was thinking about doing a round of HCG. That would have mess me up big time!! And no BHRT now, until we know if it's HA or not.

### [email redacted] — 2012-06-29T22:47:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-bumpy-road-to-optimal.400/post-34449>

95 days binge-free

Slept well two day in a row!! Wouou!! I'll start counting the days i'm sleeping instead of the binge-free ones

Went to bed too late. I told Dr Kruse I am doing pretty good with the light, but I guess I'm wrong. I've been doing really good with that since Febuary: orange glasses, little to no TV, going to bed early. I'm feeling so much better now, I feel like seeing my friends again, and there's always a night out or a get together here and there. Week days I'm doing good, but week-ends are more difficult. I have to be more careful with that.

Upped the DHEA yesterday, and was feeling sleepy by 10 pm. Only went to bed at 11, but it took no time to fall asleep. Woke up at 6 this morning.

BAB: ground beef and eggs. Coffee. First one this week . The only reason I miss coffee is because of the fat I put in it!!

Lunch: shrimps and veggies

Dinner: will be shrimps again

Dr Kruse tols me to use butter and cream to up the fat in the morning, but the only cream I can find here is 35% coffee cream, it's not organic, and it's not from grass-fed cows. It is still ok?? I'm hesitating on having any...Will stick to the butter for now.

Also, I forgot to ask him about supplements...Are the ones I'm taking ok, or should I add any? I'll have to ask at my next consult.

I've noticed a change in my sweating pattern lately...I sweat much more than before. I used to not sweat at all, even forgetting to put anti-perspirant was ok for me. I really sweat a lot right now, and odor has changed. Also this week, it seems that I'm always hungry!! I'm not eating between meals, but if I could I would eat 2 to 3 hours after each meal!

### [email redacted] — 2012-06-30T15:28:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-bumpy-road-to-optimal.400/post-34571>

96 days binge-free!

Slept well again last night. Slept is nit as restfull as I would like it to be, but I'll take it anyway. Went to bed with a gel pack on my neck, kept it there maybe 20 minutes and took it off so I could fall asleep. It took me a little longer to fall asleep. I try to lay there, not focussing on actually falling asleep, just feeling good about being there, and then I quickly slip in sleep land. Not dreaming though. Or at least not remembering it. Woke up at 7 this morning, in the same position I fell asleep. Getting up is easier. Instead of laying there for half an hour, I'm up in a few minutes and feeling great. I get some sesame oil to do oil pulling, and I post myself in the window a few minutes to get some sun light, and then BAB.

BAB: 250g shripms with brocoli, mushrooms, red curry and turmeric, coconut milk. Cooked 3 eggs with that but couldn't eat them, I was full.

Lunch:

Dinner:

Third day of high dose of DHEA.

Have a BD party tonight, I'll try to come back home not too late so I get a good night sleep.

### [email redacted] — 2012-07-04T04:36:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-bumpy-road-to-optimal.400/post-35413>

Time for a little update. Had a really nice long week-end, lots of times outside. I'm still going to bed too late tho. I'll focus on that in July, its gonna be my goal.

Bought some cream to add to my BAB as suggested. Felt full all day today! Had dinner, but ate too much (can't leave lobster in my plate)

Sleep has been good for the last few days but saturday night. had 2 drinks at my friend's party, and paid for it. I sleep, but very poorly. I'm still on high dose DHEA, I reduced from last week, but I'm still pretty high. Have much more energy too, that feels great.

Not doing much CT and I miss it. It difficult for me to go to the pool in the morning, so I'll try to get back into my tub. I don't really like when water is 60 something degres, I prefer way colder, but for now it's better than nothing.

Good night all! Going to CT my carotids in my bed.

DHEA: 350mg

### [email redacted] — 2012-07-05T03:08:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-bumpy-road-to-optimal.400/post-35569>

100 days binge-free!! wouou!!!

Never thought I could go so far. And now it's basically effortless. It's like a had a switch that was on for so long and made me vulnerable at any time to binge, and now it's off. Being stressed, being tired, being starving, feeling guilty because I had something I shouldn't have, alchool: it's not bringing cravings anymore. So, so glad about it. Not saying there's no risk at all anymore, but now I can concentrate on semething else than food and the next binge.

Went to bed early last night (9:30), and was wide awake between 3 an 5. Went back to sleep til 9 this morning and I'm in pretty good shape today. I usually get up in the night when that happens, turning on the lights, reading, and sometimes getting on Internet. Last night I foccused on staying in the dark, listening to music, and trying not to worry and stress about being awake.

Did some CT this morning: 30 minutes in a 71F bath, and did ice spotting on belly for an hour. Will do it again tonigh, and now I'm going to bed every night with a soft ice pack on my neck.

Will go to bed early again tonight, lights are down and I'm gonna relax with a book and my ice packs.

DHEA: 400mg

Slept good last night again (thank's to my dear DHEA), but woke up aroubd 5 for a while. Pretty tired this morning. Had a big day yesterday for work, maybe that's why.

### [email redacted] — 2012-07-10T15:36:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-bumpy-road-to-optimal.400/post-36529>

Spent two days with a friend by a lake. That was amazing. Could have stayed there forever! Got plenty of sun, slept very good and enjoyed the nature.

Slept good again last night after doing some yoga by myself in my living room. I did some last year at a studio and really enjoyed it. Found a beginner video on you tube and just did it. It was ending with breathing exercices. Was much harder than last year, even with basic postures. I could fell that the weight I put on had a big impact. I'd like to do it several times a week to unwind and help with sleep.

Running out of DHEA. No good! Will drive to the States tomnorrow to pick up an order from amazon. Just talked to my doctor friend, I should get labs prescription by tomorrow. Will test asap so I can do another consult with Dr K with the results.

I'm still not working and enjoying the summer. I'm starting my businness and it's taking longer than expected, so I'm taking advantage of it. I have work to do and meetings, but don't need to get up with the alarm in the morning. It really happens on a good time for me, allowing me to get plenty of rest and taking care of my health. I'm looking forward to work again, but I know when I'll do I'll be in a much greater place in my mind and in my body. As soon as I start working, I'll look to move out of the city (work will be away from the city too) and find a more quiet place. I want a place with a big bath tub and a terasse so I can enjoy the summer (can't do it at current place). And if possible, I'd love a place by a river. We have lots of lakes and rivers here, it would be great to take advantage of it and do outdoor CT

In the tub right now. Cooled it with huge ice cubes I made by frozing water in plastic containers. Will leave the keybord and submerge myself to the neck.

Have a good day all, thank's for reading

### [email redacted] — 2012-07-11T00:15:05+0300

Thank's girls! I'm actually feeling super good!  Never felt that good before. Still feeling tired at the end of the day if I have too much on my plate, but people who know me are commenting on how great and relaxed I look. Pretty nice to hear

I'm a podiatrist. I finished school last year and worked for others in the last year, and my office should be starting by the end of August. I couldn't have done that last year, I was too much stressed and had no energy. I can deal much better with stress, I have more energy and now that I'm sleeping better, everything is falling into place and I can concentrate on work

### [email redacted] — 2012-07-11T15:40:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-bumpy-road-to-optimal.400/post-36801>

> **Souldanzer;36821 wrote:** I hope you like vibrams and barefooting.... my pod did not  had to fire him. oops.

Podiatry is very different here compared to the States. Most podiatrist don't do surgery. And lots are into manual therapy (Foot mobilization and Active release technique), which is really great.

You won't find many pod liking vibrams and barefooting...I think it's great if you have a good mechanics, but patients I'm seeing have heel spurs, tendonitis, bunions, knee pain... And they don't want to do the simple exercices we are given them. So we often end up with insoles...

I like my profession, and I love the fact that I'm helping my patients, but knowing what I know now, I would have choosen another field. I'll probably take another degree within a few years in biochemistry or nutrition to be able to concentrate my practice on chronic pain and DM patients. I already talk about food and impact on arthritis and other conditions with my patients, but few are willing to change. It's killing me. They prefer to take their cocktail of medications than avoid chimical stuff in their plate. But I'm talking about it anyway, and I show them testimonals of people that healed with changing diet.

### [email redacted] — 2012-07-11T15:49:49+0300

107 days binge-free

Slept very well last night again. That feels so good, I can't believe it! Had a nice evening: went reading to the park, did some yoga and then went in bed with a book. Went to sleep a little late (11:00), and slept til 7 this morning.

I'm in the tub right now, will stay for an hour. Water is 65F.

Will drive to the States this PM and pick up DHEA. Yesterday two big zits appeared on my chin: is testosterone level rising? Will soon find out, I have appointement this Friday for blood tests.

### Souldanzer — 2012-07-11T18:58:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-bumpy-road-to-optimal.400/post-36855>

> **AbetterME;36967 wrote:** Podiatry is very different here compared to the States. Most podiatrist don't do surgery. And lots are into manual therapy (Foot mobilization and Active release technique), which is really great.
>
>
>
> You won't find many pod liking vibrams and barefooting...I think it's great if you have a good mechanics, but patients I'm seeing have heel spurs, tendonitis, bunions, knee pain... And they don't want to do the simple exercices we are given them. So we often end up with insoles...
>
>
>
> I like my profession, and I love the fact that I'm helping my patients, but knowing what I know now, I would have choosen another field. I'll probably take another degree within a few years in biochemistry or nutrition to be able to concentrate my practice on chronic pain and DM patients. I already talk about food and impact on arthritis and other conditions with my patients, but few are willing to change. It's killing me. They prefer to take their cocktail of medications than avoid chimical stuff in their plate. But I'm talking about it anyway, and I show them testimonals of people that healed with changing diet.

Count me in on the bunions, knee pain, tendonitis. I have orthotics all over the place. Ditched the shoes when I was faced with surgery and for now I am fine. I'll admit I ditched some of the chronic cardio at the same time. But that doesn't keep people from wondering b/c I have \*bad\* bunions from dancing (never wore high heels but probably too tight shoes).

This winter some of the pain came back b/c I had to wear boots at times. It always went away with ditching the shoes. It's the shoes that make for bad mechanics and it's the bad nutrition etc that makes for the inflammation to go haywire. So I think it's great you keep talking to people despite them not liking it. I've got the opposite - I keep talking while my docs want to shut me up with meds

### [email redacted] — 2012-07-13T02:00:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-bumpy-road-to-optimal.400/post-37327>

> **Souldanzer;37021 wrote:** Count me in on the bunions, knee pain, tendonitis. I have orthotics all over the place. Ditched the shoes when I was faced with surgery and for now I am fine. I'll admit I ditched some of the chronic cardio at the same time. But that doesn't keep people from wondering b/c I have \*bad\* bunions from dancing (never wore high heels but probably too tight shoes).
>
>
>
> This winter some of the pain came back b/c I had to wear boots at times. It always went away with ditching the shoes. It's the shoes that make for bad mechanics and it's the bad nutrition etc that makes for the inflammation to go haywire. So I think it's great you keep talking to people despite them not liking it. I've got the opposite - I keep talking while my docs want to shut me up with meds

Shoes are rarely the source of bunions. Over pronation from ligaments hyperlaxity and lack of flexibility of the calves are. And then causing knee pain, posterior tibialis tendonitis, back pain....Shoes can worsen or accelerate the deformaty, but you have to be mechanicaly disadvantaged in the first place. I hope in does make sense!  Not use to talk about that in English.

Funny that you are pain free when ditching the shoes....Don't have enough experience since to tell if going barefoot would be better for patients...Will get back to you in a few years

### [email redacted] — 2012-07-13T02:02:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-bumpy-road-to-optimal.400/post-37329>

> **PaleoCowgirl;37160 wrote:** Just stopping by to say your daily count of how many days you've been binge free is inspiring. I think I'm going to start that in my journal. Thanks!

It helped me very much! At first, my goal was to reach the next 5-days mark, and then, the next 10-days one. When I wanted to binge, I was thinking about that and didn't want to go back to zero. I'm not counting anymore, I have to go back to calculate where I am, and I'm the first to be impressed to see I'm over 100 days binge-free. You'll get there two, be patient

### [email redacted] — 2012-07-13T02:13:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-bumpy-road-to-optimal.400/post-37334>

Slept good last night. Woke up at 5 this morning, went back to sleep til 8.

Did yoga last night even if I came back home late. It helped so much to relax the two nights before that I did it anyway. I'm already getting better with the sarvangasana pose (the one where you stand of your shoulders).

Did some CT yesterday morning for an hour, and will do the same tonight, and then yoga. I know it's better to CT in morning as long as we don't know how cortisol and friends are doing, but I crave it, it's really hot here.

I lowered the amount of protein I'm having on breakfast. I'm having 4 eggs and fat in my coffee. Didn't have lunch yesterday, and today I had a coffee with cream and CO. Was feeling fine with the no-lunch, that's a first.

Have a nice evening all

> Note to myself: don't buy cherries anymore, or buy only in small amount at the time. Ate them all in one sitting yesterday  They were pretty good BTW.
>
>
>
> Read the thread about the mackerel smoothie...Have been wanting to try it for a few weeks. I have to find a place where they sell mackerel and make a big girl of myself. I asked a friend to get me some from my home town, but he only brought me back some without the heads. He said what I wanna do is too disgusting. I didn't ask for your opinion, I asked for some heads!!  Never better served than by ourself right?
>
>
>
> Just booked a week-end on the east coast with some friends for next week. Can't wait to do some CT in the ocean!!
>
>
>
> Have a good day all!!
>
>
>
> K

I know what you mean about cherries .... I love them but my sugar shot sky high. No other fruit does that to me.

### vkiernan — 2012-07-17T14:52:24+0300

Did you actually eat the heads only? OMG, you are sooo brave! I told my dh about this and he about gagged. He might think I've gone off the deep end if I do it. ### [email redacted] — 2012-07-17T19:04:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-bumpy-road-to-optimal.400/post-38760>

Yes, only heads  Raw. In the blender with some water and lemon juice. Why mackerel? I know it's pretty high in Omega-3, but couldn't tell if other fish could do it.

Since I upped the DHEA, I am sleeping so well and feeling much better. I am now taking 200mg/day.

That's funny because yesterday I had way less carbs than usual (no fruits and berries), and I woke up at 3 in the morning, just like I used to a few weeks ago, and it took me a while to go back to sleep.

### [email redacted] — 2012-07-18T18:09:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-bumpy-road-to-optimal.400/post-39023>

114 days binge-free

Slept very good last night. did not get up at all til 8 this morning. Trying hard to go to bed by 10. No yoga last night , wasn't feeling like it. Turned all the lights down with sunset and used candles to read in silence. It took me a little while to fall asleep. Would like to pass out when I hit the pillow. Increased the DHEA to 250mg this morning to see if it will make any difference.

Did 25 minutes CT this morning. My body is getting firmer, I love that. Didn't step on the scale for a while. Not sure if the weight has move, and don't want to know for now.

Did not had lunch yesterday but the coffee + CO. Typical meals are 4 eggs in the morning + fat (cream and CO). BPC for lunch and shrimps for dinner. Few berries and fruits. Few veggies but not much.

### [email redacted] — 2012-07-20T02:04:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-bumpy-road-to-optimal.400/post-39524>

Didn't sleep last night. Was it because of the french fries I had on dinner? Or maybe because of the glass of wine? Or maybe both? I was having a party in my belly and it wasn't so great. I was drowsy til 2 or so, and then I was wide awake for the rest of the night. Finally felt asleep at 7 and slept three hours. Was miserable for a few hours this morning. It's 7 pm and feeling very tired. Will do some yoga and be in bed early.

Had srimps for lunch and lobster for dinner. Lucky me, I got a lady lobster full of eggs (don't know what you say that in English). Was soooo good, but couldn't finish it. Nice leafover for tomorrow

Read BG5 this morning...Wow. Will have to repeat the smoothie experience. Pretty hard to fing mackerel here, but I guess fresh sardines would be fine too. Have to try Inger recipe with the salmon too.

### [email redacted] — 2012-07-21T16:17:00+0300

Didn't do any CT the last 2 days. I'm having my BAB and the tub is ready for the next session. Tried to have a sardine smoothie this morning, but couldn't do it!! The odor was disgusting, couldn't get it down. It ended in the toilet. Sorry little sardines.

### [email redacted] — 2012-07-26T15:24:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-bumpy-road-to-optimal.400/post-41183>

122 days binge-free!! That's 4 months!!

Slept good last night! I'm feeling so good this morning. Did yoga, some reading, took a hot shower and listened to my relaxation app and fell asleep in minutes. Hot shower is not really optimal, but it relaxes me so much, and priority right now is to sleep!! Woke up once in the night and fell back to sleep til 7:30 this morning.

Haven't CT in the last week because of the lack of sleep. Not really craving cold when it happens, but my place is pretty chilly with the AC. I wish I could sleep with the AC, but if I want my bedroom to be in the dark, it's impossible.

I'll have to be very careful in the next few weeks. Every time sleep is getting better, I'm slacking on the protocol, having too many carbs and some wine. I don't know if it's really the culprit, but I don't want to pay for it anymore for a week long.

I'm going to Hampton Beach, NH, this week-end. We couldn't rent rooms with kitchenette, so I'm a little worry about food. I'll bring with me cans of sardines and salmon, and I'll try to have CO and other stuff through the border. Hopefully the weather is going to be nice!!

### [email redacted] — 2012-08-01T00:58:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-bumpy-road-to-optimal.400/post-42152>

Came back from the beach last night. I was a really nice week-end, even though the weather wasn't so great. Did some CT in the ocean, ate well all week-end (snacked on almonds and had a few sips of wine), but didn't sleep well.

Got some lab results just before I left and I'm pretty concerned with Thyroid Peroxidase (TPO) Antibodies: 470 UI/mL!! (positive starts at 100). Anti Thyroglob is 67. TSH went from 2.16 in April, to 1.62 in May, and now it's 5.24. What the heck?? I'll wait until I get all thyroid panel, but could it be possible that something went wrong this time and the results are no good??

Next consult with Dr K is August 10th. Hopefully everything will be in by then. Can't wait to know what's going on...

### PaulaRichards — 2012-08-01T03:01:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-bumpy-road-to-optimal.400/post-42171>

I worked with a hematologist for several years and from what I saw & heard it was not totally unheard of for the lab at our hospital to screw things up. If everything is totally out of whack and looks like they might be I'd get it re-done. IMO. At least you don't have to wait long for your follow-up with Dr. Kruse. That's good!

### [email redacted] — 2012-08-03T22:50:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-bumpy-road-to-optimal.400/post-42759>

Oh my. Don't know what's wrong with me today. I was hungry at 11 (had BAB at 8!), had some smoked salmon, and was hungry again at 2, and feeling a little hypoglycemic. Wasn't home and really needed to eat. Had a fruit and almonds. Too much almonds. I went on eating even if I knew I wasn't hungry. And then thought about purging. Which I didn't do, but for a few minutes I was seriously thinking about it.

What is that? I feel rather good today, not as tired as this morning. Is it the sweet potatoe? I wasn't especially looking for sweet, but I needed to eat NOW. Let's do some relaxation exercice to calm down and feel great for the rest of the day.

and note to myself: DON'T BUT NUTS. EVER. capiche?

### [email redacted] — 2012-08-11T00:29:54+0300

Been feeling pretty good all week. Sleep was ok. Woke up at 5 yesterday morning. Did a w/o the night before, and skiped the DHEA. Not sure what caused the short night sleep. Was tired all day but feeling better than the last weeks. Throat is not as swollen as sore as it was last week. Had some wine last night on a date, but slept ok anyway. ### [email redacted] — 2012-08-11T15:31:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-bumpy-road-to-optimal.400/post-44312>

Slept like a baby last night  Did not use my relaxatin tape and fell asleep in no time, til 6:30 this morning. Would have like to sleep longer but I had to get up anyway. Feeling pretty good.

Loved my consult with Dr K last night, he's so nice and caring. He confirmed, as expected, that I have Hashi. TSH should be .050 to 1.0, and everything over 2.0 indicates hypo-thyroid. Mine was 5.24 in the last labs. Ouch!!

I'm not sure I understood that very well, but he says that I might have had Hashi for a long time and that my binging were linked to it. Inflammation was contributing to the cravings and binges. And I was feeding the inflammation and worsening Hashi by eating crap...He says that if I feel like binging, I can up the DHEA and Vit D to calm things down.

He suggest me I listen again the webinar about the hormones because there was a lot of questions about Hashi. Didn't pay much attention at that time, but as I'm listening to it right now, there's lot of good information for me...I have to go on with the no eggs, no dairies, no chocolate, no coffee...(I have to add no corn and no rice. Adios, lovely sushis!)

Dr K told me I've been feeling better in the last week because I cut them all on August 2nd. It's crazy how fast I can feel the effects. I did the same in May, I cut completely the eggs (but not the chocolate and the coffee), and I felt wayyyy better, I wasn't sure it was because of that and I introduced them again beginning of July (where I started to feel more tired and tired). Last consult I had with Dr K, Hs-CRP was very low and Thyroid panel was pretty good, so he didn't think any AI was an issue. I'm glad we could see it on the last labs. Seems that all the time I thought it was cortisol issue, it was Hashi making me so tired and fatigued. So glad I know what's wrong! Mush easier to fix....I'm seeing my functionnal Dr next Thuesday, so I'll have him prescribe me T3 or Cytomel, and gonna wait for the other hormones until thyroid is balanced. If I'm stressed it affects my sleep, he suggested I could use P (cream or Prometrium) and oxytocin...

Will have to check Vit D and make sure it's high enough. DHEA is high right now, but that's fine and helping my immune system (and sleep!), so I'll keep supplement to 100mg/day. Have to make sure I'm takimg enough Magnesium, Vit C and CoQ10 (not takimg the later at the moment, will have to add it). Will have to test also for HS-CRP, Free T3/T4, TSH, ASI.

Dr K also suggested me bone or seafood broth. Can't really get any bones from GF beef, so he suggested I buy some New-Zeland lamb at Costco and use the bones. Will find out if I can get any here in Canada. Or I'll do the seafood one, much easier for me. Kombucha is ok early in the day, Kimchi would be good too. I should really look into doing my own fermented veggies, would be cheaper.

Now I can't wait to see my doctor and start the T3 to see how I'll feel . Thank's Dr K!!

### [email redacted] — 2012-08-12T16:01:25+0300

Slept good again last night. Went to bed a little late after a great evening with my best friend at restaurant. Had oysters and salmon tartare. I'm still having the same dose of magnesium every night, but as Dr K suggested, I have one capsule of neuro mag sublingual. Don't know if its because of that, but I was asleep in no time last night and the night before. Woke up at 6:30 again, feeling a little tired but not too bad.

Finally made a little effort to find how to make fermented veggies. So easy, I have no excuse not making any, especially that I haven't found any in store here. Bought cabbage and did it this morning. I hope it's gonna be fine and tasty

Works started at clinic yesterday. I have much to do, but I'm pretty exited. I'm so glad I feel better and not as tired as I was lately. I can take care of all my stuff without worrying and feeling worned out, and I'm not stressed anymore about the opening and starting to work again full time.

### [email redacted] — 2012-08-13T15:53:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-bumpy-road-to-optimal.400/post-44672>

140 days binge-free

Slept well again last night. This is getting boring to read, I know, but I Love it!!  And I'm falling asleep so fast, I haven't listened to my relaxation tape for a few days, I put my head on the pillow and I wake up hours later. Woke up a little too early this morning (5:30), but that mean I had almost 7 hours sleep. I'm tired this morning, it usually takes a few hours to litf up and then I feel fine the rest of the day. Coffee would be more than welcome, but I know its not a good idea.

Had oysters again last night for dinner. I really liked the ones I had at restaurant on Saturday, so I went to buy some. The lady at the fish market was so nice, she showed me how to open them. Pretty easy when you know the technique. So I had a dozen of them and some smoked salmon. I love seafood and fish so much, I could eat only that. I wish it wasn't that expensive. I still manage to eat some everyday, I eat a lot of shrimps, but they are farmed. When I went to Hampton beach 2 weeks ago, I was so amazed to see all those great places selling fish and seafood. It was paradise. And I was also pretty amazed to see how they were prepared: everything is fried!!  Didn't really enjoyed the restaurants because of that: I couldn't find anything to my taste. No wonder why people are so obese. Here, you can eat crap easily if you want to, but you can also eat very healthly too. I can't say people are more healthy here, but it's not as bad as our neighbors.

Got to work a little now. Have a nice day all

### [email redacted] — 2012-08-15T14:56:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-bumpy-road-to-optimal.400/post-45006>

Stiil sleeping good but waking up pretty early. 4:30 yesterday and 5:15 today. I feel great though today, better than usually.

Went to my doctor yesterday. A naturopath went through my file first, and then I had a Skype consult with the doctor. It was my first time with this one. He was nice, but you could tell he wanted to hang up asap. He prescribed me T3/T4, and didn't want to prescribe me T3 only. He told me Dr here in Canada are not allowed to do it, he has to start with T3/T4 first and change to T3 eventually if results are not good enough. He wants me back in 2 months, and meanwhile, he would like me to do the elimination diet. Not really enthousiastic about this one, but I guess I have to do it. I will start it this week-end, I'll make sure I cook ahead enough food to make sure I follow it properly.

He also told me to take supplements to support the adrenals. I don't understand though why they don't test first. I'll go back on some supplements I already have at home and see how I feel. He wants me to get tested for Thyroid panel and hormones before the next visit, and he agreed to add Vit D and Hs-CRP.

I'll get the T3/T4 in a couple of days and will see how I feel.

### [email redacted] — 2012-08-18T02:37:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-bumpy-road-to-optimal.400/post-45474>

Got a total of 5 and half hour of sleep. Def not enough for me. I'm tired, but still can function properly, which is pretty good. Had a nap this afternoon and will go to bed early tonight and will try to rest.

Started my T3/T4 this morning. I guess it will take several days for me to see any difference. ### [email redacted] — 2012-08-19T16:18:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-bumpy-road-to-optimal.400/post-45670>

146 days binge-free

Slept very well last night. Went to bed late because we worked at clinic until 11 to get all the cabinets built up and installed, and I fell asleep in no time, until 7:30 this morning for a total of 8 hours. Still tired this morning, but I'm ok.

Didn't eat much yesterday. Had the usual BAB (coconut milk and game meat), and couldn't go out neither for lunch and dinner, so I only had a can of sardines for both. I was surprised that I was fine eating only that. Was a little hungry by the time I got home, but didn't want to eat to make sure I would sleep well. Yeah, didn't want to eat even if I was hungry. Pretty amazing. Few weeks ago, I could never have fell asleep beoing hungry. Now I know I'll survive, and I find myself having better sleep if I eat light and early.

The T3 prescription I got is very low, so I decided to take two pills every morning instead of one, and will see how I feel in the next weeks. When I asked my Dr to prescribe me 50mg T3, he said that is a very high dose and that I shouldn't take that like candies....Oh well...Hopefully he will be open to try a few things with me in the future...

Have a nice Sunday all, enjoy your day

Kombucha has been sitting for 2 weeks now. Since it's cool in my place, I put in a cupboard and I left the light on so it could keep it warm. Took a closer look at it...Not pretty! Wasn't sure I wanted to taste it, but it's good. I'll let it sit a few more days since I want as little sugar as possible in it. ### Souldanzer — 2012-08-21T15:06:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-bumpy-road-to-optimal.400/post-46028>

take a pic?

if it just turned a bit tan... that's fine. Sometimes they get darker spots. The older they are the darker (browner) they will get. The stuff you see online for purchase (bright white perfect) is pretty unrealistic. The only way I have managed to grow such a pretty scoby is by using green or white tea only.... and grow a brand new scoby with that.

Now if the color you're talking about is mold, then you need to throw it away. Did you leave the door ajar to the cupboard? You need air circulation.

### [email redacted] — 2012-08-22T14:29:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-bumpy-road-to-optimal.400/post-46265>

### Souldanzer — 2012-08-23T05:37:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-bumpy-road-to-optimal.400/post-46488>

151 days binge-free

This is a crazy week. Not enough sleep. Bad sleep. getting up at 5 every morning. Stressing about the opening. I need a break! I'm doing good overall, but Wednesday and today are really difficult due to tireness. Patience is limited too. Working next week will feel like vacations

Food is good, having lots if caned sardines and mackerel. Shrimps were in special at grocery shore, so I loaded my freezer. They are farmed, but it's still better than nothing. Bought blueberries yesterday and ate them all at night. BAD. Slept poorly after and woke up at 4.

### [email redacted] — 2012-08-25T15:39:41+0300

Bought lots of fish yesterday: shrimps, calamari, salmon, Spain mackerel, scallops. ### [email redacted] — 2012-08-27T14:59:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-bumpy-road-to-optimal.400/post-47468>

154 days binge-free

Day 1 of Strict AI protocol

Took a while to fall asleep last night. Woke up around 6:30 this morning, pretty tired, but I'm feling better now that I'm eating. Trying to control my thoughts and tell myself I am not stressed about the clinic and that I'm rather happy about this new change and to be my own boss. Much better

Started a new batch of kombucha last night. Hopefully this one will be good, I have to stop buying the store's ones, they are so expensive.

ETA: forgot to mention that TOM got here today. Last cycle was 23 days. A little short. Wondering if it's because I used Pg for a few nights, and then I stopped, causing the bleeding to begin??

BAB: game meat, kombucha, coconut milk, avocado.

Lunch: chicken. yeah, I know. had to go to restaurent with collegue.

Dinner: got home late. Had sardines and an avocado.

Sun exposure: not much

Ct: It's time to go back on track! I couldn't tolerate much cold in July and was taking lots of HOT showers, and now with the medication and (almost) AI diet, I can't tolerate much the hot water. Will start slowly with face dunking. -

Happy Monday to all of you,

K

### [email redacted] — 2012-08-28T13:13:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-bumpy-road-to-optimal.400/post-47742>

155 days binge-free

Day 2 of Strict AI Protocol

Slept good last night, woke up around 4:30, went back to sleep and had to wake up at 5:30. This should be the last morning I have to get up that early.

Dinner: caned mackerel and an avocado

### [email redacted] — 2012-09-09T19:04:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-bumpy-road-to-optimal.400/post-50512>

Thank's Destiny, I finally fugured it out

Had my consult this morning. I didn't have any new labs, but wanted to talk about my sleep. Dr K told me to take Prometrium 2 hours prior going to bed, that should hell better than the cream. The fact that I sleep better with carbs indicates that my T3 is low... And there's two way to up it: carbs and iodine. That means I should hold on to shrimps and let go the carbs and their negative effects...

Apparently, Kombucha and fermented veggies are fine with me. Lauren, I couldn't tell you why...I'm so not used to speak in English anymore, writing is fine, but I'm a bit nervous when I have my consults with Dr K, trying to understand what he is telling me and formulating my questions!!

I can up the T3 again, but for that I'll have to get a new prescription from the Dr...Will call tomorrow to see if it's possible anytime soon.

I've had diarrhea for 2 weeks now, and he says it's fine, it means my gut is changing in a good way.

Wine is not that bad, but right now it does mess up with my gut flora, and that's not ideal. For sleep neither....

I already have Prometrium at home, so I'll start it tonight. I'm off to enjoy the weather and get some sun

### [email redacted] — 2012-09-11T03:19:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-bumpy-road-to-optimal.400/post-50874>

Thank's V. I wish you would feel the same, I know it's been a long time for you on the reset.

Honestly, I don't change it up much...Always the same things: game meat in the morning, sometimes shrimps or salmon (lefftovers). When I work, I have a can of mackerel or sardines. Easy and quick. And at night, I have shrimps or some sort of fish. I do sautÃ© with shrimps, it's really quick, or if I feel lazy, I only cook them is CO and I had turmeric ans pepper, it makes a good sauce when mixed with the CO. If I have firsh, I cook it in the oven. It usually takes no longer than 10-12 minutes 'cause I don't like it too much cooked. That's when I go to restaurant that I treat myself with tartare or some fish I don't cook. I should get a cook book on tartares, it's really easy to make, and so delicious!!

I'm really good with my diet. I want to get better so bad, the only things I was having problem with is dark chocolate, so I did throw it away. I love wine, but I know I'll feel bad for days if I have any, so I'm really careful with that.

### [email redacted] — 2012-09-11T03:24:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-bumpy-road-to-optimal.400/post-50875>

I'd say if you really want to do it the right way, you have to forget about chocolate for now. No dairies, no nuts, no chocolate, no coffee, no rice, no corn, no nightshades. I know. It's a lot of no's... But you can't expect to feel great if you don't do it at 100%. Keeping only a little of what can nurture inflammation will keep you from feeling better. We are so sensitive to it, we can't take any chance. The first few days are the worst, and then you get used to it. And hopefully you feel better.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-bumpy-road-to-optimal.400/post-52887>

Good morning V,

I got my T3 Rx by my doc (no way you can order that online in Canada). He prescribed 25 mcg/day, but I started with 50, and upped the dose til 100 mcg/day. I'm not sure yet if it's the right dose, I'll see how it goes in the next few days. They explain very well on StopThe ThyroidMadness how to titrate the doses.

Seems that there's a protein in coffee that triggers gluten antibodies. It's pretty well explained here: [http://www.thehealthyhomeeconomist.com/gluten-issues-or-celiac-dont-drink-coffee/](http://http://www.thehealthyhomeeconomist.com/gluten-issues-or-celiac-dont-drink-coffee/)

### [email redacted] — 2012-09-21T16:10:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-bumpy-road-to-optimal.400/post-53167>

180 days binge-free

Day 24 of Strict AI protocol.

Slept much better last night. Went to bed late, and slept a good 6 hours. Tired this morning, but feeling better than this week.

Read that on Metabolic Effect FB page:

"Estrogen makes it so you are better able to tolerate carbs. Progesterone does the reverse making you far more likely to store fat when you are eating carbs. Knowin

g this, you may want to make sure you pay far more attention to carbs during the 2 weeks leading up to menses (called the luteal phase, which is a progesterone dominant time).

Another thing to know about menses is that just before menses begins estrogen and progesterone levels both drop and this can have an impact on brain chemistry and cause cravings. Knowing this allows you to make smart choices during this time to control these cravings.

Our favorite strategy to combat this is unsweet organic baking cocoa powder. 1 Tablespoon in water with a non-calorie sweetener does wonders for stopping the negative impact of cravings during this time."

Wondering if it has anything to do with my bad sleep: maybe I can't process carbs very well on second part of my cycle and sleep is being impacted. I have no problem beeing low carbs most of the time, but this week I was really craving fruits. Will have to be carefull in the next months. Since my cycle is si short, in means I'm in luteal phase 14 days out of 24 every month.

With fall coming anyway, I really have to let go of the fruits. And add even more CT. Heading to the tub before work. Oh, and another thing: I have to go out when I get up to get sun light. Will be hard but I have to do it

BAB: shripms, CO, kombucha

Lunch:

Dinner:

Sun light first thing in the morning:

No lights at night:

CT:

### [email redacted] — 2012-09-24T16:09:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-bumpy-road-to-optimal.400/post-53779>

183 days binge-free.

I started this thread exactly 6 months ago, and didn't binge since. Never thought it could be possible to go so long without compulsion. I was so broken at that time, and so desperate for sleep! Haven't find my way yet to optimal, but I'm coming closer. I've never felt that good in my life!

Slept very good the night before yesterday, and turned a lot last night. Feeling good this morning. Cravings did not come back. Upped my T3 to 125 mcg/day. Stopped DHEA and now only taking 7-keto (was having acne last week).

Had plenty of sun yesterday. It was Montreal Oasis Marathon and my sister and BIL were running the 10km, so I babysat their baby and walked around. Seeing all those people running was pretty impressive! 27 000 persons in total for the whole marathon. I wish I could run a 10 km next year, but I don't want to train as a runner. Will see... Will start with weight training hopefully anytime soon.

Bought some 5% Lugol this week-end, but don't know if i should take it and in what dose. Will wait til my next consult with Dr K to see if it's usefull with Hashis...

BAB: game meat, CO, coconut milk, kombucha

Sun light: got some when I got up.

CT: 20 minutes after BAB.

No lights at night:

Here's something I read on Bellatrix Nutrition FB page this am:

"Something nobody can take away from you is the way you choose to respond to what others say and do to you. The last of your freedoms is to choose your attitude in any given circumstance. Choose to be happy and positive. It is not always easy to find happiness in ourselves, but it is always impossible to find it elsewhere. Regardless of the situation we face, our attitude is our choice. So smile as you walk away and move on from negative people and their actions." I like it...

### [email redacted] — 2012-09-28T22:07:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-bumpy-road-to-optimal.400/post-55071>

187 days binge-free

A little update for the last few days. Slept really well. Woke up at 5 this morning, but was feeling pretty good and still got 7 hours of sleep.

Having a hard time this week with the AI protocol. I'll have to start over  Monday night I was having huge cravings for sugar, and especially ice cream. I actually went to the grocery store, in the frozen aisle, looked at them, picked one, put it back, and came back home with an apple and some dates. I changed my mind, I didn't want to binge and feel like crap and fight to come back on track. After 6 months, going back to day 0 was a non-sense. So glad I didn't do it. I thought I was free of cravings, but it seems they can come back anytime...

Also had a coffee on Wednesday. I was feeling wonderful, so I thought only one decaf wouldn't hurt. WRONG!! I felt like crap for the rest of the day. Is it the coffee? Is it something else? Can't tell... And yesterday I had 99% dark chocolate, plus a little this morning. Felt fine after. Seems I can't learn my lesson. I have to be 100% strict!!

I'm down to 150 lbs from 162 at the begining of August. So glad!! Can't wait to be in the 140's again. Even though I lost weight, I've been feeling puffy in the last 2 weeks. Had my period last week, it sure didn't help, and this week I had to much carbs. This has to stop!!! From now on, exit the fruits and all that isn't AI approved. I know I'll feel better if I do.

Did CT everyday this week but today. Will get back tomorrow morning. Still taking 200 mg of Prometrium at night. I'd like to lower to 100 mg to see if I'll sleep as well. By 10 every night, I have to go to sleep, I'm so sleepy, and it takes seconds before I'm gone. How nice. I had an accupunture session Thuesday, and I sleep so good that night, and also woke up refreshed and in such a good mood. Even though I sleep well every night, mornings are difficult until I take my T3 and I have BAB. Still going outside a few minutes as I raise, I'm sure it's helping big time.

So, here's are my new goals for the next weeks:

Sun as I get up: yes

Strict AI: no (had chocolate this am)

No snacking: No

CT: no

Well...It doesn't look good. Time to stop fooling myself and get serious!

Hi K. Sorry you aren't feeling the best.

I'm wondering if upping the t3 will help or if I truly need some t4 too. I am still working on my doc to let me titrate. I did double my dose Sat. and didn't feel any different. I would think it would take more than 1 day though.

I'm wondering if upping the t3 will help or if I truly need some t4 too. I am still working on my doc to let me titrate. Hi V,

I wasn't more hypo, but I was still hypo until I upped the dose. Why would you need some T4 if the problem is conversion from T4 to T3? You'll end up with more T4 not converting in T3. Well, I think.

Hopefully your doc will be willing to help you with that. Tell her that you'll up the dose slowly and as soon as you have any side effect, you'll go back down. I think I read on STTM that if you don't up the dose the symptoms can fire back...

Slept well last night. Will lower the Prometrium tonight and see how I sleep.

Been feeling much better since Monday. Stric AI again. I'm now doing the eliminating diet. Today I had avocado: doesn't look like I have any adverse reaction from them. Next thing I'll add is coconut milk.

Changed my appointment with my functionnal doctor for next week because I'm running out of T3. I had to go back on the dose. Was at 125 mcg, and was having some palpitations. Only had 25 mcg this morning, and another 25 this afternoon.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-10-14T03:53:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-bumpy-road-to-optimal.400/post-58030>

What is an educational consult really all about? It is about life. Your life, in context. What your life in context is really about?

It is about daring greatly, about being in the arena every day you breath.

It is about touching your vulnerability and then unleashing that strength against your own demons to win.

Vulnerability is no weakness. That is a life myth that is profoundly dangerous to a person looking for an Optimal life.

### DarleenMB — 2012-10-14T19:37:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-bumpy-road-to-optimal.400/post-58132>

> **Jack Kruse;59368 wrote:** What is an educational consult really all about? It is about life. Your life, in context. What your life in context is really about?
>
>
>
> It is about daring greatly, about being in the arena every day you breath.
>
>
>
> It is about touching your vulnerability and then unleashing that strength against your own demons to win.
>
>
>
> Vulnerability is no weakness. That is a life myth that is profoundly dangerous to a person looking for an Optimal life.

You know I'm always surprised, although I shouldn't be, at how the Universe always sends you the teacher you need at the moment you need them.

What Jack has written here may sound to some a bit vague and mabye even a little woo-wooey but I can tell you if you read each sentence carefully and let it simply resonate within you, you'll find the truth of what he is saying.

I held still yesterday during my consult even though every fiber of my being wanted to run away and hide. But I didn't flinch. And the reward is I am uncovering truths like mad today. Facing my self and my truths about who I am and who I want to be. sounds crazy but that consult was worth a year's worth of therapy and now I have a road map to guide me through the dark wood.

Had a really great week-end with friends, but had a few things I shouldn't have yesterday for brunch. Was in pain in the evening

Slept good the last two night. Two 2 mL of cream P Saturday night and had a major brain fog for a couple of hour went I got up. Took 1 mL last night, and was feeling much better this morning. Throat is sore, probably because of the food I had yesterday (eggs, cheese, wine).

Natural light as I get up: yes

Strict AI: yes

No snacking: no...ate after dinner

Early dinner: had early dinner, but ate 2 hours prior to go to bed

CT: 45 minutes after BAB. Still having a problem to submerge myself up to the shoulders. Wil try to do another session tonight (did 30 minutes)

Did a 12 minutes work out this morning. I finish work too late tonight and tomorrow, so I did it this morning anyway. It felt really great, even if I'm so out of shape.

### [email redacted] — 2012-10-26T01:52:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-bumpy-road-to-optimal.400/post-60237>

I've been having crazy sugar cravings for a few weeks now. Don't know what's wrong. Can't let go of the fruits. I had some this afternoon, and things started to get worst: had nuts, and then had ice cream!! I don't know why it has been so hard lately to stay on track. I DO know I feel better when I do, but with all the bad sleep these past weeks, it's really difficult. Good news is that it didn't lead to a binge, but I'm definitely not happy about all that crap.
