### PaulaRichards — 2012-04-20T18:57:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-17127>

Yep. A month without binging, and very happy the cravings are becoming weaker. In fact there are days that I haven't thought about what I \*can't\* have!

I'm feeling pretty good & physically "feel" lighter but there is no evidence of that. The scale is hovering around 167-168 lbs and my clothes are not looser. In fact, when I put on dress pants for work my stomach looks bloated. This doesn't usually happen to me unless I eat crap. I hope it's not the CO but I don't think I'm going to change anything right now. I think I'll give it another week or two because this reset is definitely doing the job on the craving & my general sense of well-being is \*good\*!

My food intake for BAB & lunch is basically the same everyday.

According to Fitness Pal my typical breakfast = 64g of protein/91g of fat (1100cal)!!! Not including the BPC!!!

Supper varies but it's usually steak/chicken with lots of fat (the best part), with or without mushrooms dripping in butter! I love this WOE

This is awesome! I didn't battle any cravings last night. That has to be the first Friday night in years that I haven't had to resist cravings. Woke up this morning and it was like I was on auto-pilot, I was barely awake but headed straight to the kitchen to prep a BAB. It was funny, I didn't even feel hungry, but as always, it was darn good!!! ### [email redacted] — 2012-04-22T16:18:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-17711>

> **Jack Kruse;17658 wrote:** Binging will go......if you have the power to stick with what I am telling ya.......its all about the brain gut axis. Its not about your mental weakness of weakness of character. Its inflammation and circadian circuitry breakdown

I could almost cry with relief reading this...I have been struggling so bad with bingeing. I know how I am, so "black and white" "good or bad." I hate using these terms to describe eating habits "I was good today" or "bad today". Just leads to guilt and another reason to binge. I have been on the Leptin Reset for 7 weeks. Lately, I seem to be more obsessed with food. I've been really strict, really keto trying to keep my weight down (I had gained 3 pounds). Then I went on vacation with my family and although I made it through the vacation without gaining, all that pushing it down all week, led to this after vacation melt down. The things I binge on are coconut manna, NUTS, and almond butter. If feel like my diet is so strict, and any little deviation leads to weight gain which makes me miserable. I am praying this leptin reset gives me more flexibility. I don't want to go back to eating gluten, but I do want to be able to have some fruit (in season) and sweet potatoes or other more starchy veggies.

My hormones are all low and I still haven't had TOM since using Hcg last July. I've been on Estradiol for a month and nothing. I also have Hashis and have been taking progenelone to try to get my hormones going. I got triggered by news that a friend is pregnant (I have no children and am 40 and in the last 6 months, all three of my brother's wives gave birth, as well as another friend). I am happy for my friend, but this is her 5th kid and she's my age and obviously fertile and I'm looking at coming to grips with never having a child. I staved off the binge for three days, but totally snapped and ate sugar free chocolate, lara bars, nuts and dried cherries. The result - I have gained ANOTHER three pounds, for total of 6 and I know they won't come off. I haven't been able to lose any weight the last two month. IT's so crazy, because one half of me sees this behavior playing out and is saying "don't do it. one bite and you're done, you're gonna be mad when you gain the weight" and yet off I go...

However, I refuse to be mad at myself and beat myself up.

In fact, I think I will try to not think about it too much and focus on positive.

Thanks for your honest blog. I often feel like I'm the only one who falls off the wagon.

I do have a question though, what "treats" are Leptin Reset friendly?

Maybe if I have something "allowed" I'll do better.

THANKS

### Jack Kruse — 2012-04-22T16:27:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-17718>

you need biofeedback training and you need to stop eating almond butter.......and why you are one estradiol is not making any sense to me......but it is what it is. Your thoughts are driving your adrenal cortex to drink.......and you need to shut that down. Biofeedback would help you immensely. Ask Hazy Jane for help.

READ CT ten right now.........see what it says there about treats..........you'll see my answer.

### [email redacted] — 2012-04-22T16:56:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-17729>

Jennifer, reading you is breaking my heart. Of course it's not about will power, caracter or any weakness!

Your gut is sick. And that's why you can't lose weight. This is telling you somethig is wrong. You already know that your hormones are a mess. There's probably something in your diet wrong too. At this moment, I truly think that you can not expect to lose weight. You have to focuss on healing yourself first. And that might take long... You might gain more weight too. I know you don't want to (who does??), but this is an indication that inflammation is high in your body.

Do you have a good doctor? Did you do labs?

I can totally relate to your story. Been binging and purging for years. Amenorrhea. Doing the yo-yo with my weight. When I finally quit purging, I was still binging and of course gained weight. I was 150 at Christmas. Went down to 147 after a few weeks on the reset, felt off the wagon for 2 weeks, went up to 153. Now I'm at 156. Gained 3 lbs in a month, still on the reset. Very frustrating. It will come off, I know, but first I got to heal that gut I maltreated for years. I stopped beating myself up. Now that I know better, I have to do better.

Just like you, coconut manna, nuts, nut butter triger binges....My doctor says that's because I'm somehow allergic or intolerant to those. I have to avoid them completely. I know it, but yesterday I still bought almonds, thinking I would be fine with having a few. Wrong!!! I can't have a few, I have to have them all!!! I'm eating and know I should stop, but I just go on!! Just like you, I can't help myself. So next time, I just won't buy them....I do have a treat....I have it every day actually. It's CO blended with cinnamon and raw chocolate in powder. I melt the CO just a little, I mix in cinnamon and chocolate, and I freeze it on parchment paper 15 minutes. You have to try it, it is so good. And I don't feel like having more when I have one, I'm full and satisfied.

Journaling also helps me a lot. I know you started one too, Did it help? I think you have to concentrate on healing your gut and your hormones, it will be a lot less frustrating that concentrating on weight. Weight is a symptom of underlying conditions...Be kind to yourself, be patient, and it will come off. Big hug.

Katy

### BJK77 — 2012-04-22T17:00:30+0300

Lately, I seem to be more obsessed with food. I've been really strict, really keto trying to keep my weight down (I had gained 3 pounds). Then I went on vacation with my family and although I made it through the vacation without gaining, all that pushing it down all week, led to this after vacation melt down. The things I binge on are coconut manna, NUTS, and almond butter. If feel like my diet is so strict, and any little deviation leads to weight gain which makes me miserable. I am praying this leptin reset gives me more flexibility. I don't want to go back to eating gluten, but I do want to be able to have some fruit (in season) and sweet potatoes or other more starchy veggies.
>
> My hormones are all low and I still haven't had TOM since using Hcg last July. I've been on Estradiol for a month and nothing. I also have Hashis and have been taking progenelone to try to get my hormones going. I got triggered by news that a friend is pregnant (I have no children and am 40 and in the last 6 months, all three of my brother's wives gave birth, as well as another friend). I am happy for my friend, but this is her 5th kid and she's my age and obviously fertile and I'm looking at coming to grips with never having a child. I staved off the binge for three days, but totally snapped and ate sugar free chocolate, lara bars, nuts and dried cherries. The result - I have gained ANOTHER three pounds, for total of 6 and I know they won't come off. I haven't been able to lose any weight the last two month. IT's so crazy, because one half of me sees this behavior playing out and is saying "don't do it. one bite and you're done, you're gonna be mad when you gain the weight" and yet off I go...
>
> However, I refuse to be mad at myself and beat myself up.
>
> In fact, I think I will try to not think about it too much and focus on positive.
>
> Thanks for your honest blog. I often feel like I'm the only one who falls off the wagon.
>
> I do have a question though, what "treats" are Leptin Reset friendly?
>
> Maybe if I have something "allowed" I'll do better.
>
> THANKS

I'm so sorry you're struggling so much Jennifer. I have my own struggles as I'm sure we all do, but I finally feel like I have come to terms with my past binging. And based on my own experience, I couldn't even handle an occasional "treat." Anytime I tried to add anything into my diet that I viewed as a treat I binged. This definitely included almond butter, coconut manna, starchy veggies like butternut squash, etc - really, anything even remotely sweet/starchy.

When I finally realized that my body just did not have the ability to handle these foods, life became so much easier. Yes, my diet is boring, but I feel like I have more control over my life in general now. I was on GAPS for over a year before doing the leptin rx and I spent the last 6 months of that time consuming nothing even slightly sweet or starchy - no baked goodies with nut flours, no nut butters, sweet squash, carrots, nada! I made the very real decision to be in control of what I would eat and consciously CHOOSE to only consume what would benefit my body. Sure I found myself wanting these things once in awhile, but more than wanting the foods - I wanted the control I felt by being able to say "no" to them.

I've now been on the leptin rx for 12+ weeks and don't even really find myself desiring these foods at all. I do think at times it would be nice to bring them back into my diet just for the sake of variety and to be able to eat what everyone else around me does, but I can honestly say I don't crave them in any way.

Point is: You must make the choice to take control of your eating habits. You can totally kick those cravings/desires to the curb, but you have to give it all up! No small handfuls of nuts or just a spoonful of almond butter if you truly know you won't be able to stop at that small amount. It's hard, but not impossible and you're obviously here because you want optimal, which means you can do this!!

### Jack Kruse — 2012-04-22T17:38:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-17751>

actions fix thoughts.......when you begin to go against your thoughts some amazing things can occur.

### youthfuliz — 2012-04-22T17:59:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-17759>

I think it takes a little while to wean off whatever bad habit, so just keep doing the right thing and you'll find it getting easier. I had carb cravings before too, but now I crave fat.  With fat and protein, the good thing is you don't want to eat nonstop... as soon as you satisfy your craving with a good meal, the craving goes away. With carbs one can behave like an eating machine, which we are not born to be.

I find that as soon as I eat enough at breakfast, I hardly have any cravings during the day. I stopped snacking on day one of leptin reset. Still have cravings sometimes, I guess due to hormones/cycle and I would eat a little more, and eat more fatty stuff to pacify myself. Over time it becomes more and more "normal", as if I adapt to a new way of living, a new habit. Also re-reading the blog posts helps too. The more I understand the "why", the easier it is for me to do the right thing. Don't just discipline yourself into good behavior, nurture yourself into it.

### MamaGrok — 2012-04-22T18:27:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-17769>

Many great words here -

\- Do what you can. You can't make yourself not binge if you have cravings, so use your willpower where it CAN work ... doing the things that make the cravings shut up. That means, don't buy the coconut flesh or nuts (I can't have any of these, either - in fact, the more I meet people who binge on carbs, the more I find none of us can tolerate these); don't buy the dried fruits; don't have "just one bite" of any of these. Force the BAB on every morning, increase it till the cravings die (may take a few months, listen to your body as to how much you may need ... I needed 65g). If you feel you're going to fall into a binge, have a tablespoon of CO first. I mean, you're already breaking the "no snacking" rule with the binge, so do the CO first. Etc.

\- Do not blame yourself for bingeing. It is chemical. Period. The only place for blame is for not doing your best in the areas where you \*can\* succeed (see above). Guilt should be a motivator for doing better next time, not a whip. And NEVER for things you can't control - like bingeing when there are cravings.

> **Jennifer;17782 wrote:** I've been really strict, really keto trying to keep my weight down (I had gained 3 pounds). Then I went on vacation with my family and although I made it through the vacation without gaining, all that pushing it down all week, led to this after vacation melt down. The things I binge on are coconut manna, NUTS, and almond butter. If feel like my diet is so strict, and any little deviation leads to weight gain which makes me miserable. I am praying this leptin reset gives me more flexibility. ...I staved off the binge for three days, but totally snapped and ate sugar free chocolate, lara bars, nuts and dried cherries. The result - I have gained ANOTHER three pounds, for total of 6 and I know they won't come off. I haven't been able to lose any weight the last two month. IT's so crazy, because one half of me sees this behavior playing out and is saying "don't do it. one bite and you're done, you're gonna be mad when you gain the weight" and yet off I go...
>
> However, I refuse to be mad at myself and beat myself up.
>
> In fact, I think I will try to not think about it too much and focus on positive.
>
> Thanks for your honest blog. I often feel like I'm the only one who falls off the wagon.

Please - go to my journey (linked in my signature). Go to March 2011 (if you go to February or January, you'll also get some larabar binge stories, and that's fine too!). Read from there to November 2011. It might take an hour? I have been where you are; I have gained, I have binged, I have tried, I have failed, I have done it all over again. Even on the LR. And then again. And again. And again. But in the end, I win. You will, too.

BTW, I no longer want treats (mostly because they all make me backtrack physically, but also b/c I just don't), but when I do, it might be super duper dark chocolate and one strawberry (not dried!). I can't do anything else yet, really.

### PaulaRichards — 2012-04-22T19:15:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-17789>

Jennifer, if you haven't already read Mamagrok's jouney, I highly suggest you do! Seriously!! I've been VLC on & off for years and no matter how great I felt I always "fell off the wagon" & it always resulted in binge eating. The binges could last just for weekends, but they could turn into weeks and months & on and off for years. I think the latest was during the Christmas holidays when I gained almost 15 lbs! I've only been on the reset since March 19 and am certainly no pro!

I have to say that reading Mamagrok's journal, not only reflected a lot of what has happened in my own life over the years, but made me realize that success is waiting for me (for all of us) & I'm not going to postpone the meeting any longer!

There were days since starting the reset that the cravings were severe & I could have riped the face of anyone who looked at me the wrong way because I was so irritable!!! I reached for the CO with cocoa powder & it got me through some tough days. Yes, I broke the "No Snacking" rule but I didn't eat crap that might have resulted in a binge on crap food. Perhaps there will be more cravings to beat on my path to success but those "cheats" with the CO managed to get me to now being able to follow the rules without the killer cravings!

### [email redacted] — 2012-04-22T20:30:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-17818>

Thank you ALL for the kind words and support. I have Mamagrok's page bookmarked for reading next (I'm babysitting my niece right now!). It's amazing to me this energy build up that I feel that just has to go somewhere. Pushing it down or ignoring doesn't work. Although I am sure there must be a way to discharge it without eating (biofeedback as Dr K suggested). Amazingly enough, I don't break the "no snacking" rule. It's more when I start eating, I don't stop. I do notice that the dark chocolate, whether it be 99% mixed with CO or this 85% I just tried does not make me binge. The "sugar free" stuff does. Dr K says pitch the almond butter. And read CT 10, which I did. And moderation is definetly not a place I hang out...LOL...I'm usually swinging from one extreme to the next. I love this line from CT 10- "We do not need an intelligent mind that speaks, but a patient heart that listens. Make your choices and you create your life." And what Dr K added here "actions fix thoughts.......when you begin to go against your thoughts some amazing things can occur."

One part of my brain absolutely believes in the Leptin Reset but this other part likes to talk s@#$.

I've got a lot of sorting out to do on physical and emotional level. I have been ignoring that and focusing on the bathroom scale.

And I def am not getting the right medical help here. My gyn has made it pretty clear she is only interested in treating my reproductive organs, hence the estradiol to fix my low estrogen (treating the symptom not the disease). I have to decide between going back to this NP that does BHRT (she knows her female hormones but I don't really trust her in the thyroid area), an endocrinologist or a long distance consult.

So much of this is tied to some idea that I must never make any mistakes (black and white thinking)...It helps to hear others fall and get back up! And as you say, Paulalynn, success is waiting...

much gratitude!

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-18044>

I read somewhere that success is not a linear path. That's for sure!!! I was feeling beyond good yesterday. As I was finishing supper I decided to have some CO Manna with 100% cocoa powder & that would have been fine, had I left it at that! But, I decided to have a square of dark chocolate too, not usually a problem for me either. I wasn't battling any particular screaming craving at the time but row by row I finished the bar. Not a binge in my books, but a setback on the reset I'm sure.

Note to self---No more bars of \*any\* chocolate coming into the house again! I feel like crap & was in bed, not being able to fall asleep until sometime after 2:00am. It was a hard way to learn a lesson BUT lesson learned; as I look with very tired eyes at a very bloated belly

I dusted myself off & ate a BAB!

I need a post-it note with "actions fix thoughts.......when you begin to go against your thoughts some amazing things can occur" stuck to my forehead!

### PaulaRichards — 2012-04-29T23:20:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-19937>

Thanks Katy! I don't usually have bloating when I eat well (low carb)! This is unusual. I have been eating lots of butter & lots of CO and CO butter/manna! I haven't had nuts in a couple of weeks.

I ate the damn popcorn today. I wasn't even craving it. I went to the theatre completely "full" from the BAB. I was at the counter buying our tickets & the word "popcorn" came out of my big mouth...only to be followed by the word "large"! WTF!!!!!!!!!! Then while eating it I consciously thought "why are you eating this", it didn't even taste delicious, I didn't "love" it like I used to but I still ate most of it! ### MamaGrok — 2012-04-30T01:48:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-19956>

Eat coconut oil. Stat. Like as much as you know you can eat without diarrhea. Or have diarrhea - it's not the end of the world!

Popcorn isn't the worst thing you could have, and it's not a binge, but as you know, it leads to cravings, even if nothing else. Don't worry about it, move past, eat a BIG meal for your next meal. If you're about to break down and eat something else you know will drive the cravings, eat a full meal instead. If you can't cook one fast enough to prevent a binge, drive to the nearest burger place and order a double and throw away the bun before you can even think about it. EAT EAT EAT ! Doesn't matter if it's not time to eat. For you, and for me, preventing a binge is more important than timing rules on a day where bingeing is a threat. Timing being off once will not set you back nearly as far as a binge.

If you end up having something else that drives the craving, don't give up; go back and re-read and re-do everything else I just said.

That's what I'd do, anyway! You can do this!

After a long long time, I've determined that for me, the bloating is not the CAUSE of the irritability, but they both seemed to be caused by the same thing, which is of course whatever I ate that I can't tolerate, but I'm not sure what the entire mechanism is from food to reactions.

### PaulaRichards — 2012-05-05T17:00:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-21610>

Where have the days gone? I didn't "fall of the wagon" but it's been a busy few days. I had the appt with the naturopath & then of course I have to research \*everything\* someone tells me or something I read! So, as expected, the naturopath & I had differing opinions on diet. It was 1.5 hour appt with her asking lots of questions. She said it "sounds" like I'm healthy! She works with a herbalist and wants to get me some individualized concoction. Mainly for sleep, liver & weight loss. I thought \*whatever\*, it can't hurt I guess, so I'll get those in a couple of weeks. She thinks I may have a dairy sensitivity, which I'm going to investigate because I burp, have occasional heartburn/indigestion and generally feel bloated a lot of the time. She did suggest I add magnesium, so I re-read the doc's blog and spent a couple days researching the best kind but of course had to order from the US because it's not available in Canada. I went with the Magnesium from Jigsaw Health. I hope it is a good one. I know Jack is now recommending the Neuro-Mag with L-Threonate but I have 4 bottles of the other one on it's way. In the meantime I picked up the Natural Calm Magnesium, which I read it good. If it helps with sleep and loosens the BM's I'll be happy!

I've made an appt with my regular doc and will hopefully get lots of bloodwork ordered. That will be in a few weeks time.

I eliminated dairy this morning so BAB was 3 eggs & bacon; no whey protein, which added a nice bit of protein. I'm hoping it's not going to negatively impact hunger and cravings. No HWC in coffee. Now BPC was CO, MCT, drop of stevia, coconut cream and a little cocoa.

I have a follow-up with the naturopath although I really don't know why. She wants to look at my diet. When I mentioned the use of CO she said she likes CO and it's ok to replace other fats with it but not to add it! Too much saturated fat!!!! WHATEVER! I feel like I need to help her and bring her some of Dr. K's info. She's a lovely girl, seems so full of life. She's 37 and can barely walk from rheumatoid arthritis. She's now going to try a juicing diet for 6 months to see if that'll help her. While we were talking about me and the amount of protein I eat & how fruit sugars are good for you  She said we shouldn't be eating to much protein because it leads to inflamation. This girl really needs Jack Kruse in her life! I'm going to compile some info for her; hopefully enough that will inspire her to look into all this!

It's been less that 2 hours since BAB but I feel like I'm getting hungry! The whey protein added 25g to my BAB and it's filled the void for most of the day most times. I already eat eggs and sometimes can eat 1/2 lb of bacon but I always had the whey with it. My BAB's were close to 65g of protein. My brand has carbs @ 2g, fiber 1g, and 0g of sugar. Anyway, why am I even talking about it since it's dairy!!!!

### DarleenMB — 2012-05-05T19:58:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-21640>

Oh my dear I hear you on the popcorn. (am just catching up from the last few days)

As to your Naturopath, sounds like she needs to pay YOU.

OK I'm looking at your age on your post and wondering if you have even thought about perimenopause and/or menopause being the root of your issues? And no crying you're not old enough yet. I now know I went through all that starting in my LATE 20'S. Stress will do that to you.

If I haven't recommended TS Wiley's "Sex, Lies & Menopause" or any of Dr. John R. Lee's books (starting with "What your Doctor may not tell you about menopause") then I do so now.

I'm 65 and here's the reader's digest version of my hormonal life. Got an IUD when I was 27 and my periods went insane. Where before they had been a lovely normal 3 days modest flow tapering away to nothing over the next 2 days they became raging torrents for 24 hours with lots of cramping. It got worse. MUCH worse.

And along with the worsening flood came sleep disturbances. They started with waking up in the middle of the night for 15 or 20 minutes every few months. Then it was 30 minutes to an hour every few weeks. Then every week. I finally started nuking a cup of milk to try and get back to sleep or playing solitaire on the computer until I got drowsy.

My periods were so bad that I would go through an entire box of 30 industrial strength tampons in 24 hours. They got worse. I had to add a pad. I would wake up every hour on the hour (because the flood would ALWAYS start at bedtime) to change things. I had tampons stashed EVERYWHERE. And sleep? Forget it.

15 years ago I finally got my OB/GYN to listen to me and he did an ultrasound whereupon he informed me I had a BIG uterus. Nuh uh .. I was told it was SMALL. Not anymore. His first thought, of course, was cancer but he refused to do anything else until I had a D&C. It was done Friday morning and by Friday NIGHT I was bleeding. AGAIN. Of course I was frightened but the nurse told me not to worry (hahahaha why do they always say that) and if it got worse (it was a minor flood so I guess that was OK) to call.

Monday I called and told the doc to "take the damned thing out" because I had finally realized I had fibroids and THEY were what had been bleeding me dry for years. Actually I a kilo's worth of the damned things. Talk about estrogen dominance.

ANYWAY I really do have a point here. For the next year I LITERALLY only slept for 2 hours every single night. I cannot believe I survived all that. Someone I "met" on the internet told me about Lee's book which I finally got after she told me the second time. THAT's when I started using OTC progesterone cream. The first night I used it I slept 8 hours.

I stumbled along for the last 14 years using the OTC stuff but never feeling quite right. The creeping brain fog became a permanent state to the point I began to wonder if I had had a series of mini-strokes because I could NOT think, couldn't remember anything, didn't even know what day it was most of the time. Until last year when, through a series of happenstance, I stumbled onto the Wiley Protocol. I read the book, found a physician 400 miles away (the closest to me) and went on BHRT following a natural rhythm (the above Wiley Protocol).

It has CHANGED. MY. LIFE. The first few months I was on the protocol were a period of adjustment. But I felt at times as if I could step out the door and just fly away I felt SO DARNED GOOD.

I'm not saying it has cured all my ills. Jack's leptin Rx is helping with that as is the keto/paleo diet. It's getting better. I'm now sleeping like a rock for a solid 8 hours (unless my darling cats wake me too early. /sarcasm).

I have a long ways to go yet but the BHRT applied as my body once produced it and in the amounts it produced when I was a healthy 20 year old have given me back SO much. I hope this very long post and all this TMI I've now put out in public are a help to you and others I see suffering.

HORMONES. I now know first hand how much they do and control. And when you lose them, as I did, the results are devastating. Put them back the way nature intended and you're good to go. It's pretty basic. Mother Nature can be a real bitch and one thing she is adamant about is this ... reproduce or die. If your brain senses you've lost your ability to reproduce it starts shutting the machinery down. Put those hormones back and fool your brain and everything fires back up again.

sure HTH.

### PaulaRichards — 2012-05-05T23:51:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-21687>

I read Darleen's post 2 hours ago but just stopped crying at the thought of menopause!!! Just kidding Darleen...your sarcastic humor rubs off on me!

Seriously, thank you for sharing that!!! I mean it. That is quite a history!

My mother was 37-38 when she hit menopause. The thought has crossed my mind. Especially the last couple of months. There have been times that I have never been so \*hot\* in all my life. The other morning I was getting ready for work & I could feel the heat building inside me. I was sweaty and couldn't wait to get out into the cold. I live in Eastern Canada and it is by no means warm here yet but I left the house with a short sleve blouse and drove 30 min to work & it was about 40 degrees. It has happened at the grocery store; I'm peeling off layers & can't get out of the store fast enough. Yes, I'm trying to cold-adapt but I don't think this is considered "a change in sweating patterns" but what do I know?  I haven't had this happen at night, thankfully. There are times when I'll wake up and I have to change my clothes because they're so damp/wet but I never feel \*hot\* like I need to open a window.

The part of your story that I strongly identify with is the brain fog, which most times I would caulk up to carbs or binges but over the last few years it has been getting worse. I've had family member tell stories about things we did and I have no recollection of it and I mean NO memory of it. It's not like I temporarily forgot about it...I literally don't remember. There are lots of times when I don't know what month it is, and I don't mean I'm off by a few days. Days when I ask myself a number of times "what day is it" and an hour later I'm wondering again! Anyway, you KNOW what I'm talking about!!! I have never questioned that this might actually be linked to menopause. And I have never heard of the books you mentioned!

Soon as I finish this post I am going to see if there is an e-reader version of Wiley's book. I'm a "need to know NOW" type of person!!! So I guess hormone testing is a must!!!

Again Darleen, a big Thank You for sharing this!!!

### PaulaRichards — 2012-05-15T17:41:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-24233>

I don’t know what to cut out! Coconut oil, coconut manna and the MCT oil are the only new addition to my diet since I started the leptin reset. I’m thinking \*something\* must be causing my weight to increase & is preventing me from losing. I almost feel heavier than the scale is even showing. I don’t want to cut out the CO but I’m at a loss! This WOE is not new to me. The BAB and No Snacking rule are new but nothing else about it is.

In all seriousness, this is the most frustrated I’ve ever felt about my weight. I fully recognize and accept the fact that I don’t lose weight easily. My first attempt at low-carb, years ago, was my last “easy” weight loss. Ever since it’s been difficult and I get that but I’ve never gained or been this bloated on any of my attempts at low carb. I’ve grown out of 3 pairs of dress pants for work and my so-called “fat pants” are snug. My frustration level is showing. I’m impatient with my 12 yr old son who is such an awesome guy. Meanwhile he’s apologizing for being slow at homework, etc., & probably feeling bad about himself because of \*my\* mood! I need to get a grip!

Yesterday was an “URGHHHH” type of day. Aside from the above mentioned frustrations I had to take DS to the doctor because his foot looked infected. He got a mat burn from judo last week and didn’t mention it to me for a couple of days so by this time it already looked “not good” & we started on the vitamin c & bathing it in Epson salts and coating it in tea tree oil but it was getting worse. Dr. said he needed antibiotics because it looked like cellulitis. IDK!

I got home & hit the self-destruction button. Put some frozen fries in the oven, went to bathroom and cried because I \*knew\* what I was doing! Then told myself NO, don’t eat them. Made a protein shake. Then loaded the fries up with cheddar and ate them anyway. A while later when I was getting ready to go to the drug store my significant other (SO?) came home and knew there was something wrong when he looked at me and I started crying, my frustration level was at a 10. I felt rage inside of me. Thankfully he’s very supportive (may secretly think I’m crazy but he hides it pretty well J).

Went to pick up the meds and figured I’d go get a sundae, since WTH! I probably would have but felt so bloated and something inside me didn’t want to add sugar to the mess and didn’t want to have to label this a binge so I didn’t! Knowing I’d have to come here and re-write the binge-free date has a positive effect.

I’m up to 172 lbs this morning. For the most part my weight is, and has always been from my belly button to my ankles (except when I weighed 200 lbs and it was everywhere). Now it feels like it’s moving up & I’m getting the spare tire above my waist. I’ve never had this.

Had BAB breakfast this morning, contemplated restricting calories for a brief moment of desperation but I know I can’t.

Just came back from coffee break, where I always eat 2 eggs with lots of butter. Today…1 egg with a tea biscuit…a TEA BISCUIT! Now enter the mental chatter…WTH…gotta start leptin reset from day 1 tomorrow…what else do you want to eat today??????

Sanity will prevail because I just don’t want to fight the sugar demon again. I don’t feel like battling the binge monster again either. The tea biscuit will turn to sugar of course but I can’t fuel the fire with something that actually tastes good (ice cream) & will surely lead to weeks of madness!

### MamaGrok — 2012-05-15T17:55:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-24238>

Alright, I definitely don't have all the answers, but a few thoughts...

\- probably lost a week or so with the tea biscuit, but not everything

\- EAT YOUR BREAKFAST!!!

\- Before that, go eat a big fat protein & fat meal right now!

\- It's okay. Don't blame yourself. Your weight, your cravings, and your mood are consequences of your physical status, not a sign that you suck.

\- It's probably the manna. Manna makes me bloat & makes me crave. CO does not. I think it's the coconut flesh that is the problem; oil doesn't seem to do it. I've seen a lot of other crave-rs have problems with coconut flesh and all things made from it, like manna, milk, etc.

\- Someone who knows your whole history needs to help you figure out what's causing the gain. I think there will be a clear answer to someone who knows the history and also has a broad knowledge of health (like Dr. Kruse), whether it's food intolerances, hormone imbalance, difficulty staying on plan (so needing a craving and/or emotional plan of attack), or something else.

\- Vit C megadosing for cellulitis! May take 20 or more gm per day. Topical CO & garlic, & internal CO & garlic, too.

\- Hang in there!!!

### PaulaRichards — 2012-05-15T19:46:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-24277>

Yup...will have to go to Wendy's for a 1/2 pound bacon cheeseburger-no bun. It's the only place around where I work.

That's after I can put an end to this teary-eyed self-created drama I've created for myself! I don’t know what opened the floodgates today. I was reading other journals when I could feel the tears starting. Then I came back and read MamaG’s advise and started crying again! Not because of the advise, of course!

Thanks MamaGrok! You always seem to be in the right place at the right time, offering words of wisdom! I’ll stick to CO and forget about the manna and the milk and see what happens. Something has to fall into place or I’ll be shopping for bigger pants!

Ok…have regained composure and can reopen my office door…and to think people think I’m “with it”…if they only knew..lol!

### DarleenMB — 2012-05-15T20:12:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-24294>

Even the tiniest bit of wheat can set a person off on a binge. It is THAT addictive. The longer I stay off of it the less concerned I am with ever eating anything with wheat in it again. It isn't the sugar so much as it is the wheat.

And I've been right where you are. So I understand and empathize with your frustration.

When I got off the Jenny diet and was finally able to get into regular sized clothes again I moved like I was made of glass I was SO afraid of gaining the weight back. Of course, it came back, slowly, but it came back.

I tried doing HcG but it took about 8 months to drop 20 pounds while starving on 500 calories a day. I gave up. I was told everywhere I went to EAT more. I did. I gained. I was trying to do low carb but was still eating wheat which didn't help. Eat more fat. I did. I gained. I am now 14 pounds away from my highest weight. The one that caused my meltdown and subsequent trip to the Jenny diet.

I have now surrendered to the damage I have wreaked on my body. I no longer am trying to make it do what I want it to do. I am giving it the best nutrition I can and allowing it to heal. It will drop the weight when that moment is achieved.

What I'm trying to say here is maybe it's best to quit worrying about the scale and about what size you're wearing.

I trust Jack and his protocols. I mean let's face it ... he has walked the walk. Anyone who loses 133 pounds in 11 months and did so while eating a healthy diet with plenty of calories has GOT to be on to something, right?

Do you trust him? Do you trust your body to do what it needs to do?

If I have learned NOTHING else in the last couple of years it is that I DO NOT CONTROL MY BODY no matter what I think or what the CW says. It is a marvelous machine that knows how to regulate itself without MY interference.

It isn't easy to let go but it just might be the best thing you can do. Trust. Let go.

### vkiernan — 2012-05-16T20:58:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-24662>

You might also have Candida. If so, you will never get rid of those damn cravings until you rid yourself of it. Just ask me, I know. It sucks. I'm not sure I am over it yet. I need to be tested again. Good luck. And yes, you sound very hormonal. Keep CT'ing if that is what you are doing. Got to get rid of the crap somehow. We aren't emotional naturally. Something causes it or we would be that way everyday and we are not. Just have lots of stuff to fix. Hang in there. Are you using silymarin and DIM to detox your liver and convert bad estrogen to good?

### lisaeh3 — 2012-05-21T03:57:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-25868>

Paula, thank you so much for posting a journal. It is great that you are so humble to post your setbacks in print. I look at it as stepping stones to a new u! Honestly, I have been horrible the last 2 weeks and have been having cheats with a drink of beer, eating my daughter's birthday cake, cookies at my son's Pre-k graduation party. Ugh! I am so disappointed in myself. I feel like total crap the next day. The only difference in the last 2 weeks is that I have been making a BAB shake, which includes 3 or 4 eggs with whipping cream or halfnhalf. I guess I was getting lazy and not cooking my breakfast, but I thought I was getting enough protein from the eggs. Maybe the dairy is messing with me.

Good luck to you on your journey. Also, congrats on finding a pharmacy that will do hormone testing. I was able to fax my bloodwork to a compounding pharmacy and I am picking up my hormones tomorrow. I can't wait to feel like a 25 year old. Watch out husband here I come! LOL!!!

Lisa

Thanks Lisa! Most days I think of it as the road to a new me too!!! I was a little apprehensive about the journal but it’s become a way to track how I feel and what’s happening with the food I eat. Not to mention the advice from others out there is awesome! I don’t know your history or if you have trouble with binging but I know for me, I can’t have “just one bite”. I totally know what you mean by feeling like crap the next day, it’s not necessarily an emotional feeling (although disappointment is part of it), it is more like I literally feel like I have a hangover!

Take one day at a time. The BAB is so key to quieting the cravings. SO IMPORTANT! Your shake sounds yummy but not nearly enough protein. I had to up my BAB protein to 65 grams for a while in order to conquer the cravings. But each day got easier, with a couple of setbacks in there! If you haven’t read MamaGrok’s journal I highly recommend it! She’s been through it all! Something else that helped me is tracking the Binge-free date. I don’t want to break the streak and start over, although believe me the thought still enters my mind on occasion!

Good luck with the hormones. “Watch out husband here I come”…too funny

### PaulaRichards — 2012-05-21T17:27:31+0300

Feeling upbeat today. Cut back on the mag a bit last night, but didn't fall asleep as easily. Oh well, I can't be strapped to the toilet everyday!

I've basically been down to two meals per day. BAB and then I'm not hungry anymore until late afternoon so that brings me to suppertime. ### PaulaRichards — 2012-05-22T23:06:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-26485>

It feels like a Monday...Blah! 4 day weeks always seem longer than the norm for me. Anyway...Pretty sure I've confirmed I have an issue with cheese. After staying away from all things dairy for the better part of a week and having some of the bloating go away I decided to have a piece of "primal cheesecake" I had frozen since Easter weekend. It's an all nut crust, obviously a ton of cream cheese, sour cream, eggs, vanilla, couple drops of stevia. Tangy but delicious! So in the name of science (yeah, right!) I figured I'd test my dairy hypothesis and eat the darn piece. No immediate issues at the time. it wasn't until about lunch time today that I noticed a somewhat familiar "sort of heartburn" feeling. Yup, been having it all afternoon and as the day progresses I feel like my pants are getting tighter. I'm not going to complain, heck it was self-induced, but I'll now refrain from the dairy.

Went to the Well Women's Clinic for the yearly check. Mentioned my new delights like hot flashes and bloating but low & behold..."nope you don't need your hormones checked, as long as you're still having a cycle, any type of cycle, your hormones are fine"! I don't even know why I asked her, I guess I was in a mood! A young female doctor. Ya think they might have a clue? WHATEVER. I already have my saliva testing kit and am just waiting for day 19 to get the sample. My family doc has always been awesome. He tested my hormones years ago but at the time I didn't know any of this stuff so when he told me everything was fine I didn't question it or even ask for a copy of the results. No real point, I'm just rambling. Now, what's to eat...

### PaulaRichards — 2012-05-24T21:29:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-27129>

Got home late yesterday, wasn’t really hungry but also didn’t have anything thawed for supper, and to be honest, just didn’t feel like cooking, etc, etc,. Made a protein shake and added some MCT oil. It did the job but I was quite hungry getting up this morning. I’m also slightly congested this morning; thinking it was the whey??? No “extra” bloating though and no heartburn-ish feeling like I had with the cheese.

Not as tired as I thought I would be today since I stayed up about an hour past my usual bedtime; had to see who won American Idol!

It seems like I’m constantly researching something (wish I could get paid for it!) and although this wasn’t relevant in regard to what I was looking for I had to share with those who are reading this…The examples made me laugh...still chuckling!

The general adaptation theory is based on the research of Hans Selye, MD. Seyle defines stress as “the non-specific response of the body to any demand made upon it”. He identified three distinct stages of stress;

• 1 – Stage 1: Alarm Reaction –> this is the initial “fight or flight” response to stress where the body is prepared for physical activity – cortisol increases and adrenalin is released (_this is what you feel when your teenager starts a sentance by saying “now mom, promise you won’t get mad…..”

• 2 – Stage 2: Stage of Resistance –> if stress is persistent during this phase, the body adapts to the stressors and attempts to reduce the effect of the stressor and maintain homeostasis or balance (_this is your husband handing you a glass of wine and telling you to put down the steak knife & take a slow deep breath_)

• 3 – State 3: Stage of Exhaustion –> with prolonged stress the body’ resistance decreases – decreased immune system, adrenal fatigue (this is how many mother’s feel after teaching all 4 of her teenager how to drive)_

### lisaeh3 — 2012-05-24T23:22:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-27169>

Paulalynn,

In regard to your protein shake, use eggs instead of whey(providing that you can eat them). I have a shake at dinner time when I am not that hungry or rushed for time. Ingredients: 3/4 eggs, cinnamon, hct oil, HWC/half/half or coconut milk for you. I have found that it satisfies me till breakfast. Also, I have had no cheats since the last time I posted. Have gone back to heavy protein for breakfast! Works everytime! tks MamaGok

Fantastic to hear that your are doing good as well. Frustrating that the new doc was clueless. My gyn was too, I got lucky and was able to talk to her nurse instead(found out she was taking BHRT). I told her that my hormones were in the trash due to my labs and she referred me to her pharmacy. So happy!!!! La La La La La!!!!

Also, good news with weight loss. I have been on BHRT since Monday and down 2 pounds. I know it is not much but I have been stuck on 141 since March. I am also losing inches around the middle. I thought I was stuck with the loose skin(fat stomach) due to having 3 kids within 4 years. My mood and energy has improved as well. So ladies please be proactive and get your hormones checked and then convince your doctor to use a compounding pharmacy. It is totally worth it!!

In regard to your research, I believe that I have been in the last phase for numerous years. I was living on Starbucks to function. I am so much better now. Also, my sleep is improving with my new hormones. I have used Flexeril for sleep/muscle issues which I have been taking since I was 20. I am not taking it now, but have quit many times but end up needing it again because of migraines/pain. Since I am on my new hormones I am getting tired at night and sleeping sound with no meds. Swweet!!

I am also a researcher! I would be a millionaire if I was getting paid!

I have not used Maca for hormones but for adrenals. I did not have good luck with it because it made me feel jittery. I did use Chasteberry prior to my hysterectomy for hormone regulation. Did an awesome job until my adhesions from my endometriosis took over. Others on this forum are using DIM. Chasterberry is used to even out your estrogen/progesterone. Also, dandelion root is also good to take because it flushes out excess estrogen. I had great luck with it when I was trying to get pregnant. Thank you for reminding me about dandelion root. I need to buy some to help me when I am CTing.

Have a good night! Time to take my hormones and off to bed.

### PaulaRichards — 2012-05-25T17:49:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-27349>

11:40 am – I am hungry. I haven’t been hungry at this time of day for quite a while. It was the usual morning with one exception; I had BPC with my breakfast instead of my drive to work. My thinking being, by the time I drive to work it’s already been about 45 minutes since I’ve eaten breakfast. Seems like I’m snacking. So I thought I’d combine it with BAB.

7:00 am – BAB – 3 fried eggs & Â½ lb of bacon (I know! How can I be hungry!) & BPC

10:30 am – 2 boiled eggs with butter

…and I’m hungry ?

I’m having thoughts about the “tastes awesome right now but makes me feel like crap for a week” kind of food! Not good. I may have to eat again soon. That means a Wendy’s fast food burger (no bun of course) since I’m at work. I have a huge t-bone defrosted for supper but don’t think it would be a good idea to wait that long today.

### MamaGrok — 2012-05-25T18:06:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-27351>

Your BAB wasn't really all that big in that protein arena. Bacon is, what, 50% fat or more? You \*might\* have gotten in 49g of protein.

JMO, FWIW, but I'd go ahead and eat, as big and fatty and protein-y a meal as you can stand, for now. Don't worry about how long it's been since the last meal, b/c as we both know, if you get too crave-y, you're going to blow the LR "rules" in a much worse way. Blowing the timing once doesn't set your progress back days or weeks; binges do.

Awe…I am pleasantly satisfied! Had a greasy Â½ lb double burger with bacon. So good. I needed that!

> **MamaGrok;27449 wrote: JMO, FWIW, but I'd go ahead and eat, as big and fatty and protein-y a meal as you can stand, for now. Don't worry about how long it's been since the last meal, b/c as we both know, if you get too crave-y, you're going to blow the LR "rules" in a much worse way. Blowing the timing once doesn't set your progress back days or weeks; binges do.
>
>
> You’re right MamaGrok, I was starting to fantasize about my favorite binge foods; that’s scary! Seriously, does it every go away?? So far, I’ve been able to deal with the occasional craving but do they ever “stop”? Did you ever come to place that you can see one of your favorites and not even “think” about eating it, even for a moment??
>
>
>
> MamaGrok;27449 wrote:** Your BAB wasn't really all that big in that protein arena. Bacon is, what, 50% fat or more? You \*might\* have gotten in 49g of protein.

I’m going to have to add something else to my breakfast. The BAB was perfect when I had the protein shake with the Â½ lb of bacon and 3 eggs!

A while back I did plug 200 grams of bacon into myfitnesspal.com and it told be 77grams of protein and I didn’t bother questioning the fact. So crap, it’s only 30grams. That explains the hunger!

I can’t imagine eating a whole pound, although I do love it! Three eggs is my max, although I could experiment with making a shake with some eggs to add to the BAB! Quelsen’s shake sounds awesome but without the HWC or coconut milk?? I haven’t found a sausage yet that I’m ok with. There’s always something in it that gives me heartburn & brings on craving for something sweet.

### MamaGrok — 2012-05-25T21:21:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-27414>

> **paulalynn;27491 wrote:** Seriously, does it every go away?? So far, I’ve been able to deal with the occasional craving but do they ever “stop”? Did you ever come to place that you can see one of your favorites and not even “think” about eating it, even for a moment??

Yes. Absolutely yes. For me, it happened when I increased the protein to 65g and kept it steady there, day in and day out. No compromises. Top food priority.

I do think that at this point, if I dropped my breakfast down to like 25g, or dropped fat, or started eating coconut, dairy, any grain, or nuts again, that I'd have a problem ... soon. But as long as I keep the BAB steady and don't indulge in those binge-y foods, I'm solid.

I do hope that one day, I can drop the BAB to maybe 30, and have some coconut milk & butter without any issues!

### DarleenMB — 2012-05-25T21:31:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-27417>

PaulaLynn if you're doing protein shakes you HAVE TO PUT FAT IN THEM. Fat slows down the digestion otherwise all that protein just blasts through your tummy. I use Thai Kitchen canned coconut milk and usually put about 4 TBS in a shake. Get's all those good MCT's in there. Here's another book recommendation for you (cause I know you don't have anything else to do, right?) [Eades's 6 Week Cure](http://http://amzn.to/Kgy6Je)

And I don't know how you all can eat so much at breakfast. After 7 months of trying I still cannot manage more than 40g. And if I add lots of fat I just fill up faster. Maybe it's my age. Now put an 8-10 oz steak in front of me for dinner and I can demolish it, no problem-o.

### MamaGrok — 2012-05-25T21:47:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-27418>

Because of my cravings, I prioritize the 50g protein over fat. I still get lots of fat, but if fat was crowding out the protein, in my circumstance, I'd lower the fat enough to get more protein in.

I wonder if you ate a bit less at dinner for a couple nights, maybe going to be just a wee bit hungry just 2-3 nights, if you'd be hungry for a bigger breakfast? Or maybe you've tried that, in which case, ignore me.

### DarleenMB — 2012-05-26T00:42:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-27481>

LOL ...nah, I'd never ignore you or anyone else here. Disagree occasionally, but never ignore.

To further clarify ... I'm usually STARVING in the morning. Some mornings I have been so hungry I could have eaten everything raw if I'd had to just to get it down my gullet and to shut my stomach up. I really don't have cravings so much, just every-now-and-then lapses of good judgement when the 7 year old takes over. she SO loves hot buttered popcorn.

I had a 60g protein shake this morning. It was passable but my stomach lmk that it was still too much.

### PaulaRichards — 2012-05-26T00:57:07+0300

It would be nice if you could eventually have some coconut. Will you just "try it" some day or are you waiting until your enery levels are where you want them, etc.?

Ok. Well it is encouraging to know that the "thoughts" do go away. I'll have to look back and see if I had any cravings when I was easily having 70 grams of protein for BAB. Although that included the protein shake with the bacon & eggs and I haven't figured out if I have a problem with the whey. I haven't had whey, dairy or coconut in about a week (except the one night I needed a quick dinner one night) and about 4 pounds of bloating have disappeared.

### PaulaRichards — 2012-05-26T01:02:43+0300

lol...I'm a little surprised I can eat so much at breakfast too but I have NO problem with it and the more fat the more I enjoy it! ### MamaGrok — 2012-05-26T16:09:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-27622>

I think when the bloating is no longer a persistent problem, i will consider re-visiting coconut.

### PaulaRichards — 2012-05-27T17:35:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-27848>

Got through yesterday while flirting with the idea of cheating, probably inspired by the protein shake I added to BAB. After Thursday's hunger and cravings I had to up the BAB again and the options were limited so it was:

3 fried eggs in CO, 1/2 lb of bacon, & 25 gr protein shake = about 75 gram BAB. It was so good and so easy to add the shake to the BAB but it probably is to blame for the slightly intrusive food thoughts.

Today's BAB was the first one I couldn't quite finish. Knowing I need at least 65 grams of protein to keep the cravings at bay I had:

3 eggs fried in CO, 1/2 lb of bacon, 3 mini burgers = 70 grams. Couldn't finish the last bite of burger but did drink a coffee with CO.

I really don't want any thoughts of binging today because I wonder how many mental fights I'm going to win! BAB is starting to settle and I'm starting to feel FULL! That should help.

### PaulaRichards — 2012-05-28T18:49:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-28050>

After yesterday's large BAB I wasn't hungry all day and had no cravings, none! I wasn't hungry at suppertime but wanted something good so I cooked a steak with lots of mushroom cooked in CO & butter.

7:00 am - Today's BAB was the typical 3 eggs & 1/2 lb of bacon & coffee with CO. So barely 50 gr of protein.

9:00 am - I wasn't hungry but had myself convinced I would be eating a tea biscuit at coffee break. I now KNOW I have to EAT (can't include protein shake = cravings later) 65 grams protein for breakfast!!!

Thankfully there were no tea biscuits today so I ate the usual 2 eggs with butter!!! Whew, got lucky there!!!

Was tired all day today. Slept very little last night and for no more than an hour at a time. I don't know what it was. I ran out of the magnesium I've been taking before bedtime so last night was the first night without it, coincidence?? I made sure I picked up some more today.

### PaulaRichards — 2012-05-29T18:55:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-28196>

This morning was saliva collection time and I really hadn't anticipated any problems. Wrong. Apparently I have a bit of a dry mouth early in the morning. Since it needs to be collected within an hour of waking (per my directions), I had to give up and eat breakfast. So, not only did I only get 2 ml when I needed to get 7 ml, I was quite late for breakfast and am hungry now for lunch. Used EFT prior to going to coffee break this morning because I knew they'd have those homemade tea biscuits. I didn't eat one. Yah for me! Have to go get a greasy burger now though, I'm hungry!!!

Ended up getting distracted and actually forgot about having lunch. I was on the way home from work and realized I wasn't that hungry. Ate a chicken thigh and square of 85% chocolate when I got home.

### PaulaRichards — 2012-05-31T16:32:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-28575>

Diet is still good. Haven't been that hungry at suppertime but am quite hungry for BAB. A

Finally managed to get the suitable amount for the saliva testing. That was a task! I can't imagine doing the month long testing. It'll probably be a couple of weeks for the results and I'm looking forward to it.

I was still having the occasional burning sensation in my throat, even after omitting dairy and CO products so I added HCL a couple of days ago. I really can't imagine being sensitive to anything else!

### PaulaRichards — 2012-06-01T18:21:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-28883>

Still ticked off about wasting 1.5 hours of my life in a follow-up appt with the naturopath. My fault for not just canceling in the first place because I already knew we weren’t on the same page (or even the same book!) when it comes to food! But I did have some other questions.

Why am I even wasting time typing this??? Apparently I can’t let it GO! SO, of course, she was flabbergasted by my diet. She has her one train of thought & that’s it. Didn’t matter what I said, there was no wavering.

So there’s people/patients out there who have come to realize the SAD diet is crap & want to help themselves and go see someone like this and they’re told their diet should consist of 70% carbs & 15% protein (meat no more than 3 times a week). No wonder people give up sometimes. I mean, you have the SAD preachers and then here’s a ND preaching more crap advice on diet??!!! WHATEVER!

She did tell me I have a problem with dairy, I’ll give her that one! I think I’m ticked off at myself for not just thanking her for her time and leaving. Instead, I sat there, trying to come up with the right facts to counter what she was saying but once I realized it didn’t matter what I said I think I just zoned in & out. Oh, and the explanation for my brain fog…meat…yup…

OK…I'm done…I’ve let it gooooo…

On another note...all is well. Still eating huge BAB's. I think 75 grams of protein is my magic number.

I'm considering going to a movie tonight but am hesitating because of the darn popcorn. I associate the theatre with the stuff. Maybe I just need to stay away from the place for a few more months. Of course I would eat a steak with lots of fat before I went and I know I wouldn't be hungry but that didn't work the last time. Hmmmm.....

### MamaGrok — 2012-06-01T20:36:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-28903>

Good for you for standing up for yourself!

Movies come out so fast on DVD these days ... would you feel treated with a new one you watched at home with that fatty steak? Netflix has some great old classics, too, that are fun to watch.

### justjoan060 — 2012-06-02T00:22:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-28949>

I went to see The Best Exotic Marigold Hotel last Sunday, all by myself. Was tempted to get just a Diet Coke (I really don't drink pop hardly EVER anymore) until I found out it was $5.00!! I said no thanks, and during the movie - which was great - I had a few sugar free mints I keep in my purse. Didn't miss the popcorn at all!

### PaulaRichards — 2012-06-02T04:30:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-28990>

> **MamaGrok;29002 wrote:** Good for you for standing up for yourself!
>
>
>
> Movies come out so fast on DVD these days ... would you feel treated with a new one you watched at home with that fatty steak? Netflix has some great old classics, too, that are fun to watch.

I stayed away from the theatre & didn't feel sorry for myself, especially after I read from your journal that you had to stay away from your daughter's girl guides (girls guides?? my apologies if it was something else, brain fog!!) celebration in order to avoid the cake and cookies. I figure it's safer to just not see or smell the popcorn, at least for a while.

We went out to a decent restaurant instead. The steaks are usually awesome. I don't know what happened tonight but it wasn't the best. First, they didn't have any rib steak so I had the striploin, not one of my favorites but that's ok. However, it must have been marinated and had some kind of seasoning and lots of pepper. The veggies had "something" on them too. DH had some scallops so I tried one and although it was good, there was a definite hint of maple syrup. So by the time we got home I was burping, had a slight headache, little heartburn-y and wanting something sweet. There is something about spices, and I have yet to figure out which ones set me off, so I just don't use any at home. Anyway, I unintentionally had trace amounts of a whack of things that are sure to affect me over the next couple of days. BAB may have to be extra big tomorrow!

### PaulaRichards — 2012-06-02T04:36:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-28992>

Good for YOU!!! I think it's great when ppl are comfortable enough to go to the movies by themselves. I know ppl who just won't but I rather enjoy going by myself sometimes. I'd have no problem passing on the $5.00 pop but would probably pay $15 for the popcorn...lol...which is why I stayed away tonight!!

### MamaGrok — 2012-06-02T16:12:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-29034>

> **paulalynn;29089 wrote:** I stayed away from the theatre & didn't feel sorry for myself, especially after I read from your journal that you had to stay away from your daughter's girl guides (girls guides?? my apologies if it was something else, brain fog!!) celebration in order to avoid the cake and cookies. I figure it's safer to just not see or smell the popcorn, at least for a while.

And didn't this year! It wasn't even a question this year; I've been not caring about sweets for six months and there's just no way it was a temptation. But last year, I HAD to ... and did. It's just for a time.

It's the industrial oils in restaurant food that makes me feel bad. They might have put margarine over your steak (to fake the traditional butter topping), too. Good warning about marinades; I bet certain cuts are more prone to it in restaurants than others. I don't put anything but salt & pepper on my steaks, to let the delicious beefiness shine through, but poorer grades of meat "need" something, so they think.

### PaulaRichards — 2012-06-02T16:32:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-29041>

I don't feel as crappy as expected this morning. I'm still in PJs but I don't feel that bloated either, it may come later.

Scale 169

I hope I'm not losing my love of bacon. I just didn't taste good this morning. My ds said it was awesome. My choices are limited and there's nothing else yet that I enjoy as much as bacon that I could see eating everyday. I know I don't have to eat the same thing every morning but I can only eat other cuts of pork so often. Steak yes, but that's a regular on my dinner plate. I need a "safe" sausage.

Tried some dairy cream alternative this morning. Almond & cashew cream. YUCK!

Today was the first morning I sat outside in the sun as soon as I got up this morning. That was nice.

### DarleenMB — 2012-06-02T17:25:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-29044>

I"m sorry your trip to the ND was a bust. But just because they're a naturopathic physician doesn't mean they haven't been brainwashed like everyone else. I had a HUGE argument with "my" ND about Wiley Protocol and BHRT in general. We did this via email. She was judgmental and uninformed but by the time I got done with her .. .not so much anymore.

If yours was worth pursuing for future consults it might have been worth it to use the short form argument about eating so much meat. 3+ million years our ancestors ate ... MEAT and fats and minute quantities of wild fruits and nuts. 11,000 years our ancestors have been eating grains (aka GRASSES). Which do you think is going to have a firmer hold on our biology? 3 million years or 11,000?

I have finally gotten this straight in my own head. We **cannot** escape or override our biology just because we THINK we can. Mother Nature will have her way or she will get rid of us.

don't know if that would have helped or not but it sure SHOULD have made your ND stop and think for a minute.

And I hear you on the popcorn. I had some when we went to MIB. Naughty naughty and I don't care. But it may well have been the last bag of movie popcorn I will eat. I know they use coconut oil to pop it but that "butter" stuff they use? Not sure about that. And I just read the most interesting article over on Dr. Mercola's site about crisco. NOW I'm thinking that it's not the nuts I have a problem with but what they're roasting them with ... cottonseed oil. ewwwwwwwww

Since the mushroom incident I am beginning to notice all kinds of stuff that I'm reacting to. So I guess it was a good thing.

/martha\_stewart\_impression

I haven't had coconut manna is a couple of weeks but since there was still some in the house I had a few tablespoons this afternoon. The manna isn't to blame for my mood but definitely for the bloating I'm starting to feel. URGH! Is it time to go to bed yet.

### PaulaRichards — 2012-06-04T01:58:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-29268>

This weekend just plain sucked for me. Yesterday I was just in the most horrible mood, for no apparent reason. Then today, just didn't start out well & by late afternoon I snacked, which wouldn't have been THAT bad if it was coconut oil, or something similar but I ate potato chips. I don't keep junk food in the house. I don't buy it but had to go buy sneakers for ds; enough said!

I'm "over it" and refuse to use it as an excuse to eat more crap despite really wanting to go to Dairy Queen! In the past, specifically prior to March 19th, I would have definitely turned the indiscretion into a full day of crap. I guess I'll consider it some kind of progress.

I looked back in my journal because I remember having another really bad mood episode, which, I believe resulted in eating french fries. I started wondering if it's hormone related. I'm grasping at straws. I know what happened, more accurately what \*didn't\* happen and that was the BAB.

### DarleenMB — 2012-06-04T18:49:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-29376>

paulalynn, this whole thing is a journey and most of us are bound to make missteps along the way. It happens. It's when the missteps become a daily occurrence that you have to watch out for. I guess I'm trying to say there's no point in beating yourself up, just keep moving forward.

I ate a few things I knew I shouldn't have over the weekend (gluten-free crackers, beanito chips, a handful (literally) of popcorn). I paid for it this morning. But I'm actually learning to listen to my body because of all this. Seems some of that stuff my body DOES NOT LIKE and shows it with tummy aches and (sorry if this is TMI) loose stools/diarrhea. Since the mushroom incident I now pay REAL close attention, LOL.

And because of all that I'm finally starting to feel much better. Keep going, hon'. One foot in front of the other. We'll all get there.

>
> And because of all that I'm finally starting to feel much better. Keep going, hon'. One foot in front of the other. We'll all get there.

Thanks Darleen, that was really nice to read, especially considering what I came here to write!

### PaulaRichards — 2012-06-04T20:32:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-29398>

Yesterday I was 77 days binge-free. Today I’m back to ZERO. 77 days hasn’t been my longest stretch but it’s certainly been my “best equipped” stretch. I really thought I had it this time. Nothing in particular “happened”. I had cravings for much more than chips yesterday. I don’t normally binge on chips; it’s usually ice cream or something dessert-y. I resisted. I left it at the chips.

I woke up this morning “feeling” the potato chips but I ate the BAB with BPC. I was Full. Bloated and disgusted but full. Although I was ticked that I ate the chips I was willing to move on, dust off, etc. The cravings were still there. I think I was more annoyed that I didn’t just eat what I really wanted yesterday and move on. The cravings, the thoughts, just wouldn’t stop this morning. At coffee break I ate an egg and, given the WTH type of attitude, I asked for a tea biscuit & by lunch time I couldn’t take it anymore. I wasn't one bit hungry; I was still full and bloated but I left to get ice cream. I resorted to McDonalds’ since there’s nowhere else nearby. Had a Flurry with oreo & caramel, a caramel sundae and a couple of caramel dipping cups on the side in case there wasn’t enough on the ice cream. I used the drive thru because of course I couldn’t let anyone see me it this!!! I have never done that during work before. Never! When I binge it’s on the weekends or at the very least in the evening. I could have eaten more and would have if I were home but I knew I had to at least look like I was functioning at work!

The guy with the loud speaker sitting on my shoulder is yelling “…the day’s not over yet! Let’s stop for more on the way home…may as well be a good one before we start again tomorrow!”

I know I'll get there, one of these days, I will! Someday I will be posting about having no cravings or at least the ignorable kinds. I will!

### MamaGrok — 2012-06-05T00:27:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-29437>

BIG HUGS!!!

Learn what you can from it, forgive, be kind to yourself, move on, and EAT SOME COCONUT OIL!!! and a big fat meal stat. Don't forgte your BAB in the morning.

BIG HUGS!!!

### indigogirl — 2012-06-05T00:46:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-29444>

Hugs from me, too!

And you know what even if you eat more ice cream today you did 77 days binge free! That's huge!!

If you're going to eat more ice cream get some that you really like...enjoy it...don't beat yourself up.

BAB tomorrow!

### [email redacted] — 2012-06-05T01:11:51+0300

Oh yes you will get there, that's for sure! You know you can do it, even if from time to time you slip up. You are already doing much better, basically no cravings most of the time. This one was too loud, but that's ok, tomorrow will be another day. Big hugs from here too!!

Thanks girls, I know you _know_ where I was yesterday and what I was feeling. BTDT right??!! Even while I was eating there was a silent conversation playing in my head. “You realize you have to start from scratch again? You’re going to have cravings for days because of this. There’s going to be some white knuckling required to get back on the wagon.” I guess it’s a good sign that there was no question about starting again!

>
>
>
> BIG HUGS!!!
>
>
> MamaGrok, I read this last night but couldn’t reply because even internet {hugs} made me cry  I don’t think I really beat myself up. I read the 35 pages of responses to you plea for help on MDA and then went back to your journal, knowing there is an answer for me in there somewhere!
>
>
>
>
> > **indigogirl;29544 wrote:** Hugs from me, too!

>
> AbetterME;29549 wrote:** Oh yes you will get there, that's for sure! You know you can do it, even if from time to time you slip up. \*Big Sigh\* Thanks ABetterMe! Yep, yesterday’s craving was a loud one!!! Well, I guess I have a new “Binge Free” date to reset in my signature. I think that’s more frustrating than the food hangover and the bloating that I feel today. Oh well, I had to make the title of my journal relevant…lol! Back on the wagon! Back on the road to success

### MamaGrok — 2012-06-05T18:06:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-29555>

> **paulalynn;29650 wrote:** Even while I was eating there was a silent conversation playing in my head. “You realize you have to start from scratch again? You’re going to have cravings for days because of this. There’s going to be some white knuckling required to get back on the wagon.” I guess it’s a good sign that there was no question about starting again!

Exactly. This is one victory. The fact that it took you 70 days before falling again is another - a HUGE one. What other victories are there? What it was? How much? How long it lasted?

Pick up, dust off, go again. You'll make it. None of us knows how long it will take, but if you never give up, I bet that in a year, you're there.

Never give up. Never never never never never never never give up. (Paraphrase of Winston Churchill.  )

### [email redacted] — 2012-06-05T19:20:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-29571>

Well, I give you applause for stopping! My last bender lasted a week

I know that feeling when part of you is saying "don't do it, you know you're gonna be mad" and the other part is doing it. But I think that each time the sensible part gets stronger and stronger and eventually it wins.

As for your brain fog, you might want to listen to this podcast. Also, have you been tested for food allergies? Hormones? Do you know which foods your body don't like? Do you think the disappointing appointment with ND was a trigger?

Well, keep trucking along...we'll get there!

[http://www.blogtalkradio.com/undergroundwellness/2011/12/02/brain-drain-with-dr-dan-kalish#.T84vpROq7CQ.email](http://http://www.blogtalkradio.com/undergroundwellness/2011/12/02/brain-drain-with-dr-dan-kalish#.T84vpROq7CQ.email)

### PaulaRichards — 2012-06-05T21:55:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-29591>
