Thanks Jennifer. I’m thankful the binge happened on a Monday because, based on my history, if it had been closer to the end of the week, I would have used it as an excuse to wait until Monday to start again!

I’ll definitely listen to that podcast because the brain fog just isn’t clearing up like it normally does when I eat clean. I just mailed my saliva sample yesterday and am SO looking forward to the results. I do think there has to be some hormones messing with my system. As for food intolerances, IDK, probably dairy but I haven’t given it up long enough to see any real difference. I don’t know if it’s the case or not but the ND told me it takes about 1.5 months to really feel the difference after eliminating dairy. I don’t think the ND was a trigger, although, because basically everything she said was crap, I added HWC back after seeing her, probably in defiance of what she said!

### PaulaRichards — 2012-06-05T21:58:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-29594>

Week 1 Day 1

Binge Free: 0

**Breakfast within 30 min of waking: Yes, 7:15

**Breakfast 50 grams of protein: 65 grams, BPC, 2 HCL

**No Snacking: So far so good. 10:00am is coffee break and that’s when I usually have 2 boiled eggs with butter. Since I eat breakfast at 7:00am I think this would be considered a snack. I am going to eliminate this when I can. If I’m hungry I won’t worry about the timing right now because I have to prevent further binging. I wasn’t hungry this morning so I had a black tea.

**5 hours between Breakfast & Lunch: Yes, 12:30 - Â½ lb burger with bacon (no bun)

**5 hours between Lunch & Dinner**: Yes, 5:30 - 4 mini burgers + 2 HCL

Dinner within 4-5 hours of bedtime**: Yes

Energy?** Blah, as expected considering yesterday’s food.

Mood?** Ok, all things considered.

Cravings? When?** Around 3:30 some minor thoughts about food started, along with the heartburn-y feeling in my through. Hunger setting in again by 8:30pm but I'm going to be having my Magnesium soon going to bed.

Notes to self-TOM started yesterday. Perhaps explains the mood on Saturday.

Added HCL last week and am taking 2 with each meal. Maybe I need to add digestive enzymes as well. Up until the last year I have NEVER had heartburn or any sign of it. Although I rarely have heartburn I get this strange burning sensation in the back of my throat.

### DarleenMB — 2012-06-05T22:15:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-29598>

Oh man do I feel your pain! I have been "low carbing" since January of 2011, wheat free since 9-11 and trying to really stay the course on the leptin Rx since 10-11. It has taken THAT long for me to be able to "just say no" to sugary crap.

DH wanted a blizzard last Saturday. I breezed through the drive up for him without even a qualm and never even thought about asking for so much as a bite. WOW. Never thought that would happen. but look how long it has taken me.

You. Will. Get. There.

Be patient with yourself. Be kind to yourself. Listen to your self-talk and lose the stinkin' thinkin'. I know you can do it!!

### Souldanzer — 2012-06-05T22:53:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-29603>

Binging just prior/during my period was one of the hardest things to stop for me. I think it's a combination out of hormones and that we tend to be hungrier (what the heck attitude) during this time.

### PaulaRichards — 2012-06-06T02:30:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-29642>

> Yeah, I'll have to keep an eye on the calander in the future and keep myself well-fed!!!

> indigogirl;29724 wrote:** Could the hcl be giving you heartburn? Isn't heartburn too much stomach acid coming up your esophagus?

About a year ago I started getting a feeling like I had a lump in my throat and after some investigation was told I most likely had silent reflux. Gastro doc wanted to put me on meds but after researching that I thought "no way"! I read that a lot of the time heartburn is caused by a lack of enough stomach acid. Since I continued to have heartburn off & on over the last few months I thought I'd try the HCL and just started it last week. I don't know how long it takes to figure out if that's my problem or not. I'm open to suggestions

### indigogirl — 2012-06-06T04:14:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-29666>

I dont know very much about hcl and heartburn. I have had both kinds...horrible chest pains as well as sore throat.

I have taken meds for it, but can control it by diet and rarely get it now.

I tried hcl for it at one point and got stomach pains so stopped.

**Breakfast within 30 min of waking: Yes, 7:15

**Breakfast 50 grams of protein: 65 grams, BPC, 2 HCL

**No Snacking: Black tea at 10:30. Was eating eggs (during the first 77 days!) at this time but have not missed having them.

### [email redacted] — 2012-06-08T19:53:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-30170>

### vkiernan — 2012-06-08T20:40:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-30173>

Hi Paula. Just wanted to drop by and say hi and tell you that it does get easier over time with the LRx. For me it was a slip everynow and then but then I would move forward and then a slip and then move forward. What I am trying to say is, it seems every now and then I might have 1 slip but I also made good forward progress. I think I needed to adjust to something and then could move forward and not do some of the slips I was doing. Of course I still slipped over something else but got right back on the train the next meal. As long as you are moving forward and implemting more, you are on the right track. Perhaps you can find a treat that works for you that you can sub when you are wanting that something special. Maybe coconut milk with flavored stevia frozen with coconut and/or brazil nuts thrown in or? Of course that still might make you crave sweets and may not be the best thing. I did have to find something too once in a while. I found the coco bark works great. I eat 1 tbsp after lunch almost every day now and that is all I need. My dh eats crap all the time right next to me and he will sit there and totally enjoy himself verbally. Thank god I am a little stronger than I think. Yeah, sometimes I really want it but usually I say to myself, he is going to end up with diabetes and I am not.

As for the HCL, I can tell you that mamagroks helped me immensely. I was taking 7 with my BAB and 3 or 4 with my other meals. After I started CT'ing, I had to stop the HCL. I was getting acid reflux like crazy. It was gross. When I did the HCL, I worked up to more everyday. I never felt the burn they talk about, I just stopped at 7. Can't really say why. CT'ing has done so many wonderful things for me. There are some great websites that will tell you the symptoms of low stomach acid. That is how I knew I needed it.

Just take 1 day at a time and don't ever give up. I can tell you that I never thought I could give all that I have given up now. It didn't happen overnight. I have been doing the LRx since July 3, 2011. Almost 1 year!!!! Can hardly believe it. But, I now know so much more than I did then and realize I have a lot to fix but it is FIXABLE. You will get there as long as you keep getting up everyday and try!!!

### [email redacted] — 2012-06-08T21:53:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-30188>

You can do it Paula! Up your BAB to make sure you won't be hungry and have any cravings at all. And have CO whenever you feel like going down the carbs path

Have a nice week-end!!

### PaulaRichards — 2012-06-09T16:45:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-30269>

Week 1 Day 5

Binge Free: 4

Stayed up later than usual last night watching a movie; but wore the blue blockers, as always, and had no trouble falling asleep. Woke at 2:00am in a very disoriented way but went to bathroom and back to bed.

**Breakfast within 30 min of waking: No, more like 60 min

**Breakfast 50 grams of protein: 9:45 - 65 grams, BPC, 2 HCL

**No Snacking:

**5 hours between Breakfast & Lunch: 3:15 - very hungry-need to eat

**5 hours between Lunch & Dinner**: will probably only be 4 hours today since I'm eating now@ 3:15

Dinner within 4-5 hours of bedtime**:

Energy?** ~5

Mood?** 5, a bit indifferent

Cravings? When?** After I finished my BPC, which takes a little longer to finish than the BAB, I was, and still am, wanting something sweet. I am full. Hopefully a glass of water will fix it! Yup, problem solved.

### PaulaRichards — 2012-06-11T17:06:31+0300

Binge Free: 6

I'm back to thinking the bacon was not causing the bloating. Probably the tanked progesterone. Pharmacist told me that would be one of the first issues I'd see resolved. Cool! I wonder if there's any weight loss under all this bloating?

### [email redacted] — 2012-06-22T20:07:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-32976>

Common signs of progesterone deficiency (from Natural Hormones Balance for Women):

\- Amenorrhea

\- Oligomenorrhea

\- heavy and frenquent periods

\- Spotting a few days before periods

\- PMS

\- Cystic breasts

\- painful breasts

\- Breasts with lumps

\- Endometrios, irritability, nervousness with difficulties sleeping

\- Water retention

### PaulaRichards — 2012-06-23T03:28:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-33089>

> **MamaGrok;33089 wrote:** Did you just say tanked progesterone can cause bloating????? Tell me more!
>
>
>
> Mine was .4 in a range of .2-1.5, where ideal is supposedly 6-28!

I don't know if this is "solid info" and I no longer have the link to where I found this but it's something I noted a while back when I was searching for info...

"The hormones progesterone and estrogen play a significant role in fluid retention. When estrogen levels are elevated, women tend to retain more water than usual. This is why bloating is common in the days leading up to a woman's menstrual cycle - more estrogen means more water retention. During menopause, estrogen levels erratically fluctuate and bloating becomes more chronic. Also, progesterone is a natural diuretic, so when progesterone levels are below where they should be, women may also experience bloating.

While having too much estrogen can lead to bloating, having too little estrogen can also contribute to abdominal discomfort. That's because estrogen also has an effect on the production of bile - when estrogen levels are low, bile production decreases. Bile is fluid produced by the liver that aids in digestion. Bile helps to emulsify fats from the foods we eat and aids in cholesterol synthesis. It also acts a lubricant for our small intestines"

### PaulaRichards — 2012-06-23T03:35:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-33092>

Well, I'm off to pack some "just in case" food for a 4 day road-trip. I don't feel \*ready\* for this quite yet but I'm going to be as prepared as I can be. There is a baptism for my niece on Sunday and of course a get together with lots of "crap food". This is the worrysome part. I going with the thought that I will only eat what is optimal!!!

Have a good weekend everyone!

### PaulaRichards — 2012-06-27T17:13:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-33938>

Although the 4 day road trip involved one snack (nuts) and a couple of potatoes, this is the **first time I have EVER been away from home and didn't use it as an excuse to "eat whatever you want"!

Saturday (Week 3 Day 5)– was on the road for about 7 hours. Breakfast & lunch worked. Dinner may have included some questionable ingredients. I was on Prince Edward Island and our family went to a traditional “Lobster Supper”, which, apart from PEI lobster, includes all you can eat mussels (I am not a fan but I ate quite a few with loads of butter!!), there was “just out of the oven” breads & rolls & desserts. I DID NOT HAVE ANY!!! I did have a cup of seafood chowder, partly because Mom told me she doesn’t put flour in hers, so I didn’t give it another thought and enjoyed it immensely. I’m sure it had \*something\* in it as a thickener but it didn’t trigger any instant cravings or instant bloating.

Sunday (Week 3 Day 6)– Breakfast was a protein shake and a breakfast sandwich minus the bread. Questionable egg product?! Then after the baptism there was the celebration which of course was overflowing with delicious food!, including many of my favorites! I DID NOT EAT ANY! I was starving. I had some salad and some nacho dips without the nachos! I survived. Soon as I left I had a 4 hour drive ahead of me so had to stop for a bag of nuts in order to prevent a derailment later on. Dinner was a large steak and since I “forgot” to tell them to keep the baked potato and I ate most of it!!! So, I had nuts as a snack & a potato with dinner

Monday (Week 3 Day 7)– Breakfast was the norm. No lunch and dinner was a 12oz ribeye and again, I ate the potato!

Tuesday (Week 4 Day 1)– 5 hour drive back home. Breakfast was okay but not as much protein as I needed. Lunch was a small burger. I was hungry & craving the whole drive home, probably the potatoes I ate the last two days. When we stopped to eat there was nothing that didn’t include breading and no other restaurants or stores around. So, rather than wheat I opted for homemade French fries.

So glad to be home**. Eating is so much easier! Although our official camping season starts this weekend and I'll be away every weekend but that's basically being like home since we upgraded from tenting to a trailer a few years ago  It may require some discipline when it comes to not having a cold beer during hot days at the beach. The summer is the only time I ever drink beer.

I am planning to start my CT this weekend. I am very excited about it. We camp right on the Atlantic Ocean. I just heard from a fellow camper who takes the water temperature regularly and it was 60 degrees the other day! May not be as exciting as those who took the plunge a couple of months ago but I plan to get it every day. It will warm up over the summer but hopefully not too fast! Other years it's usually August before I get it. Not this year!!!

### PaulaRichards — 2012-06-28T18:13:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-34178>

The meeting with the pharmacist was like night and day in comparison with the Naturopath!!! We talked for about 45 minutes about diet, supplements, inflammation and we’re on the same page!!! Rather than recommending BHRT he thought I would benefit from supplements. While he said my estrogen is low, due to the low progesterone I’m showing signs of estrogen dominance (I know!!) and I need adrenal support, etc. During our conversation he said he was surprised at the stuff I already knew (Thank you Dr. Kruse!)

I’ve often wondered if the supplements I’ve been taking twice a day for years were worth the money. They cost about $100/month and supposedly have an 80% absorption rate when compared to Centrum or One-A-Day! He looked at the RDI’s and ingredients and said they were awesome and to continue taking them. It’s kinda nice to know you’re not throwing money away!

Anyway, I don’t know much about adaptogens so therefore I don’t really have an opinion on what he recommended but I am okay with trying to fix the hormones with supplements, at least for now! This is what he recommended. If anyone reading has an opinion, I’d love to hear it

Douglas Labs **Progesto-Mend 90 caps is a combination of phytoprogesterones, progestogenic adaptogens and other herbs that support optimal progesterone function by maintaining the health of progesterone producing glands and by supporting the healthy function of progesterone responsive tissues. Progesto-Mend may be a useful dietary supplement for individuals wishing to support healthy progesterone production and function.

Progestomend Herbal 2,100 mg (Proprietary Blend):

Rehmannia (Root), Bupleurum (root), Passion flower (aerial parts), Peony (root), Coleus forskiohlii (root, standardized to 10% forskohlii), Dioscorea vilosa (root) and Chasteberry extract(fruit).

Douglas Labs Adreno-Mend** 120c is a synergistic combination of ten highly valued herbal extracts with adaptogenic properties designed to support healthy, balanced adrenal gland function. These plant adaptogens include Schisandra chinensis, Bacopa monnieri, Rhodiola rosea, Eleutherococcus senticosus, Magnolia officinalis, Rhemannia glutinosa, Bupleurum falcatum, Panax ginseng, Coleus forskohlii and Withania sominfera.

### PaulaRichards — 2012-07-05T18:13:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-35644>

Wow! Up to:

Week 5 Day 2

168 lbs

The camping weekend was great. I broke two rules; once in the afternoon with a handful of cashews, and the next night with a glass of wine. I didn’t have cravings. I always want to snack at the beach and eat after drinking wine but I didn’t!!! I was in the ocean for 30 minutes on Saturday and was in twice on Sunday! Most of the CT happened from the waist down since it took me forever to get wet up to my neck. That’s the hardest part! It was good but definitely not as cold as it was this time last year!

The strange thing is I woke in the middle of the night on Sunday with this terrible aching in my whole arm. I was almost in tears. I put on a pain relieving cream and had to take Tylenol. It was the weirdest thing! Then when I woke Monday I had a sore throat and a cold and I couldn’t go to work on Tuesday or Wednesday. I have never had a cold like this, ever!!! I rarely get colds but when I do I usually feel it coming and can usually ward it off with some remedies. I had no warning, it hit me like a ton of bricks!

I’m on the mend today & am at work again. On a positive note, I’m down 2 pounds. Even though I felt like crap I ate my BAB every day! Usually when I’m sick I want carbs but the cravings are gone! Gone. Gone. Gone. I am loving this!!!!!!

Now, have to catch up with everything that's been happening here

### PaulaRichards — 2012-07-17T18:22:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-38739>

Week 6 Day 6

Weight: 170 (Darn beer!)

Well, where do I start? With the positive I guess. Didn’t binge or have any terrible cravings. Although the weekend did result in snacking, mostly in the form of liquid carbs!

Saturday – as with everyday it started with the BAB. Drove in the rain to the campground. Sun came out so I sat and finished reading Fifty Shades. I had a beer. Then another. It must have been the book LOL!!! The only time I EVER drank beer in the past was at the beach on a hot sunny day. So I ended up drinking 4! Then had a big steak for dinner.

Sunday – was a hot sunny beach day. Drank more beer. 5! I know. I know! I won’t continue this trend! Late in the evening I had a handful of tortilla chips. I blame the earlier beer!

Monday – back on track but had to white knuckle through some cravings. Ended up eating some potatoes at dinner.

So, not exactly an optimal weekend but I’m still in a positive frame of mind. Last year this type of weekend would have definitely resulted in a binge!

The ocean CT didn’t exactly feel like CT. The ocean is noticeably warmer for this time of year. At least two weeks I felt cold when I was it but not this weekend. Oh well, it was still awesome!

**Breakfast within 30 min of waking: Yes

**Breakfast 50 grams of protein: 65 grams, 1 HCL, +progestro-mend & adrenal-mend

**No Snacking: Black Tea

**5 hours between Breakfast & Lunch:

**5 hours between Lunch & Dinner**:

Dinner within 4-5 hours of bedtime**:

Energy?** ~5

Mood?** ~5

Cravings? When?**

Re the chocolate cream cheese ... I plan on softening a brick of Philly, then running it in the mixer and adding some sweetener (you choose what you want). I plan on using double the amount I would normally because I'm going to mix in about 2 oz of melted UNSWEETENED chocolate. I have a bar of Dagoba in the cupboard I've been wanting to use. So essentially it's going to be like coco/crack only made with cream cheese instead of coconut oil.  I'm thinking of all kinds of yummy things to add to it. chopped coconut; chopped macadamias; or how about peanut butter??? Just a touch. Or just spread it on strawberries ... or just eat it with a spoon.

### Souldanzer — 2012-07-18T02:45:09+0300

> **paulalynn;39067 wrote:** OMG...eating it with a spoon sounds good to me. I was an ice cream addict in my past life! This could be dangerous

Nothing better than a can of coconut milk, blended on high in the vitamix, put in the fridge overnight. Heaven.... like whipped cream to me. I always \*loved\* whipped cream as a child but had to abide by my mother's thinking that it's only to top something off - NOT to be eaten with a spoon! Then AN came... and now I'm going to eat cans of whipped, cold coconut milk until I'm sooooo tired of it  With a SPOON. Despite coconut oil being better without the carbs - it's so worth the 7g I get a day from this.

Maybe one day I will add something to it, like cinnamon or pumpkin spice. But for now, the pure taste of the coco milk it hitting the spot. Vanilla sounds good maybe.

### PaulaRichards — 2012-07-25T21:21:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-41060>

Week 7 Day 7

Weight: 169

So either I need to take a computer with me camping or have to stay home and staying home isn’t an option! The problem is I’m NOT AS accountable when I’m camping. When I’m home my diet is close to optimal (oops, maybe not with the Epi-paleo revealed!) over the last 7 weeks or so, including no snacks and proper timing! But when I’m camping the same isn’t quite the case. I haven’t binged which is a blessing in itself and haven’t really struggled with cravings but let’s just say I’ve been caught with my hand in the tortilla chips and the bag of nuts, not to mention the beer (low-carb but I KNOW!)! I had such resolve and determination before the summer started and still want to continue on but what happens to me on the beach??!!! Believe me, no one has to twist my arm. I sit down and the words “pass me a beer” are out before I had time to break a sweat! It’s not like I love beer or crave it at any other time. Ever. I must change my thinking and think of it as I do any other junk food as poison (thanks Katy!)! Another motivation for not drinking it is becoming aware this weekend that I have terrible sleeps when I do drink it.

### vkiernan — 2012-07-27T05:00:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-41446>

Good luck on staying accountable. Remember, beer is no different than a piece of bread. It's probably actually worse cuz of the alcohol. Have fun and stay safe! Camping sounds like a lot of fun.

### PaulaRichards — 2012-07-27T15:16:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-41497>

Thanks vkiernan!!! It'll be an accountable weekend!

### PaulaRichards — 2012-07-31T23:39:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-42134>

On a positive note the weekend was awesome. I felt great. Spent lots of time in the morning sunshine reading and afternoons on the beach with a few dips in the ocean, which is getting noticeably warmer. I haven’t had this kind of a tan in literally 20 years! 20 years ago I would tan to a dark brown but the last few years I would burn and it would fade away. I’m tanning again. This has to be from the Leptin Reset!

Food choices were good all weekend but the beverage choices were the negative aspects of the weekend. I didn’t drink as much beer as last weekend & it was gluten free (Katy!). Then on the way home yesterday I stopped and bought homemade fries. OMG they were so good!

So, I guess I have no right to complain about not feeling very good today. I felt horrible getting out of bed this morning. It was the first morning in months I didn’t even want a BAB but I \*knew\* it would likely be a disastrous day if I didn’t eat it. So, here I sit with a bloated belly, swollen feet and my legs feel so tight I can hardly bend them. Hmmm….time I learned a lesson! I mean WTH?!!! My weakness has never come in a liquid form. I mean my cravings, my past binges were always food. Why am I sabotaging myself on the weekends with beer, even if it is gluten free? I’ve done great these last few months & now this!!! I won’t continue setting myself backwards. I can’t have my weekends resetting the progress I’ve made these last months or have they already?

### PaulaRichards — 2012-08-13T19:39:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-44713>

So where do I start? Hmmm…CRAP! These last few long weekends camping, as we know, have included a few gluten free beer and the odd handful of corn chips, even though I know this is getting me nowhere. Well, the “indiscretions” finally caught up with me yesterday on the way home. I had cravings and it didn’t end with homemade fries. I ate ice cream too. Geez I hate admitting that one! There was a lengthy internal debate going on inside my head but the ice cream won! I felt horrible afterwards (big surprise!) but was still thinking about what I “might as well eat tomorrow since I royally screwed up” !

I didn’t want to but I ate the BAB this morning. I feel like it’s going to take some willpower to resist the cravings today. Part of me doesn’t even want to resist them URGH! Of course the WHY WHY WHY’s are going through my head constantly today! Ok, enough with the pity party, time to move on!

### KiwiLauren — 2012-08-14T00:54:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-44786>

> **paulalynn;45022 wrote:** So where do I start? Hmmm…CRAP! These last few long weekends camping, as we know, have included a few gluten free beer and the odd handful of corn chips, even though I know this is getting me nowhere. Well, the “indiscretions” finally caught up with me yesterday on the way home. I had cravings and it didn’t end with homemade fries. I ate ice cream too. Geez I hate admitting that one! There was a lengthy internal debate going on inside my head but the ice cream won! I felt horrible afterwards (big surprise!) but was still thinking about what I “might as well eat tomorrow since I royally screwed up” !
>
>
>
> I didn’t want to but I ate the BAB this morning. I feel like it’s going to take some willpower to resist the cravings today. Part of me doesn’t even want to resist them URGH! Of course the WHY WHY WHY’s are going through my head constantly today! Ok, enough with the pity party, time to move on!

I don't know if my experience can be helpful (and I haven't read your entire journal, so apologies if I'm staying something stupid here) but I have found that when I added lots more good fat and started pounding the epi-paleo menu (seafood x2 per day, some of it raw every day) the cravings have totally gone away. IT is really easy to get caught up in self-blame (and self-hate) for the 'indiscretions' but I have learned that my cravings simply signal that something is off.. and once I right that issue, the cravings go away. So they become knowledge to point to how I can help myself, not blunt instruments to beat myself up with.

Also, Dr K mentioned in my first consult that any cravings at all meant that I am not yet leptin sensitive, so to really step up the BAB and to make sure I hit the mark on protein and have a lot of fat. Once I upped it (and stopped hoping I was LS and ready to 'graduate') things have really settled down.

Hang in there!

Lauren

### PaulaRichards — 2012-08-14T16:18:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-44893>

Thanks Lauren!!! I admit I have not started to include regular seafood in my diet yet but I will. A few months ago I learned that I need 65 grams of protein in the BAB and found that to be the key. I have no fear of fat. I love it! Except the stuff that's attached to my hips! LOL!

Thanks again for the words of encouragement!

### PaulaRichards — 2012-09-11T21:20:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-51019>

I guess I’ve lived up to the title of this journal once again! It’s been an on-again-off-again three weeks, with the last week being just disgraceful! I was in the cool ocean the other day just bewildered with myself. For the last two months I was blessed with being able to spend 3-4 days a week in the ocean but then I let my diet go to hell! But enough crying over spilt milk. There’s always the bathtub. One thing that did surprise me during this derailment was the fact that I didn’t turn into a hateful, irritable b&\*%^h! I’m guessing the adrenal support and progesterone adaptogens are working.

Back on track as of yesterday. I love this forum. I didn’t realize how much I missed it. Can’t wait to catch up on everyone’s progress.

Finally saw my doc yesterday with the list of bloodwork. For the first time in 12 years I left without what I wanted. I have always been impressed with my doc but yesterday he started talking about pseudo-science after I showed him my saliva testing results. He begrudgingly ordered vitamin d level & TSH. I’m going to get the testing that I want but I’ll have to pay for the testing. If anyone knows of a good & cost-effective mail order lab in Canada please let me know.

He did refer me to a circulation specialist because my lower legs & feet have been swelling to the point it feels like my skin is going to tear. I didn’t ask for a referral I just wanted the darn bloodwork ordered.

### [email redacted] — 2012-09-11T23:53:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-51066>

Paula! Welcome back!!

It might have been uo and down in the last weeks, but at least you can still see improvement! What labs do you want to order? I did salivary testing with ZRT Lab, ordered through Canary Club.

### PaulaRichards — 2012-09-17T20:16:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-52173>

I feel so much better compared to this time last week. Being back on track feels so good! There were a few urges to cheat/binge that resulted in some white-knuckle moments but all is well. The derailment bloating is gone.

I even enjoyed a day/night of camping without cheating. The ocean temp this weekend was 66 degrees. A little chilly compared to c couple of weeks ago but I stayed in for 20 minutes.

I’m eating a bit more seafood. Not as much as I should or as much as everyone else seems to be but it’s a start. I discovered I like shrimp. What kind of shrimp are people buying? Most of the stuff in the grocery store says it comes from Thailand. I don’t remember where but I thought I read somewhere else that this stuff was crap.

### PaulaRichards — 2012-09-21T19:35:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-53225>

The progesto-mend supplement I’ve been taking must be working. This is the first time in about 5+ years that I’ve had a “normal” period. Several years ago they went from an average of 4-5 days to a 1.5-2 day TOM. My mood…loving this mood!

Over the past two months my mood has gradually been improving with no extreme lows which I didn’t know if I could attribute to the supplements or maybe it was just my perception but the last few weeks has been awesome. I love it! I’m just in a “loving life” type mood and not the kind that I’m forcing. Lots of times over the years I’d try to change my outlook after reading self-help books. But it never lasted. Right now it seems effortless.

I used to think that feeling good for me had to start with losing weight but after experiencing this genuine positive mood weight loss has finally moved to second place!!! I never, in a million years, would have ever thought I’d be saying that!

### PaulaRichards — 2012-09-21T21:10:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-53255>

> **Cavemam;53862 wrote:** Yay for you!!!! So glad to see this. Help us out, what does your new primal sense say is the reason?

Seriously I think my hormones are playing a big role. Perhaps my adrenals are recovering as well. I had hormone testing done a few months ago and everything was in the basement. I'm almost finished my third months worth of supplements recommended by the BHRT compounding pharmacist (he actually recommended the supplements in my case vs the BHRT). My mood and how I react to stress improved a bit during the first & second month but not enough to know whether it was a placebo affect or not. According to the way I've been feeling the last few weeks...this is real!

These are the two products that I've used

[http://www.yourhormones.com/adreno-mend/index.html](http://http://www.yourhormones.com/adreno-mend/index.html)

[http://www.yourhormones.com/progesto-mend/index.html](http://http://www.yourhormones.com/progesto-mend/index.html)

### PaulaRichards — 2012-09-25T18:42:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-54144>

It's lunch break & I'm sitting in the sun. Love it! I work @a hospital so most ppl here think I'm...hmmm...what't the word...not being smart...because I have this wicked "been down south" type tan. It's been 20 years since I've tanned like this. Still amazes me!

Anyway...doc thinks I've responded well with the adrenal supps and wants me to come off them for a couple weeks. I haven't done a lot of research on adrenal fatigue but I'm a bit reluctant to stop then because I don't want to feel like I used to feel again. Advice anyone?

### PaulaRichards — 2012-09-27T15:51:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-54738>

Lost my energy level & loving life feeling for a couple of days last weekend but I had a bit of a head cold so I'm thinking that was the reason because it's back! I'm almost giddy with excitement at this feeling. I can't imagine what optimal will feel like because I'm probably only a fraction of the way there but this increase in energy levels and positivity sure tells me what a mess I've been most of my life!

For as far back as I can remember the only thing I would want to do after work would be eat dinner, watch tv and go to bed. If there were activities I had to participate in it would just ruin my night. I'm just starting to realize I'm not feeling this way anymore. Now, it's so different! The last few nights I've been busy but it doesn't bother me. I feel an inner happiness and I never thought I'd be someone saying that! Now, add some weight loss and I'll think I won the lottery!

### PaulaRichards — 2012-10-01T15:13:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-55553>

> **Endless;56137 wrote:** Sounds like you are doing great! I wish I had a supportive doc like that. What adrenal supps are you taking?

Hi Endless,

I’m not sure which supplement deserves the credit for my newfound positive outlook and increased energy levels. I’ve been taking Progesto-mend and Adreno-mend from Douglas Labs. As for the supportive doc. Nada!!! I can’t even get decent blood work ordered. Thankfully we have a compounding pharmacist in our area and he is very knowledgeable. He has a difficult time with most doctors around here with BHRT. Based on my saliva results he knew I needed help but also knew I wasn’t going to get the BHRT prescription I needed so he recommended supplements instead. They don’t work as quickly as the BHRT but I did see some improvement in the first month but it was in the third month that I saw the most noticeable differences!!!

Here's the supplements I've been taking:

[http://www.yourhormones.com/adreno-mend/index.html](http://http://www.yourhormones.com/adreno-mend/index.html)

[http://www.yourhormones.com/progesto-mend/index.html](http://http://www.yourhormones.com/progesto-mend/index.html)

### PaulaRichards — 2012-10-01T18:05:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-55584>

When you know better you do better??? Not always!!!! At least not for me. I ate ice cream yesterday and it wasn’t a quick decision. I spent time thinking about whether or not to buy it. You would think good reasoning would have prevailed but nope. Why do I sabotage myself? I’m sure the cravings were triggered by the Â½ cup of mashed potatoes the night before. Followed by a BAB that didn’t include enough protein. Urgh! Do I know better? Yes!!!!! Is there a supplement for stupidity??!!! I have plenty of reason to stay on track. I’m going on a cruise in about 6 weeks and even though I’m feeling good I’d love to lose a couple of pounds. There’s a part of my brain telling me “WTH…you know you’re going to eat EVERYTHING when you’re on the cruise & have to START again when you get home!” I have to shut that voice down!!!! I don't want to eat a weeks worth of crap while away. I want to make the sensible food choices because I want to keep this "feelin good" type mood while on vacation. Okay, I'm done complaining!

### [email redacted] — 2012-10-10T18:26:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-57343>

> **paulalynn;58645 wrote:** Damn wagon! Fell off again. Feels like it's 50 miles down the road and I'll never catch up! Urgh! No excuse really. I CAN do this. I "see" myself as healthy, "see" myself eating properly but I'm back to giving into cravings on the weekends. Blah, blah, blah...back on track today. Need to get my history, etc sent in so I can schedule appt with Dr Kruse.
>
>
>
> _\-- Sent from my Palm Pre using [Forums](http://http://developer.palm.com/appredirect/?packageid=com.newnessdevelopments.forums)_

I've been in the position where that effing wagon was flying down the road with my fat arse trying to run behind and catch. It's not so easy to do - recognize that it's going to hurt for 3-4 days.

What I've found is a triage approach - start making sure you do one of the things that work for you, and hopefully that will send the good juju down the cascade.

Like, just FORCE that awful BAB - does meat work better for keeping you satisfied and less snacky than fish? THen go for some meat. And, when getting back up on the wagon, it's ok to not be perfect right away. When you've made great strides in the past, did you do it by being perfect with all the RX's from moment one? nah. Did you start this journey by having a big dollop of cream in your coffee? Perhaps it's a crutch, but if you need a crutch to get going - only you are going to judge you. Sometimes the ends justify the means.

### PaulaRichards — 2012-10-25T15:24:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-60126>

Still on the wagon but drove over a bump while I was away for work. Great choices for 3 days but the day I ordered scallops I ate the fries. I didn't use it as an excuse to binge

Update-need these notes to keep track due to the brain fog.

Almost finished 4th month on Progesto-Mend and Adreno-Mend with many symptoms improving, particularly mood and stress level.

3 weeks ago added Testo-Gain but also took a 2 week break from the adrenal supp. Not sure if it was coincidence but when I started the testosterone supp my “lovin life” mood changed a bit and I felt angry & aggressive. Was it the testosterone supp or lack of adrenal supp or neither??? It has eased off somewhat.

Had my monthly check-in with the compounding pharmacist and went through a long list of symptoms to determine what’s improving and what isn’t. Weight still not budging. Plan is:

Continue with Progesto-Mend & Adreno-Mend (lovin these!)

Continue with Testo-Gain for 2 more months minimum

Adding EstroFactors to promote healthy estrogen metabolism.

Re-evaluate in 1 month.

May consider thyroid supplement. Hard to determine without testing but can’t get the testing when doctors won’t order it. Urgh!

For curiosity sake with a bit of frustration involved I entered my food into fitnesspal.com. Yesterday , which is very similar to everyday I’m on the wagon, was:

1508 calories

9 grams of carbs

126 grams of fat

87 grams of protein

Hormones have to be improving considering the improvement I'm seeing in symptoms. Is there more wrong than out-of-whack hormones??? Sigh...BHRT would have been so much faster...but there's no shopping for doctors in Nova Scotia...there aren't enough to go around! I'm thankful for the supplements

### PaulaRichards — 2012-10-29T17:46:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-60720>

I haven't been afraid of fat in many years but I've deliberately been increasing the fat the last couple of weeks. I've added 3/4cup of HWC at breakfast and more later in the day. I haven't noticed any difference but yesterday I ate breakfast and ate lunch, which I rarely do. Was busy and didn't eat dinner so by the time I went to bed I was a bit hungry. Scale moved for the first time in weeks this morning. Dropped 2 lbs. Don't know what that means because what I normally would have eaten at dinner I ate at lunch. Hmmm...whatever!

Less than 2 weeks until the cruise. Haven't been obsessing about how I'm going to eat while away. I'm actually looking forward to continuing to eat good while I'm away. This just might be my opportunity to start eating more seafood!

### PaulaRichards — 2012-10-30T14:25:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-60894>

OMG...so tired today. HAve stayed up until 11pm the last couple of night. I blame the person who told me about the tv show Sons of Anarchy". Man I must have an addictive personality. I've watched all 4 seasons on Netflix in the last 3 weeks! Crazy! Then when I did go to bed I was freezing and it always takes me longer to fall asleep when I'm cold. Makes me wonder why am I always cold at night. I drive 30 min to and from work with no heat on and we've had some cold mornings lately. Night time comes and I'm going to bed with pj's, socks and sometimes a hoodie!

Just had bloodwork done. Not that there'll be much info since the good ole doc barely ordered anything but looking forward to finding out if my vit D supplementation over the last year did any good.

### DarleenMB — 2012-10-31T19:38:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-61127>

LOL, have you started the latest season yet? I mean OMG ... ooooo JIMMY (I adore Jimmy Smits can you tell?). There's also a line in one of the later eps of this season that just keeps rolling through my head and makes that chubby, hairy, unkempt little man who SAID it look totally adorable.

I like my bedroom cold ... don't sleep well otherwise. But I still do have to put socks on because my feet are so cold still. But that's all I need. You might try skipping the pj's. You actually warm up more (I think) and faster if you're naked. Just add an extra blankie. Those acrylic jobs actually do too good a job warming me up and I wake up most mornings with just the sheet and thin cotton summer blanket on me. Which is pretty weird given how low my body temp actually is and the fact that's it's usually (right now anyway) about 62 in the bedroom. I find I really heat up about 4am then start cooling off a bit.

don't know if any of that helps but it's my story and it works for me.

ETA you might be getting chilled at night because you are just sitting watching tv. I find that when I'm not moving around much I get chilled and sometimes have a hard time warming back up. Maybe bundling up while you're inactive would help?

### PaulaRichards — 2012-11-06T14:56:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-62348>

This morning I realized I’ve been waking up a bit easier in the mornings. On the weekends I normally set the alarm for 9:30am because if I didn’t I could stay in bed until noon. Anyway this weekend I woke up both mornings at 7:30am & couldn’t fall back to sleep (believe me I tried!). Weekdays I have the alarm set about 25 min early so I can hit snooze at least three times and many morning I have no recollection of hitting the snooze. The last two mornings I woke with the alarm but didn’t go back to sleep, I was ready to get up. Very nice. Perhaps a coincidence because I’m leaving Thursday and my mind is on fast forward but still nice!

### PaulaRichards — 2012-11-26T09:45:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-65619>

Another Day 1, this time 177 lbs!!! Iâ€™m not going to focus on the negative but boy do I feel disgusting! The cruise wasnâ€™t a food fest, but I did have some off-limit things. It was when I got home that the binging started, maybe because I was up 5 lbs, felt bloated and had the WTH kind of attitude. It ruined the improvement I was starting to feel in the mornings. I started feeling a bit more rested in the mornings but this past week I felt like I had a hangover every time I woke up and I just didnâ€™t want to get out of bed. Okay, Iâ€™m done complaining. Onward & upward!!!

Finally got some blood work results, although nothing useful, except the vitamin D. Iâ€™m hoping my consult with Dr. Kruse will be in the next couple of weeks. I just canâ€™t figure out how to book it because it was paid for a while back but Misty said sheâ€™ll get back to me with some instructions

### DarleenMB — 2012-11-26T11:21:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-65626>

I'm sorry to hear you're having such issues. Â It's really the pits when one backslides. Â Since I am not a binger I have no good ideas to offer just my support.

For me, sticking to that BAB and then paying attention to when I'm hungry and eating something good helps a lot. Â I have also learned the hard way NOT to keep junk in the house. Â It's way to easy to just grab "stuff" and eat that instead of taking a few minutes to fix a little something. Â 

I've found that keeping cans of salmon in the pantry along with sardines and other fishy stuff has been my saving grace. Â It takes about 30 seconds to open a can, plop it on a plate and sit down and eat. Â Problem solved.

I hear you on the weight thing. Â Trying to let go of that is probably one of the hardest things I've had to deal with. Â That whiny voice that screeches at me 'I'M FATFATFATFAT' just won't shut up so I have learned to tune her out. Â The calm voice of reason that also lives in my head reminds her and me that just as it took many years to \*\*\*\* up my body which resulted in this weight gain, so it may take a few years to reverse it.

The payoff for me at this point is (a) I'm sleeping better than I have in years. Â (b) My brain is starting to come back online. Â (c) Which means that after living underwater for at least a decade I'm starting to break the surface again and it. feels. Â wonderful!

This didn't happen overnight. Â That last round of starvation with that stupid jenny craig diet was probably the last straw for my poor beleaguered body. Â So I've spent close to 2 years doing low/no-carb and gained about 10 pounds in the process. Â I've been sticking (about 99% of the time) to the leptin Rx for almost a year. Â My weight has not budged except to go up another couple of pounds in the last few months. Â It's disheartening until I remind myself what I have gained (in addition to the pounds, LOL).

Maybe it's just because of my age that I notice these small, incremental changes and can celebrate them so much. Â when you're standing there watching yourself slide into the abyss of mental darkness, to be given a hand back out is a precious thing.

Hang in there PaulaLynn. Â don't be so hard on yourself. Â One step and one day at a time.

### diane — 2012-11-26T11:45:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-65629>

It really is hard when you start sliding back. I haven't had the greatest weak - or that's what I first thought. Then I realize how hard I am on myself - and that it wasn't so bad after all, and I made a lot of GOOD choices. But somehow just choose to beat myself up over the bad.

Â 

What I am finally learning to do (which has taken time) is to figure out what are the key factors that take me off track. And I have finally realized that it's my sleep - which is thrown off by staying up late, drinking coffee (any coffee during the day seems to affect my sleep), and eating too late. Then, by not getting enough sleep, the next day I feel like I need to drink more coffee, and eat things that will keep me awake. If you can use this time to help you understand how you go off track and how you can get back. I'm learning to appreciate what all of this teaches me.

Like Darleen, I'm also seeing so many improvements with my thinking (vs. cloudy) and energy levels. And once I see that, I can make a decision as to whether I'm ready to give that up!

Definitely book that consult with Dr. Kruse. He can help you focus in on what you need to work on. I'm getting updated labs done next week and will booking mine after that!

> Finally got some blood work results, although nothing useful, except the vitamin D. Iâ€™m hoping my consult with Dr. Kruse will be in the next couple of weeks. I just canâ€™t figure out how to book it because it was paid for a while back but Misty said sheâ€™ll get back to me with some instructions /wp-includes/images/smilies/icon\_smile.gif

Paula,Â I can tell you that even if you follow this protocol 100% you still might not get better or it might take a really, really long time.Â  It really depends on what is all broke.Â  I have been doing this for 1.5 yrs and I am finally making progress.Â  Even when you start to find out, it still takes time and probably more testing.Â  For myself, it has been getting better ever since really knowing the things that need fixing and working on them directly.Â So many factors come into play and screw things up even when you areÂ 100%.Â  If you have gut or hormone issues of any type, this can stall you or make it impossible to succeed.Â  Truly, it isÂ not all your fault.Â  Your body is messed up and craves things due to that.Â  You have to find out what is going on in there.Â  I am finally not caring about sugar but I have had a lot of things to fix first.Â  But, my point is, now that things are coming around it is much easier to stay the course and even want to stay the course.Â 

Most of my tests that have HELPED me have been out of pocket.Â  It has cost me a lot of money but now that things are getting better it has been worth every penny.Â  I am still not there but certainly a lot closer than ever.Â  Make sure you write up one hell of a history for your consult with Jack if this is your first.Â  He will lead you with his flashlight and you will succeed in the end if you keep trying and figure out what is wrong and then do something about it.Â

### PaulaRichards — 2012-11-26T15:06:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-65644>

Thank you ladies, I appreciate the support. Really I do!!! I agree with everything you all said. Over the last 9 months with the Leptin Rx I have definitely learned a lot about my body and what I need in order to be successful (must eat BAB!). Obviously, I donâ€™t always put it into practice. This past week was the worst â€œslip-upâ€ of the last nine months but I havenâ€™t even considered throwing in the towel! I know what it feels like to feel betterâ€¦the brain fogâ€¦I have experienced definite improvementâ€¦a few months ago I was seriously wondering if I was losing my mind. If I hadnâ€™t found Jack Kruse and this forum I would never have considered it was hormone related. My emotional wellbeing is worldâ€™s ahead of where I was four or five months ago and that alone is worth more to me than the number on the scale and I never thought Iâ€™d be able to say that! Iâ€™ve learned so much here and am so thankful for Jack Kruse and the support of this forum!

Testing is the challenging part. Iâ€™m willing to pay and did have the saliva testing done but need the proper blood work. Iâ€™m trying to find out if we have a private lab in Canada. Iâ€™ll pay for it because my doc wonâ€™t order what I want and thereâ€™s no shopping for docs here because we have a shortage and no one is taking new patients and most donâ€™t even have waiting lists!

### PaulaRichards — 2012-11-27T09:11:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-65685>

Well I made it through Day 1 again. Had to go for a walk last night in order to deal with the cravings. Itâ€™s actually not that bad but I know the next couple of weeks could have some trying moments! I was planning to start some CT this time round but since the weather has turned cold I am freezing! I wake up warm and donâ€™t mind the cold temps until after 7pm, then as the evening progresses I am almost shivering. Does this scream THYROID?? How the heck to you get into the tub when youâ€™re already freezing!

### DarleenMB — 2012-11-27T12:29:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-65696>

I run the tub full of cold water. Â Get my iPad set to HBO-GO and queue up the next ep of True Blood, put my socks on and just GTFI ... I make myself SIT before I can even think OMFG this is COLD. Â I'm not going to lie and tell you it gets easier because it does NOT. Â But I AM starting to LIKE it.

I sit for 11 minutes now with the water almost up to my waist and then take my legs out of the water and lie back. Â More gasping more cursing more epithets hurled Jack's way. Â Then I'm in and I stay there for now 32 minutes. Â My goal is 30 minutes sitting and 30 minutes on my back.

YES I'm shivering now like the proverbial leaf in the wind. Â Yesterday it took a couple of hours to warm back up. Â don't care.

I re-read CT 2. Â It helped. Â Now I'm agonna do to you what Ealachan did to me.

Â 

JUST GET IN THE DAMNED TUB!!!

### vkiernan — 2012-11-27T14:27:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-65703>

> **paulalynn said**
> Well I made it through Day 1 again. Had to go for a walk last night in order to deal with the cravings. Itâ€™s actually not that bad but I know the next couple of weeks could have some trying moments! I was planning to start some CT this time round but since the weather has turned cold I am freezing! I wake up warm and donâ€™t mind the cold temps until after 7pm, then as the evening progresses I am almost shivering. Does this scream THYROID?? How the heck to you get into the tub when youâ€™re already freezing!

What I do is CT when I am the warmest which is in the early morning and after eating my BAB.Â  When I get up I turn on the little heater in my bathroom (it is about 60 in there otherwise) and then eat my BAB and then CT in 50 deg. for 30 min. fully submerged.Â  Oh yeah, I also started putting my makeup on before CT'ing otherwise I get shiver messes with mascara.Â  But, it works and I get the job done.Â  Might not be as ideal as the pm but I am too tired and cold to go there at this point.Â  It is helping me with losing a few pounds now too so I am excited and want to do more.Â  Just do what you can, but DO IT!Â Good luck!Â  I also found that I love reading magazines in the tub.Â  It is fun reading and makes the time fly.

### DarleenMB — 2012-11-27T14:37:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-65705>

In thinking over your question about "thyroid" I think I'd be more inclined to ask if your omega3/omega6 levels are out of balance. Â How much seafood are you eating?

Â 

and another question popped into my head ... what are you craving? Â Are you sure you're eating enough? Â When I'm fully sated I don't want ANYthing. Â While I was at the store yesterday I kept walking by stuff and thinking "ugh who in their right mind would eat THAT?" and looking at junk in people's carts and thinking "why are you doing that to yourself?" Â It wasn't self-righteous by any means. Â It was just NOTHING I saw even appealed to me on any level. Â And yes, I had eaten lunch before I went ... the usual can of wild salmon and baby dills and sweet potato chips. Â Not many chips, just enough to help cut the salmon. Â I mean, I don't even particularly want chocolate these days. Just. Don't. Want. It. Â And I am a chocoholic supremo. Â Or used to be.

### PaulaRichards — 2012-12-01T11:13:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-66027>

I just love that hampster pic, reminds me of myself lol!

December 1st...I love December, not because it's cold but I'm a huge fan of Christmas!!!

Anyway...this week hasn't been as tough, diet-wise, as I thought it would be.Â  The cravings aren't that bad.Â  But my mood...my mood yesterday was bad and again today?!!!Â  It's been a longtime since I've been like this, the best way I can describe it is...indifference/bit\*h!Â  I wondering if it's the newer supplements I added.Â  I'm thinking either the testosterone supplement or the estrofactors (for detox & metabolism of estrogen)!Â  If it is detox do I keep taking the estrofactors and keep plowing through this?Â  For at least today I didn't take it.Â  Might leave it our for a few days and see what happens.

Met with pharmacist again and he suggested I add a thyroid supplement.Â  Mainly because there is still a piece missing from this puzzle.Â  I feel much better than I did 5 months ago.Â  A lot of the estrogen dominant symptoms have been relieved but the weight just isn't budging.Â  He suggesting something called Medcaps T3 which is supposed to help convert T4 to T3.Â  This is starting to get a bit frustrating because it's like the blind leading the blind without the proper test results.

I found through the Canary Club that I can have the tyroid panel (reverse T3 not available) &Â HS-CRP done through blood spot testing.Â  Is blood spot testing effective???Â  I'll askÂ Dr. Kruse when I have my consult.

### PaulaRichards — 2012-12-05T09:14:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-66432>

Only ate breakfast yesterday and a couple of pieces of 90% chocolate in the late afternoon because I had an appt after work and was too late getting home. I thought I'd have some trouble going to sleep because I was hungry but I slept well.

Saw the vascular specialist the other day, I don't know why other than my family doc insisted because of the extreme water retention I had in my legs this summer. I was confident I'd be able to get the specialist to order the blood tests I wanted. Nope! I'm nice about it...I don't talk like I know it all! I even asked him...is it possible thyroid could do this and he said yes but soon as he saw I had a normal TSH he was done discussing it! I asked about T3 & T4 but he gave me the standard "if your TSH is normal everything is fine". Whatever...

On a positive note, my consult with Dr. Kruse is Sunday. I'm doing the happy dance!!!

The cranky, irritability from the weekend has lifted. Not sure what it was. PMS? The EstroFactors? The Testo-Gain?

I have a new found respect for all you people that have to prick your fingers on a routine basis. My monitor arrived yesterday and my finger is bruised. I've never thought of myself as a wimp but Holly Hanna Batman...that hurt...and I could still feel it for a few hours after I did it. I must have done something wrong. I thought I read somewhere that if you do it on the side of your finger you would barely feel it. I have a freakin bruise! Crazy!!!

### DarleenMB — 2012-12-05T13:03:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-66451>

Did your glucometer come with one of those little finger stick thingies? Â I do the pad of my finger and change which one I use daily. Â Sometimes I hit a tender spot, sometimes I get a huge drop of blood and other times have to hit a couple of spots to get any. Â sometimes it will sting for a bit but I never have bruised myself. Â And no, I don't HAVE to do it. Â I'm tracking daily for the next couple of months while I try to get my T3/RT3 straightened out.

On the thyroid test, I ultimately found, as you may recall, it was easier just to order the stupid test direct from the lab on my own and present the findings to the PA. Â There was no arguing at that point, she just whipped out the prescription pad and gave me the cytomel.

Other than that, I've got nothin'. Â Sorry.

### PaulaRichards — 2012-12-13T10:59:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-67351>

My consult with Dr. Kruse was Tuesday evening and it was great (how could it not be great; spending an hour talking to a RockStar!). I was nervous though and donâ€™t think I phrased one single question properly and am sure I sounded like an idiot. Oh well. I learned a lot and things made sense. It would certainly help to have better labs but given the doctors and lack of private labs in Canada Iâ€™ve got to work with what we have. Ferritin is a bit high, B12 is good, high TSH, Glucose is good, Hormones show Iâ€™m headed to menopause, vitamin D should be higher, canâ€™t quite remember but I think my triglycerides should be lower. So based on sketchy labsâ€¦what did I learnâ€¦

â€¢ Iâ€™m fixableâ€¦not a complete train wreck but train wreck-ish in areas.
â€¢ Probably dealing with pregnenolone steal syndrome on top of peri-menopause heading to menopause. And hypothyroidism, probably not Hashiâ€™s.
â€¢ Iâ€™m a slave to my cravings but I can fix it. Eat Fat. Just Do IT! Eat butter, drink olive oil, eat coconut oil or bacon fat, etc. JUST. DO. IT. After 4-6 weeks Iâ€™ll be well on my way. Leptin Rx to fix this and Epi-Paleo to keep this freakin train on the tracks

Given my lack of access to BHRT Iâ€™ll have to be a little more patient to in order to see results.

Focus on LIGHT, DIET, and embrace the damn Canadian COLD.

Raise dopamine level to make better decisions. Upon waking: LIGHT > BOB > BAB

Looking forward to fixing ME! Thanks Dr. Kruse!

### PaulaRichards — 2012-12-17T11:10:49+0200

All is well. Iâ€™m wearing the uvex earlier in the evening now but I have to get to bed earlier, itâ€™s been close to 11pm lately and Iâ€™m used to going around 10pm.

Discovered I like coconut butter in the solid form. I used to melt it and mix with chocolate. Not sure if this is a good discovery but Iâ€™m liking it and it definitely helps kill the craving when one shows up!

On a completely unrelated topic. My father is a type 1 diabetic and has always had issues with hypoglycemic reactions, to the point where my mother has had to call the ambulance. Over the years Iâ€™ve been telling him about low carb and buying him different books, which he does read but never wanted to give up the bread & potatoes! After seeing Dr. Davies on Dr. Oz he asked to read Wheat Belly again and read Good Calories, Bad Calories. He is giving up the carbs. Actually he has given up everything except his glass of milk. He was a dairy farmer and loves his milk. We grew up on raw milk. Heâ€™s a bit frustrated because he expected his sugars to instantly drop to normal, which they havenâ€™t, but I think heâ€™s going to stick with it. Now I just have to get him to reading material on the thyroid because he wants to have surgery for carpal tunnel but Iâ€™m thinking itâ€™s a symptom of his thyroid. Heâ€™s been on synthroid for years and the docs here dose according toâ€¦you guessed itâ€¦TSH! They never test T3 or rT3.

Happy Monday. One week till Christmas Eve. I love this time a year. Tree is up and everything is decorated! Itâ€™s really the only part of winter I enjoy. Although I plan to start enjoying the cold winter sometime soonâ€¦come on cold adaptation!

### PaulaRichards — 2012-12-20T09:19:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-68051>

Iâ€™m tired. More tired than Iâ€™ve been in quite some time. My son, who turned 13 on Tuesday, was given tickets to a concert for his birthday. He didnâ€™t get home until 11:30pm. Donâ€™t good for any of us but he had a good time and has never done something like that before.

My friend, a hematologist, is visiting for the holidays. He practices in the states now but used to work here. When he visited last year he did a few days in our cancer centre so Iâ€™m hoping heâ€™s doing the same thing this time because that means he still has privileges here and Iâ€™ll be hitting him up for the labs I want!!! Fingers crossed!

Iâ€™ve noticed over the last couple of weeks that Iâ€™m not nearly as cold in the evenings. Last night I was actually wearing a t-shirt and wasnâ€™t shivering. A good sign I think.

Taking DS and his friends to the Hobbit on Saturday for DS's birthday. Oh how I should be staying away from the theatre (because the popcorn speaks lovingly to me) but it's what ds had planned for months. Perhaps if I stuff myself with enough fat I'll make the right decision. DARLEEN...send me your "Did not want popcorn!" energy!

Everyday I receive an email message from "Notes from the Universe", it's a great way to start the day. Anyway, I just wanted to share today's message:
Haven't all the years you've spent being Santa Claus been more fun than all the years you spent waiting for Santa Claus?

And not just for the joy you've helped place on your favorite faces, but for your ability to act instead of wait. Acting makes the suspense bearable. It gives you a starring role. Time passes more quickly. And best of all, it makes possible your dance with life as you network and mingle with other dancers.

And so it is in all things, especially those dreams that have placed their trust in you... act, don't wait.

### PaulaRichards — 2012-12-21T09:09:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-68113>

I never eat late anymore but I had to last night.Â  I was out finishing Christmas shopping and didnâ€™t get home until 8:00pm.Â  I knew it was too late but just had to do it.Â  Funny thing was I had just bought bacon wrapped scallops, thinking weâ€™d have them some night during xmas.Â  Nope!Â  Didnâ€™t even make it out of the grocery bag to the freezer; went straight to the oven! OMGâ€¦So GOOD!Â  Iâ€™m pretty sure I could live on them and maybe if they werenâ€™t $1/piece I would.Â  They were Nova Scotia scallops, thatâ€™s where I live,Â  why so expensive? But SO GOOD. Did I mention they were good!!! I think itâ€™s safe to say my journey onto the Epi-Paleo track has started. Finally!

Happy Friday!Â  4 more sleeps!!!!Â

### PaulaRichards — 2012-12-22T09:53:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-68171>

So went to bed too late.Â  I usually have no desire to get out of bed on the weekends but I woke at 7:00 and despite trying for more than an hour couldn't fall back to sleep.Â  FollowedÂ my dopamine rx...light...BOB...BAB!Â  Have a slight headache though...not liking that...too much to do today, including seeing the Hobbit.Â  I guess it's time I shut of the Christmas tunes, shut down the laptop and get to work.

### PaulaRichards — 2012-12-26T10:04:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-68423>

I felt a cold coming on the morning of Christmas Eve and apparently the mega doses of everything, including vit d, vit c, etc has not helpedÂ 

. Felt like total crap yesterday. I don't think I slept a wink on Xmas eve. In hindsight I think it was cold fx, which I have never taken before but I was determined not to be sick! Well, I will never take that stuff again. Obviously I am sensitive to going ginseng or something in it. Thankfully I slept last night but still feel like I have an elastic band around my head from my sinuses being so congested. Boohoooo!!! Â I love the relaxation of Boxing Day. Nothing is open here in Nova Scotia and I love it. We usually visit my grandmother but she's 92 and I do not want to bring her this cold virus.Â 

I'm done crying over my stuffy nose. Â After all, I do have a new iPad to play with. Obviously my favourite gift! I'm not materialistic but I sure do love it!!!! Can't wait to use it for the new webinar! But what's up with the darn auto spell thing, holy crap...it's driving me insane already!! It completely changes my words to things don't even remotely make sense.Â 

Well...gotta go blow my nose.Â 

Have a great day everyone!!!

Anyway it was great because you can preset the timer to shut off so once the evening meditation was over I was almost asleep and just had to remove the headphones and off to sleep I went. Now...the dream...beyond odd! Have lost some of the details but what I do remember...I was having an affair with a very young member of the Ewing family (remember Dallas) and someone was trying to shoot me after they found me with the young stud muffin in the barn...

Â 

So, dream interpreters, put a positive spin on that one!!!

Other than that, feeling much better today. The last two day I had lost my appetite from the cold but today I was back to craving my BAB. Half pound of bacon, couple eggs, etc. YUM! Had a bit of headache and thinking it was because I didn't have a coffee yesterday. I think that's a sign I need better coffee.

### PaulaRichards — 2013-01-02T11:58:22+0200

Ok...just experienced why you gals are frustrated with posting. I could not post all morning and the only way I got the window to open was to hit quote and then delete so this might look like a quote but WTH!

First day back to work since Christmas. Yuck!

Feeling tired, stiff and sore. Perhaps from the potatoes and popcorn I had during the past week. Thankfully no bloating. Stiff neckâ€¦maybe too much time spent looking at the ipadâ€¦oh well, novelty will wear off soon! Waking up tired. Need to get to bed earlier than 11pm. 10:00 seems to be perfect but fell out of the routine early December.

173 lbs to start 2013, up about 10 lbs from this time last year but thatâ€™s irrelevant so why am I even wasting the keystrokes??!!!

Nothing earth shattering to report. BAB as usual. Will start face dunks tonight in preparation for the tub. Also going to ice my neck, hopefully that will help with the stiffness.

Anyone use Natural Calm magnesium powder? I use the citrus flavor and opened a new bottle last night which definitely does not taste the same as it has tasted for the past few months. It tasted so â€œdifferentâ€￾ that I double checked the label on the bottle. Anyone ever experience this? I have used Natural Calm. Taste was never an issue since I put it in hot water prior to steeping my green tea. The tea completely hides any taste of the Natural Calm. Plus, the maker of Calm suggests it works better using hot water, so I kill 2 birds with one drink.

Also, I started using a product sold by Vitacost called Natural Tranquiilty Magnesium ... looks and acts like a private label version of Calm at about the price of Natural Calm. http://www.vitacost.com/vitacost-natural-tranquility-magnesium

It's just pretty darned amazing. Luckily where I have my chaise is out of view of anyone unless they're walking through the field across the creek or sneaking up on my blind side. But since it's cold enough to freeze the balls off a brass monkey I don't worry about it. It feels deliciously naughty in addition to the wonderful feeling of having the sun all over me. BOY do I like doing it! If you can find a spot where you feel comfortable ... GO for it!

### PaulaRichards — 2013-01-06T21:09:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-69669>

It's late, I don't feel like typing and there's not much to tell, except...I haven't been on the scale in three days. Why is that noteworthy??? It's unheard of for me. It's part of my normal routine, I get on the scale just before getting into the shower. I kinda like this! Feels sorta freeing because there's always some dishearted feeling when the numbers never change or go up! I wonder how long I can resist?

### endless — 2013-01-06T22:15:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-69690>

### PaulaRichards — 2013-01-11T09:31:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-70291>

First time I measured blood ketones in the morning was today. They were 1.8. From what Iâ€™ve been reading ideal is between 0.5 â€“ 3.0. I can usually tell when Iâ€™m in ketosis so why am I testing??? Maybe because itâ€™s something that I â€œcanâ€ actually test. Many things I want to test I canâ€™t get from the Dr and with no private labs here Iâ€™m sh\*t out of luck. So Iâ€™m testing ketones, at least thatâ€™s one part of the puzzle I know I have right. Iâ€™m in ketosis.

But, holy sweet hanna I feel like crap today. Tired, tired, tired. Headachy, kinda stiff and sore. I donâ€™t feel like Iâ€™m getting sick or anything.

That was yesterdayâ€™s post which I didnâ€™t get to post because I had no luck getting a window to open in order to post it!

Besides being tired yesterday I became cranky as the day progressed and by the evening I was a real â€œyou know what!â€ I do not like this. That negative, cranky, critical mindset has been out of the picture for months and I donâ€™t want it back. Go away!!!

I ate scallops andâ€¦thatâ€™s rightâ€¦andâ€¦shrimp. Hubby had a delicious concoction made when I got home. Not sure how protocol-ish they were because they tasted sweet. Apparently he cooked them in some wine, which turned out to be a sweet wine.
Didn't sleep great last night, I remember waking at least twice. No problem getting back to sleep but from the look of my eyes I definitely need uninterupted sleep! Maybe it was sugar from the wine?!!! Sigh...of well...it's Friday!!!

### endless — 2013-01-11T11:48:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-70296>

Coudn't have been too much sugar in the dinner, if you are in ketosis this morning...so that's good! Wish I had a hubby to make me yummy seafood dishes!

### Charlotte — 2013-01-11T13:40:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-70308>

### PaulaRichards — 2013-01-11T15:52:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-70325>

Yup, I know about the salt thing. No lack of it in my diet. It wasn't like the carb-flu, I was just feeling like I could rip someone's eyes out. Sounded kinda harsh! I think my negativity yesterday and still some today is a combination of things. I'm doing what I can to improve my body, mindset, etc, you know...the whole package and it's starting to work..but I think the past few days I've let the negative energy & mood from a particular person suck the life out of me. Even though I am determined not to let myself be affected by that person I find myself having negative conversations (in my head...the pretend kind...I'm losing it!) to that person and I end up feeling bitter...and...enter the cranky irritable me! Deep breath...deep breath...

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-11T16:56:00+0200

### PaulaRichards — 2013-01-11T22:15:54+0200

Well tonight was an exercise in futility!!! Went to the mall, which I hate, but hey, every few years one needs new dress pants! It is next to impossible to get pants that fit my thighs and waist. My thighs are quite large so IF I can get pants that aren't skin tight in the legs they will HAVE to be altered in the waist. Anyway...nope...nadda...not one pair of pants were fitting these thighs tonight! So, since I could use a pair of boots I thought i'd give that a go. Never had a problem with boots before, except of couse I could never consider a pair of those riding type boots because of the thick legs again, they'd never fit. That's okay because there are some decent short boots. Yup...nope!!! Couldn't even get boots tonight that would zip up past my ankle. I must have the thickest legs ever! Big sigh! Whatever! I'm going to bed. New day when I wake up.
