I buy slacks from Lands End .com. They are (wait for it!) Starfish pants. { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

They are the most comfortable, best looking slacks I've EVER owned. They fit like a dream, they are fully stretch but do NOT look it. Wash up easily, throw in the dryer (but do NOT over do it, I take mine out when they're still just a tad damp), press them and they look like top dollar duds.

When we went to Ireland I bought 4 pair to take with me. The wear like iron because I just ditched them a few months ago and bought new ones. They still looked really good but I got a tear in one pair, another I over dried so they're like 2 sizes smaller than originally and one pair was getting a bit thin. They still fit my fat behind even though I'd gained 30 pounds since I bought them. I did go up to my current size with the new ones though.

Oh and they do NOT look like stretch pants! I'm serious. They fit so well I still cannot believe it.

### diane — 2013-01-12T12:41:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-70399>

Paulalynn - have been there for those frustrating shopping days. Then I realize that I spend my time trying to fit into what I think I should be able to fit into, and when I accept what I actually fit into, it's much more relaxing LOL.

Someone at work showed me a trick for comfortable "dress pants". Lululemon yoga pants. Oh my god - changed my life immediately. Just pair with a dressy top or sweater and shoes/boots - and they really do look like dress pants. I didn't even know that's what she was wearing until she pointed it out. I've done it since then - amazing! I know - everyone says that Lululemon is so expensive, but I have my first pair that I bought 10 years ago. 10 years! They're still black, still in shape. How can you beat that!

And the boots? Just consider how much money you saved yourself!

Although I confess, I love boots too. My favourite pair right now are my Blundstones that I bought last year. Oh my god. So comfortable and I wear them everywhere.

Darleen - I'm going to check out those Lands End pants too!

### caroline — 2013-01-14T20:46:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-70765>

Great job Paula - I don't know as it gets easier - sometimes - but you do feet better and you are accomplishing so much! My tap water comes out at 75 degrees. It is impossible to get it below 60. So now I just lay in the tub and cover myself with the bags of ice. I don't know if it is the best but I do wind up shivering quite a bit.
I think when you extend yourself like this - the food and other parts of the R/X becomes easier because you don't want to wreck all the good work you are doing - a snowball effect I guess.

### PaulaRichards — 2013-01-15T21:06:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-70958>

Didn't sleep well last night. It was the first time in months that I woke to go pee. Can't really be related to my first cold bath? Woke again at 5:30 with terrible back pain. The kind that made me wonder if I was going to be able to get out of bed. That hasn't happened in many years. I took a robaxacet and managed to get to work. It's still not right and boy do I NOT have patience for a bad back!!!
Spoke to my compounding pharmacist guy about my crappy mood and I am going to quit the supplement I was taking for testosterone. The thinking is that the adreno supp I was taking (which works on some HPG axis??...or something like that) may have helped improve the low testosterone situation and now the testo-supp is not needed. He said my symptoms sounded like the "testy" symptoms that come with high testosterone. We'll see what happens!

### PaulaRichards — 2013-01-16T09:06:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-71016>

>

Krista, I was easily irritated for the better part of 10 years and it seemed that anything & everything could tick me off and I tried many things to resolve this part of, what I thought was my personality. Once I found out my hormones were basically non-existent and I had symptoms of estrogen dominance I started on supplements to address this (suggested by my compounding pharmacist). The difference in my mood was remarkable, it didnâ€™t happen overnight but in a month or two I felt like a different person! I should have left well enough alone but I thought maybe it was possible to feel even better so we decided to start the testosterone supp since my saliva showed that was low too (however, I had already been on the other supps for 4 months by that time). Iâ€™m grasping at straws, since my crystal ball isnâ€™t working, but in hindsight, given my change in moods, I donâ€™t think I needed to add the testo suppl. Weâ€™ll see what happens.

Thanks for the insight on CT and soreness. My back feels much better today.

### diane — 2013-01-16T12:41:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-71042>

Paula - I also had noticed mood changes over the past few years. Wasn't sure what it was -whether I was just getting grumpier, life was wearing on me, or (gasp) was I turning into my mother???

Changing my diet had a huge affect on my mood. A couple of years ago I went to a holistic allergist and she told me a list of things I was allergic to. Within 2 days of eliminating them (and believe me that list was LONG) I felt better. Things that would grate on my nerves didn't bother me as much. Then, changing the light at nights, eating earlier - that brought around even more changes in my mood. It's crazy the things that change that you least expect! And I haven't even started any hormone therapy yet!

### PaulaRichards — 2013-01-18T19:45:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-71502>

So much to catch up on here when you miss a day! I didn't get to the Q &A; 9:00 pm really isn't that late but it kind of is for me right now. I've really been concentrating on getting to bed earlier since I had my consult with JK. I'm sure I missed some great info but my head is hurting just trying to figure out the latest supps and dosages from the last webinar! I think I ordered everything except the L carnitine. Will work on finding that one.
My joints are hurting like never before. CT is going to help improve this right? This is new for me. I go for a walk and feel it for days, I've been limping from pain in my hip region and my wrist just hurt so much while cleaning the bathroom. Weird!
Finally got hubby to try Mg at night because his sleep is so bad. Tonight, after having it for the last five nights he tells me...not tonight...apparently he was strapped to the toilet most of the day...oops!

I could have worn something warmer but I wanted to big ole legs to freeze LOL!

I gotta say I love being back to the old forum. I find I read many more threads this way and it's easier to find where I stopped reading. I love the "first unread post" option. Perhaps there was something similar on the other version but I could never make it work!

Are you keeping track of your CT temps? I didn't get pink skin much over 55F, and not really till I got the nerve up to go under 50...now I'm under 40 and it's not pink, it's lobster red! But it's not white, so I'm ok. I like doing CT walks in yoga pants...my thighs are usually burning cold and pink when I get home too....believe it or not, it's less uncomfortable in the water than on the walk!

### PaulaRichards — 2013-01-28T19:00:43+0200

Monday...yuck! My body feels yucky too. I had a scratchy throat and an annoying cough yesterday and felt like I was fighting "something". Still feel crappy today. This must be the third time in a month I've felt like this. Is my body going to react this way everytime I CT??? (rheutorical!!!). Cranky, cranky, cranky! Maybe it's partly because I have a friend who is keeping me posted on her daily weight loss! A friend who struggles to lose weight like me. Well, she read "The Plan" by Lyn-Genet, or something like that and thinks I should try it too. She knows what I'm doing and she doesn't disagree or anything but she knows I've not lost weight...she think's she's found the answer because it's not "a diet" and is basically an elimination thing aimed to reduce inflammation, etc. So she's sending me things like "lost 0.8 lbs after my beef test with a porterhouse steak"! I should be happy for her, right?!! BUT given my cranky, irritable mood of late, I'm thinking, "Great, she'll look good this summer at the beach and I'll still be sportin' rolls & cellulite!"
Alrighty...pity party over (stealing words from Darleen)!

### PaulaRichards — 2013-01-31T19:31:12+0200

Endless...I spent a bit of time reading the forum yesterday but, as much as I hate to admit it publically,...hehe...I enjoyed way too many episodes of Vampire Diaries...my brain must be mush by now...but it's okay...the addiction is over...I'm all caught up to live TV. It can't be an addiction if I only watch once a week, LOL!

I'm feeling better and back to work today. Not 100% but I can breath clearly and my head is better. I didn't CT while sick. On a positive note, the scale moved....down....4lbs...unheard of for moi! I could understand it if I had the flu but even with my lack of appetite I still made myself have protein drinks made with 1/2cup of HWC, which I had 3 each day. So, sure I was around 1200-1300 calories, which even though that is like starvation mode for me  the scale still never moves. I'll take it...perhaps a short-lived drop but we'll see. It does have me wondering if I'm eating something my body doesn't want me to be eating, hence the "damn scale never moves"! Hmmm...will be giving this some thought

### Zorica Vuletic — 2013-02-05T06:02:04+0200

And if he is right about the theory and his practice is right then that's fine too.

I question a lot of things these days....BUT I am very positive about the magnets versus the grounding (unless if it's natural grounding like feet on grass/mud/snow/trees). Something about the magnets seems 'right'. Also I know Jack puts more emphasis on magnets under the bed versus the grounding wire.

I can understand how it is confusing with EMF things. It is something tricky whereby you can remove all YOUR EMF but still have to deal with the rest of the populations who have their high EMF polluting your space. It's a balance thing for sure and it's tricky sometimes.

I have some experimentations in the next 6 months that are being sprung upon me out of my control...so instead I decided to treat it like an 'experiment'. Not what I wanted (in some regards) but at least I am not completely ungaruded in terms of education. If it was in the past I would have been unprepared and not even knowing what I'm getting into. But this time for this situation (which it's a trade off and there are MANY good aspects to this situation) at least I DO have knowledge/education and a better understanding of myself/body based on previous trial and errors so I hope this 'run' of experiment goes better than it could under normal circumstances.

Wish me luck!

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-75022>

Still laying in bed, feeling kind of lazy today. We're in the middle of a blizzard warning so it looks like it's a good day for lazy! I can't even see across the street, the wind and snow have made visability close to zero. And that concludes the weather forecast portion of today's show.

Feeling yucky (popcorn...I know, I know...tsk, tsk. Seriously....no excuse, i just did it). Moving on. I must get up and get a BAB into me or I'll be thinking about more than popcorn! Thankfully, I look forward to the BAB.

Have a good day all!

### PaulaRichards — 2013-02-10T23:54:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-75289>

Too much of "nothingness" today. I'm still in pjs and it's almost 6pm. Oh well, I blame it on our blizzard conditions for the second day in a row. No where to go so I'm taking it advantage of it. Not much of an appetite yesterday and today. I still had the typical BAB but other than that I didn't want much. I take that as a blessing because weekends like this used to be dangerous in eating department. I did re-read an old book, The Pulse Test, which is about identifying food sensitivites. Very interesting stuff. Oh, and the constant thinking about oxytocin therapy (I mentioned over on the thread) was short lived...thoughts are gone...fun while it lasted.

### endless — 2013-02-12T04:02:23+0200

### PaulaRichards — 2013-02-15T21:58:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-76011>

This week bordered on disastrous. Seemed like any negativity circulating in my mind all got together and turned me into a WTH kinda-thinking monster. I’ve resurfaced and am starting to feel somewhat “normal” again but not before eating a Tea Biscuit Tuesday morning and then French fries covered with cheddar for supper. So needless to say Wednesday was spend battling a whirlwind of food fantasies. Thankfully I made it through and yesterday wasn’t so bad.

I left eggs off the menu yesterday. I usually eat 4 eggs on weekdays, 2 with BAB and 2 for lunch. I’ve been wondering whether eggs & I don’t agree so they’ve been tossed for now. I’ve also ditched the dark chocolate a couple of weeks ago & haven’t had nuts in a long time. Thinking my precious New Zealand whey isolate needs to go too. Just feeling a bit of desperation today & want to see something change. Please, oh please, don’t let it be the HWC…or the coffee.

### DarleenMB — 2013-02-15T22:46:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-76013>

I feel your pain. Really you know I do. I tend to flop around in public and whine and cry and suck my thumb. Then it gets better.

Did you do 23andme yet? I'm laying off eggs for a while because of the genetic genie results. Also throttling back on the protein just a smidge.

I've given up on things changing. I just can't torture myself like that anymore. If I wander off in that direction I start having black thoughts, i.e., cutting calories big time to force the issue. I know it won't work but emotional desperation makes one toss logic out the window.

well that was cheerful, wasn't it.

sorry.

### PaulaRichards — 2013-02-16T00:11:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-76021>

Darleen you can always get a chuckle out of me. I _know_ you feel my pain!!! I hope you enjoyed your cheesecake as much as I enjoyed my tea biscuit, at least you didn't eat the crust. I forgot just how bad that crap makes me feel, oh well, another lesson **re-learned**!! No worries about me ever cutting calories, I can't go back there, I cringe at the thought of my old weight watcher days!

I haven't done the 23andme yet, but I should get on that.

### diane — 2013-02-16T00:38:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-76030>

For some reason, that's one test i'm not anxious to do - just feels like it's so much work to figure out what it means! Then if you have genetic defects, I would feel hopeless about changing anything. But that's just me.

### DarleenMB — 2013-02-16T00:42:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-76031>

Diane it really isn't that much work. Genetic Genie will sort through the raw data and pull out anything related to methylation issues. the information that is included with your profile is relatively straight forward and easy to read/understand. The info I have so far directly from 23andme is interesting. The only slightly elevated risks I show are for coronary disease (ain't gonna happen as long as I stick with epi=paleo) and (this made me laugh) hypothyroidism. Everything else is average to low. I'll blame that on my Viking ancestors, as well.

ETA you can then show this to your physician (operative word being SHOW and not let him/her add it to your permanent medical records) and work with them to deal with any issues.

### diane — 2013-02-16T00:47:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-76033>

Good to know Darleen!!

### endless — 2013-02-16T19:36:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-76130>

I think a lot of us are having struggles with cravings and mood lately....maybe it's just being ready for longer light cycles to start. I'd love to embrace being cold and dark but I'm craving sun, darn it! And somehow in my brain that translates to carbs.

I did the 23 and Me....I am a bit more confused about my results but maybe I have more SNPs to decipher, or maybe me brain fog is preventing me from figuring it out. Either way not much has changed because of it yet!

### DarleenMB — 2013-02-28T02:23:17+0200

### PaulaRichards — 2013-03-02T17:50:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-78613>

Geez, it's been 2 weeks?!!! Could have sworn it was only a week. No wonder I'm having trouble catching up with everyone!!!! I went off the rails a bit and it ended with an ice cream binge 7 days ago. Then frustration took over and I read a new book a friend of mine has been bugging me to read. The Plan. Basically an anti-inflammatory WOE combined with an elimination diet. So, I decided to give it a try because I was really starting to think there's gotta be someting I'm eating that is reacting with my body and preventing any weight loss. I haven't left the JK camp and I'm still eating paleo, but still not epi-paleo. I haven't tried adding eggs or bacon yet, both of which are favourites. I suspect eggs were a big problem for me since I lost 4.9lbs this week. I know the journey isn't about the weight loss but I can't lie, it feels good to feel the pants getting looser! And the increase in energy is a welcome change. Anyway, it's a journey and mine continues...

### diane — 2013-03-02T18:10:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-78622>

Hi Paulalynn! Yep, everyone has their own path for sure.

I have to say, though, that after reading the last few blogs, I'm not sure I can read any of those books and believe them.  While I may not be able to understand everything in there, I'm understanding the enormity of it all and the implications.

### endless — 2013-03-02T18:21:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-78624>

Woohoo on the looser pants! Good idea to check into what you are reacting to.....I am considering going back to an AI protocol, despite my labs not really suggesting I need it, because I felt better on it.

I can relate to the ice cream binge....I did a similar thing a few days ago with sugar free gummy bears. I ate the entire bag, and then sat on the toilet in distress most of the night...if you've ever overindulged in Maltitol you'll understand why.

Good to see you posting! What is the book you've been reading?

### PaulaRichards — 2013-03-02T18:48:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-78640>

Krista, the book is "The Plan". For a quick explaination, here's the website:
[http://lyngenet.com/](http://lyngenet.com/)

But like Diane said, can we beleive this stuff??? I don't know. I do feel better after a week and if it was eggs or bacon, for example that was, in some way, preventing weigh loss or positive change, than I'm more than happy to have discovered that fact and lost a few pounds while doing so!

I can honestly say I have not been able to connect all the dots from everything I've read on the blogs but I'm trying! I know there's enormous implications and hoping each small change I make will come together to create more positive changes!

Oh, and Krista, I feel your pain on the maltitol issue! It was years ago when I was low carb that I ate things with sugar alcohols, it was a hard lesson but I avoid the stuff like crazy now!

### DarleenMB — 2013-03-02T20:17:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-78660>

EWW on the sugar alcohols. Man I learned the hard way my body does not tolerate them AT ALL. Plus I gain weight on them the same as I do sugar so what's the point. Easy to give them up when you figure THAT out.

I got back on the dark chocolate train about 10 days ago. I'd had a stash sitting in the "bread" box for months and now it's all gone. I have some 100% sitting in there and I think I may try a nibble since I no longer like anything below 90%+ and even that's a bit much. Weird how tastes change.

GLAD YOU'RE BACK!

### caroline — 2013-03-03T00:24:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-78683>

Glad you are back Paula - great news about the weight loss! I am long suspected eggs and bacon for me isn't good.....

### PaulaRichards — 2013-08-24T17:44:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-93610>

6 months! Has it really that long?!?! Someday I'm going to want to come back and read about my obstacles and successes so it's time for a success update!!!

After so, so, so long of doing all the things suggested I hesitate to say it, BUT, I think I've figured out the Puzzle of ME! I'm down 25lbs. 25lbs!!!! And most days am feeling great. I knew there was 'something' preventing me from dropping the weight. It took a bit of effort figuring it out but it was food. The effort was beyond worth it!!!

I'm still eating 2000+ calories most days! Sometimes not because I'm simply not hungry. Cravings are gone. I haven't binged in months and don't even think about a binge! I feel free!! I must admit I haven't bid farewell to popcorn entirely but when I do indulge it doesn't drive me over the deep end.

I shed a quick tear when I realized pork was a no-no but I don't miss it too much. It was harder to bid farewell to chocolate but I've adjusted. I also discovered that I can eat eggs but not everyday.

So if anyone is still struggling and scratching their heads in dismay don't give up!!!!

### caroline — 2013-08-25T02:34:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-93656>

Hi Paula! I am so happy to see your post.....congrats on the successes.

I can't eat eggs every day either - don't understand that - but that's okay .....and I really don't do well if I eat chocolate.
So you don't eat bacon anymore either? I don't do well with bacon either.

It is amazing when you get control back .....I love it! Being craving free is wonderful.

Do you find that you just need to eat more simply? That really works for me.

Please pop back in and tell us more .....it helps other women who are really struggling here and can't seem to get a handle on it .....

Life is good?

### PaulaRichards — 2013-08-25T21:37:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-93730>

Hi Caroline! Life is good!

I do eat quite simply now. Still high fat and very low carb but it's so easy when I'm not battling cravings. I haven't officially 'tested' any seafood yet so I can't say I'm epi-paleo yet. I'm still not exercising and that's not a complaint! At the beginning of the summer I got some CT in when the ocean was 60 degrees but it's up to 72 now so my CT is over until I get my butt in the bathtub but I've never been able to get disciplined enough to do that on a regular basis. I drink more red wine than perhaps I should but other then that the weight loss became almost effortless once I figured out the pork and chocolate. And believe weight loss was never easy.

I'm still not exercising and that's not a complaint! At the beginning of the summer I got some CT in when the ocean was 60 degrees but it's up to 72 now so my CT is over until I get my butt in the bathtub but I've never been able to get disciplined enough to do that on a regular basis. I drink more red wine than perhaps I should but other then that the weight loss became almost effortless once I figured out the pork and chocolate. And believe weight loss was never easy.

How exactly did you get rid of the cravings? Did you only increase your fat intake or did you also increase protein and decrease carb? I'm underweight but I'm struggling with hunger (not cravings for particular things) and it annoys me that I can't focus because of it.

### caroline — 2013-08-26T01:44:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-93767>

### PaulaRichards — 2013-08-26T04:35:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-93788>

I'm very low carb, high fat and moderate protein. I did struggle with not being able to lose weight for years but once i figured out what i was reacting to (pork and chocolate) everything seemed to fall into place. I thankfully stumbled upon the book, The Plan and it definitely helped me eliminate my problem foods.

> I'm very low carb, high fat and moderate protein. I did struggle with not being able to lose weight for years but once i figured out what i was reacting to (pork and chocolate) everything seemed to fall into place. What is this book you speak of? sounds really interesting.....

### PaulaRichards — 2013-08-27T21:49:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-93937>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=93871)
>
> What is this book you speak of? sounds really interesting.....

"The Plan" by Lyn-Genet Recitas. I felt like she wrote it for me. The basic premise is that we're not all the same and our chemistry is different. Just because bacon works for one person doesn't mean it's going to work for me. It was very interesting. It was a lot of work during the first few weeks but it was SO worth it!

### caroline — 2013-08-28T02:09:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-93968>

Wow...over a year! Is it even appropriate to post now?!?
Seems I've stayed 'On the Wagon' for over a year now. Down 40+lbs and have never felt better in my life, physically and or emotionally! Carpel tunnel went away...haven't worn a wrist support in about a year, arthritis-type feeling in a few fingers disappeared, no more hot flashes, regular periods, the list goes on.....
Thank you Jack Kruse!

### caroline — 2014-12-28T00:32:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-151073>

Good morning from Oz Paula! looking forward to more details about what has been working for you. Lots of newbies here who would love to hear your story - when you have time!

### PaulaRichards — 2015-01-02T00:01:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-151329>

Well, winter temps have finally hit Nova Scotia. Ski hill opens Saturday, yay!!!
Caroline, I will definitely work on a summary of my story. It certainly took a few years but it really all came together this past year and. I've never felt better. I'll never forget JK's words during my consult....'you're not a total train wreck but you're definitely off the rails', he's the best!
In short, I was feeling good following what I was learning here but just couldn't figure out why the weight was barely budging. I never did well on carbs and that was a given. Even squash triggered binges. Anyway, Long story short, I determined that I couldn't eat pork. That was a biggie. After years, the weight starting coming off...effortlessly...even surpassing my goal. I could no longer go 'shopping in my own closet'...which is fun...but it was more fun when that was no longer an option.
Happy New Year!

### caroline — 2015-01-02T00:50:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-151332>

Happy new Year Paula! can't wait for a summary!!

### PaleoMom — 2012-03-25T07:50:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/but-sherpas-have-a-high-starch-diet.421/post-5720>

In CT 4 Jack talks about the Sherpas eating a more keto diet:

"They also ate a very different diet than the rest of India. It was one that was heavily skewed to animal protein and fats. No carbs really grow that high up or in those temperatures with any regularity."

[http://www.sherpatrek.com/culture/sherpa-food.php](http://http://www.sherpatrek.com/culture/sherpa-food.php)

[http://www.everyculture.com/wc/Mauritania-to-Nigeria/Sherpas.html](http://http://www.everyculture.com/wc/Mauritania-to-Nigeria/Sherpas.html)

[http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GxqoGYW36So\[/video\]\]\[video=youtube\_share;GxqoGYW36So\]http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GxqoGYW36So\[/video\]](http://[video=youtube_share;GxqoGYW36So)

I don't see any evidence to back that up. Wim Hof also does not eat a keto diet and he certainly is cold adapted. I'm not saying Wim is optimal, but he is getting tons of benefit from the cold still and is very lean.

### janagram — 2012-03-25T23:10:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/but-sherpas-have-a-high-starch-diet.421/post-5997>

well as I recall from reading, the Tibetan diet was a lot of meat, dried cheese, butter, and some barley....not much grows up there....of course in recent times, change comes to all original diets....in my understanding, potatoes are from the new world....so sure they get around...

### Jack Kruse — 2012-03-25T23:58:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/but-sherpas-have-a-high-starch-diet.421/post-6024>

What is common to the Inuit and the Sherpa's? They are the only humans who constantly live in the cold the last 25K yrs.

What is not common to them? One has access to the sea and the other does not.

So that means that one has access to the ideal source of food and one has to put something together to make up for it.

Following yet.

The Sherpa are good.......but not as good as the Inuit were in 1900. go read the account that was left on CT 6 by dr.bass or Dr. Schweitzer original papers on the Inuit..........what he said about them was hard to believe.

And remains that way because since their diet was polluted by the SAD in 1950 we have NO HUMANS who live in the perfect environment and who eat the perfect diet..........

So modern humans remain completely numb to the reality of the Ancient Pathway..........All biochem textbooks were written AFTER the Inuit diets were destroyed........so their adaptations were never studied.

Until NASA went to the moon in 1969, until Mark Spitz, Armstrong, Phelps, Sherpas........were rediscovered doing some amazing things.

got it?

### PaleoMom — 2012-03-26T01:25:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/but-sherpas-have-a-high-starch-diet.421/post-6092>

I'm not arguing if cold is good or not. I'm also not arguing whether the Inuit are amazing specimens or not. What I am wondering is why you stated the Sherpas eat a keto diet (no cites related to Sherpas on that post) when I could only find information stating the traditional diet of the Sherpas to be starch heavy.

It is fine to say that the Sherpas are healthy in spite of their diet because of the cold, because the cold is like a compensating factor for the lack of marine foods the Inuit have. However, stating they are healthy and live long lives because of the cold + keto (without a source to prove that) makes me question the how true this may be. The evidence that I see seems to be showing a culture that has cold plus starch. I understand that this is probably a diet that wasn't with them thousands of years ago, but how are they doing now healthwise on starch and do we have evidence of their health, endurance etc. from before starches were brought up the mountain?

Please, also understand that I am NOT trying to argue for the sake of arguing. I very much hate rocking the boat. I do want to fully understand why I should be eating keto vs. lower carb (say under 100) when I feel terrible at that carb level in spite of being paleo for about 6 years and being fat adapted. I also have great energy, sleep well and don't have any hormonal troubles. I have read the blog and have heard all the science behind the theory, however I don't see real life examples of people thriving with an active lifestyle on a keto diet in the cold.

### chewingthefat — 2012-03-26T01:58:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/but-sherpas-have-a-high-starch-diet.421/post-6105>

I understand your questions. I, too, have questions in regard to the necessity of keto if you are long-term paleo, no leptin problems, no weight issues and eat an occasional good starch. Is it going to take away all benefits? I just feel better with occasional good starch, ie sweet potatoes one night a week and rice or quinoa two nights a week? Should I not delve into CT if I don't slide into complete keto?

### PaleoMom — 2012-03-26T02:08:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/but-sherpas-have-a-high-starch-diet.421/post-6114>

> **chewingthefat;6140 wrote:** I understand your questions. I just feel better with occasional good starch, ie sweet potatoes one night a week and rice or quinoa two nights a week? Should I not delve into CT if I don't slide into complete keto?

Wim Hof has had lots of scientific tests done on himself that prove amazing things happening to him, yet he eats a regular diet, drinks alcohol etc. It _may_ be true that eating keto + CT is better, but there is certainly evidence in Wim that doing CT with any diet is better than not doing CT. I really enjoyed his book "Becoming the Iceman". Very peaceful, happy guy that just wants to share the gift of health from CT with the world.

### chewingthefat — 2012-03-26T02:38:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/but-sherpas-have-a-high-starch-diet.421/post-6141>

I saw him on youtube. Amazing.

I understand the circadian aspect of CT and the need for the body to have balance in seasonal and light cues. I've been tuned into this for a long time.

I also know how my body feels and the fact that I was horribly fatigued after several months of a strict keto and no starch diet. Lost too much weight. And I got a serious sinus infection. Cycle stopped. Made me stop and think about what I was doing wrong. Started eating starches and I felt better. ?

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-26T02:44:55+0300

There are two phases I think - one is "becoming adapted", and then the "adapted". Dr. K said he eats seasonally now (when not n-1 experimenting) but that to assist in our transition to being cold adapted our cells need keto. That was my take-away on it.

### PaleoMom — 2012-03-26T02:48:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/but-sherpas-have-a-high-starch-diet.421/post-6152>

Many years ago I read a book called "The Three Season Diet" by John Douillard. I don't back the non-paleoness of it, but the seasonal part I think is great. It is about the same as Dr. K is saying. With my paleo slanted view of the book, the advice was eat lots of fruit and veggies in late summer/early fall, in winter eat lots of fat and meat, in spring eat low fat (animals have leaned out considerably) lots of greens and generally less calories (not much plant matter is available in spring/ early summer.

### chewingthefat — 2012-03-26T04:12:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/but-sherpas-have-a-high-starch-diet.421/post-6190>

Yes, I read that book, too. It made a lot of sense to me at the time. As a gardener, I noted the availability of various foods at different times and season. Potatoes of all sorts, onions, winter squash, and root vegetables are available for "forage-ing" long after the snow flies. These used to be my go-to foods for late fall and winter. And it felt right eating them.

I know we are striving for earlier than the agricultural era, but I'm afraid I am running out of food to eat. No safe starches, no nightshades, no eggs (Hashimotos), no dairy (another sensitivity)...none of the obvious... no wheat (celiac), no corn, no rice, no grains, no quinoa, no berries, no nuts (mild sensitivity). What am I supposed to eat? I know...meat, fat, greens and other veggies. But no greens or veggies grow naturally during the winter, at least here? The strange thing is, I'm not hungry. But I don't need to lose any more weight.

### PaleoMom — 2012-03-26T05:30:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/but-sherpas-have-a-high-starch-diet.421/post-6224>

> **chewingthefat;6225 wrote:**
>
>
>
> I know we are striving for earlier than the agricultural era, but I'm afraid I am running out of food to eat. No safe starches, no nightshades, no eggs (Hashimotos), no dairy (another sensitivity)...none of the obvious... no wheat (celiac), no corn, no rice, no grains, no quinoa, no berries, no nuts (mild sensitivity). What am I supposed to eat? I know...meat, fat, greens and other veggies. But no greens or veggies grow naturally during the winter, at least here? The strange thing is, I'm not hungry. But I don't need to lose any more weight.

Yes, exactly. Root veggies and squash are actually much more seasonal in the winter than greens and other veggies. And while they are certainly not something we would have had in the winter before agriculture (or very, very little of at least), I just can't see eating nothing but meat and fat and organs all winter long. Perhaps it will give me a bit longer life, however there are many thriving people that eat these things and worse in the winter and I would be plenty happy to grow old as healthy as they are.

Dr. K talks about the Monks in the "The Monk Who Sold His Ferrari" book. I'm currently reading it, yes they live up high in the mountains, but they also eat a vegetarian diet. I'm not finished with it yet, but so far the only mention of cold has been that as a punishment they will walk to an icy cold waterfall and stand under it until they can't stand it anymore. They do this to themselves if they have allowed a negative thought into their mind.

### KiwiLauren — 2012-03-26T06:24:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/but-sherpas-have-a-high-starch-diet.421/post-6238>

There are actually plenty of greens that grow in winter. I've harvested swiss chard and other similar greens from under snow in my garden. Some herbs, too. I do think, however, that in the dead of winter you drop to nothing but meat and fat you'd be fine. But if you want seasonal veggies, they do exist.

# Thread: PlaeoDentist's Journey
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/plaeodentists-journey.431/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 22
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### PaleoDentist — 2012-03-25T17:23:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/plaeodentists-journey.431/post-5802>

INTRO:

42 y/o male, 5'9 medium build.

I have been on lowish carb Paleo Robb Wolf/ Sission for 14 months 225lb->184lb . I may have 5 lbs of body fat to lose if that much. (my wife thinks I look good already and thinks any more fat loss will make me look bad, but I want better body composition. ). ALthough according tho the charts I need to be 164, which will make me look like an anorexic. I do 30 min Heavy weights once per week using Body by Science protocols, but add in a few more exercises. I have done them fasted, but now will follow Dr K's protocol. The only thing I have is major sleep issues. I wake up 3-4am and can't go back to sleep. and feel very rested on 5-6 hrs sleep. But I know I need to sleep more. I bet my cortisone is messed up. may have it tested. I ate only dinner following my hunger cues.

I began Leptin Reset 6 days ago and lost 2 lbs. I eat a BAB although not hungry. I began CT yesterday using cold tap water (not measured temp but it was chilly) did 6 - 20 sec face dunks. and spot iced one problem fat area for 30 min. skin was white not pink. I plan on following your protocol to help me shed the remaining fat, especially spot cold the one problem area and to improve my sleep- which is the main driving factor.

Attached files [](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/5836=116-Slide1-1.jpg)

### PaleoDentist — 2012-03-25T17:27:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/plaeodentists-journey.431/post-5803>

Following my BAB ( eggs, bacon, smoked salmon wrapped in nori.) I took one spoon full of Raw organic Coconut oil (Trader Joe's) 16 oz ice cold water and did face dunks.

Sink with ice got it down to 45F tested with a digital meat thermometer. The eater was very uncomfortable as compared to yesterday. I managed 6 20 sec sessions with a 10-15 sec rest in between. my face burned and it did not feel good. no shivering. face was red. spot icing 30 min on lower abdomen with icepack.

### PaleoDentist — 2012-03-27T03:49:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/plaeodentists-journey.431/post-6736>

well, as per Dr K's observation that I am not Leptin resistant, I switched to the maintenance protocol. I am continuing to iet a protein rich breakfast, just not going for the 50 gm protein. I'm at about 35 gm. I skip lunch and eat a good dinner. tonight was a big ass salad with 1 lb GF ground beef with mexican spices. folioed by face dunking in ice water that was 50F. what I noticed today is that I became used to the cold a little bit better.

### PaleoDentist — 2012-03-28T03:14:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/plaeodentists-journey.431/post-7469>

continued with face dunking today. used lots of ice. temp down to 43F water. did 5 dunks working my way form 45 sec to 70 sec. I noticed that after about 20 sec my face began to feel warm. if I moved my head I felt a lot colder.. I wonder if its just the water around my skin warming up. didn't feel bad a t all. maybe this is what it feels like during baths.

### PaleoSue — 2012-03-28T03:33:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/plaeodentists-journey.431/post-7477>

Just saying "hi" and to say best of luck with continuing the CT. Your progress is very inspirational!

### PaleoDentist — 2012-04-01T23:26:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/plaeodentists-journey.431/post-9956>

Today I tried a bath. I sat in my bathtub and started the cold water. It was 55F but then warmed up to 63F. it was up to my waist. I kept legs fully immersed. sat there for 25 min. not too bad. no shivering. I was fitting with the drain , which is leaking, so the water level started to go down after I turned the faucet off. I think I will add ice next time to get to 55-60F.

### PaleoDentist — 2012-04-07T15:49:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/plaeodentists-journey.431/post-12434>

I only missed 1 day of bath since starting 4/1. the water is 58F and I stay in 30 min immersed up top mid stomach which is basically a full bath tub. I only missed one day, but did use an ice pack on lower abdomen for 40 min on that day. so far no major improvements. but I also realize it has only been 6 days! I have a long journey ahead of me. I plan to do 30 min sessions for 2-3 weeks then up it to 45 min and then 1 hour daily at each month increment. I think 1 hour bath is going to be my limit due to the constraints. I spend my time listening to Robb Wolfs earlier podcasts. There are plenty on the Jimmy Moore site to keep me entertained for a while!

### Jack Kruse — 2012-04-07T16:25:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/plaeodentists-journey.431/post-12442>

I just posted a pic on FB......CTing at sunrise. I need optimal.

### DarleenMB — 2012-04-07T17:50:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/plaeodentists-journey.431/post-12462>

Man you folks just diving into the ice tub are braver than I am.

and I do NOT think that 164 will make you look anorexic. If you're packing extra muscle you might wind up at 170-175 and still look trim and great. The problem, I believe, is that we have all gotten so used to everyone around us being at a minimum 20-30 pounds overweight with a majority being obese, that we have lost sight of what a healthy, fit human being looks like.

Go look through photo archives from the 1800's and the early to mid 1900's. You'll see what I mean. I'll bet you'll think most of those people look like they're one meal away from starvation. But EVERYone looked like that.

My unasked for advice is to quit worrying about the scale and about body fat percentages and concentrate on how you feel. It's what I am trying whole-heartedly to do, myself. Not easy but much saner than the other way.

### PaleoDentist — 2012-04-07T22:36:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/plaeodentists-journey.431/post-12523>

Thanks for visiting!!!!!

### PaleoDentist — 2012-04-07T22:39:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/plaeodentists-journey.431/post-12524>

I'm off to Jamaica, only had time for a cold shower today. will CT there as much as possible.

### PaleoDentist — 2012-04-09T00:21:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/plaeodentists-journey.431/post-12802>

OK first day in Jamaica. Its HOT! after breakfast I attempted a cold bath. now I understand the frustration of all the southerners with the cold tap water, which is actually warm! I do not have a thermometer, but its warm. I needed 3 waste baskets full of ice to get it down to a chilly level. not sure the exact temp, but it did make my skin cold, but all the ice melted. I guesstimate it was in to low 60'sF.

did a 30 min soak. the cold shower water is barely cold. I just turned down the room thermostat to 65F from the set 72F. Hopefully the wifey won't object (much). now off to the gym...

### PaleoDentist — 2012-04-09T05:23:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/plaeodentists-journey.431/post-12900>

Well, i made a discovery at the hotel today. they have a58F cold plunge pool. so after today's full body workout I took advantage of it! considering that my cold bath attempt was pretty lame, the cold plunge pool more than made up for it. I soaked up to my neck for about 15 min. it was pretty cold! I will try to do 30 min after breakfast tomorrow.

### DarleenMB — 2012-04-11T04:35:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/plaeodentists-journey.431/post-13597>

I soaked up to my neck for about 15 min. it was pretty cold! I will try to do 30 min after breakfast tomorrow.

YAY! That's great. although I'm so jealous you're in Jamaica and we're in cold old Wyoming. sigh. But our tap water is better ... about 43 straight outta the pipe.

### PaleoDentist — 2012-04-11T23:23:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/plaeodentists-journey.431/post-13899>

I've been going to the cold plunge bath every day so far, sometimes twice (although for a brief splash the second time as I do not want to over do it)

Today was a relatively comfortable 30 min soak. I'm going to miss it for sure. leaving tomorrow.

will continue the bathtub at home.

I am seriously considering going in the ocean soon. It occurred to me when Jack mentioned that he wished he lived next to an ocean for free CT. I live 5 min form the Pacific ocean! which I have taken advantage of for year round kayaking, but now may just jump in!

### PaleoDentist — 2012-04-11T23:25:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/plaeodentists-journey.431/post-13900>

### PaleoDentist — 2012-04-14T00:33:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/plaeodentists-journey.431/post-14891>

first day back. I did not have time for breakfast, as I overslept and rushed to work. got back and filled up my late tub. it was at 59F I added a bowl of ice and got it down to 58F. sat in it for 35 min submerged to chest. as the ice cube sulfated around me and melted I began to shiver in last 5 min. drank my ice water and now shivering in my robe. I never shivered before. this is my first CT without a high fat meal. i wonder if there is a relationship?

### nonchalant — 2012-04-14T00:39:17+0300

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/plaeodentists-journey.431/post-14898>

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-workout-between-3-and-6pm.438/post-5935>

What's the reasoning behind this? What if I need to workout more than one time a day. When I go more than one time in the day, I usually do the first at 6AM and the second around 530PM. I realize long term optimal health is on the line by doing this ... and I accept that. Other than don't do it, what should I do differently to try and be optimal when I'm not working out in the 3-6 window?

Best!

Snuffy

# Thread: Youthfuliz's joyful life as a cavewoman :)
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/youthfulizs-joyful-life-as-a-cavewoman.440/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 90
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### youthfuliz — 2012-03-26T02:47:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/youthfulizs-joyful-life-as-a-cavewoman.440/post-6151>

Well, welcome all. I'm going into a cold shower so I'll keep this short.

I'm in my 30's, hoping to stay young and look like a 25 year old for as long as possible. I started to eat Paleo in the middle of 2011. Went strict Paleo in January of 2012, and have stayed in ketosis most of the time. I'm eating meat/seafood/leafy vegetables/99 percent chocolate/coconut oil/manna/butter. Lots of green tea and some coffee.

Not exercising much, but have started walking to school and back somedays now that the Spring is here. I'll pick up more sculpt/hiit in a month or so. I started leptin reset on January 26th. Now I go to sleep at 11pm, sometimes a bit earlier than that, and usually sleep to 6:30am, and I sleep very well. I dim the lights in the evening and enjoy a cold shower/bath at least every other day. My skin is becoming softer, clearer, and more elastic/youthful looking. Hair is smoother, healthier and feel silky through my fingers. I can't stop touching it!  I love touching myself all over my body too. haha. Love the cold shower. And I have been wearing less clothes and turning the room temperature down to 65F during the day and 62F at night. I cover myself with thinner blanket at night too and feel warm when I wake up.

Today's weight is 151. Started in January at 153. I have lost more inches than pounds and my legs are getting firmer. My goal weight is 138 by end of May, and maybe 132 by end of August. Or whenever I have a 26" waist. I don't really care about the weight, I just want to be lean and strong. Right now my waist is 29".

OK now I go to my cold shower.

### youthfuliz — 2012-03-26T04:02:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/youthfulizs-joyful-life-as-a-cavewoman.440/post-6184>

32 minutes of cold shower. Started at 72F, down to 61F. Cold feels good! I realized that I sing louder when it gets colder.

### youthfuliz — 2012-03-26T18:55:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/youthfulizs-joyful-life-as-a-cavewoman.440/post-6430>

150 pounds this morning.  Forgot to mention, I'm 5'5", medium frame.

### youthfuliz — 2012-03-26T19:53:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/youthfulizs-joyful-life-as-a-cavewoman.440/post-6449>

A little more history. I have always been a curvy gal - hourglass shape, about 20~30 pounds above my ideal weight. I was in the lower 130s in my 20's and felt and looked great. I'd like to go back to that body.  My highest weight was in the 160's. I tried to lose weight by eating less and exercising more. In 2007 I dropped about 20 pounds and got to 138. When I stopped exercising I slowly gained back. In 2011 I started to try again, by eating lowcarb and exercising. I found the Paleo community and started to live the Paleo way. I gave in to carb cravings from time to time, and got back to ketogenic Paleo each time within shorter time. And I really don't enjoy the "mistakes" as much as I did before. They are just the old comforts that don't really work any more, but still flash a quick flirt sometimes. After reading the CT series, and especially the part on no safe starches in the winter (or CT process) it become easier to say no to junk food. I make my own legal dessert now  home made chocolate from 2 parts of coconut manna plus 1 part of 99 percent chocolate. I have three pieces before breakfast, and two pieces before dinner.

In 2011 my weight fluctuated between 150 to 162. I have been below 153 for almost all the days in 2012, below 150 for more than half of the time. I think I have been eating too much coconut manna/chocolate in Feb and March - almost forgot that they've got calories too!  Now I eat them slowly, and try not to exceed 6 pieces a day - 10g per piece.

And no snacking has been almost natural as soon as I started leptin reset. Not really feeling hunger any more. I almost always skip lunch, but usually still eat dinner. Although I think I won't feel bad even if I don't. So I usually have a light dinner. CT about 5 times a week. Not using very cold water yet, but pretty cold for me.  One day I'll just use the cold tap water which is 48F now.

OK, this is about it. My goal for the coming month is to stay in ketosis, eat meat/seafood/my home made coconut chocolate/butter/leafy greens that're in season. And keep doing CT. I'll do more walking, but won't add in HIIT/weights until later.

### youthfuliz — 2012-04-01T20:34:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/youthfulizs-joyful-life-as-a-cavewoman.440/post-9883>

April's plan:

1\. CT at least 4 times a week.

2\. Food: BAB, Dinner: eggs or 3 pieces of my homemade dessert (coconut manna + 99 percent chocolate)

3\. Walk to school whenever the weather allows

Todays's CT:

10 minutes of cool cleaning shower, followed by 30 minutes of cold bath (70F down to 63F), followed by a quick cold shower.

### youthfuliz — 2012-04-01T20:39:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/youthfulizs-joyful-life-as-a-cavewoman.440/post-9886>

**April's weight loss goal:**

Down to 142 pounds. No more ups and downs, it will be down down down all the way.

Eat 100% ketogenic paleo, light dinner. I don't really feel hungery any more. Eating is more of a fun activity. haha. I'll find other ways to keep myself entertained.

### youthfuliz — 2012-04-03T17:47:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/youthfulizs-joyful-life-as-a-cavewoman.440/post-10790>

Today's CT:

35 minutes total. Cold bath started at 68F, down to 66F. Followed by a quick cold shower. Played Barry Manilow's Summer of 78. It eneded exactly when I finished... 38 minutes total. I'll use it to time my CT for now.

I was feeling cold before CT, sneezed quite a few times - so I had to talk myself into the cold bath and kept the water not too cold for me, although it still felt cold and I even shivered a bit, which hardly happened before - but I didn't sneeze in the bath, and only started to sneeze again after I put the bathrobe on!  Kinda funny.

Next CT is Thursday. After breakfast.

### youthfuliz — 2012-04-04T06:12:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/youthfulizs-joyful-life-as-a-cavewoman.440/post-11150>

Will travel out of town this Thursday so probably no CT until Saturday. I'll do cool showers daily though, just not cold. Cool showers do wake me up.

### youthfuliz — 2012-04-08T16:24:23+0300

40 minutes today. 66F bath followed by a quick cold shower. Feel good. Next one will be Tuesday. I'll keep the temperature at 64F - 66F this week, 40 minutes each time, 4 times (S, T, Th, S) and drop the water temperature to 62F - 64F next week.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-04-08T16:29:42+0300

### youthfuliz — 2012-04-09T02:41:22+0300

### Jack Kruse — 2012-04-09T03:16:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/youthfulizs-joyful-life-as-a-cavewoman.440/post-12853>

### youthfuliz — 2012-04-10T19:57:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/youthfulizs-joyful-life-as-a-cavewoman.440/post-13413>

Sure thing Doc. Thanks for providing us with this "cave home"!

Today's CT:

35 minutes bath. Started with 66F water, down to 63F. Spend half time immersed up to my shoulder, the other half sitting up with water up to my stomach. Finished up with a quick cold shower. I loved it when the cold shower fell on my face. Almost like a "Finally!" moment - anticipated peak moment realized.

My skin was pink when I got out of the bath. I followed what others have done - I warmed up naturally by staying naked for a little while  and it worked! Within minutes my skin returned to normal color, my body didn't feel cold without clothes, only my hands and feet still feel cold. So this is what I will do in the future. Warm up naturally.

Next one will be on Thursday.

### youthfuliz — 2012-04-13T02:55:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/youthfulizs-joyful-life-as-a-cavewoman.440/post-14507>

Today's CT:

After dinner. 35 mintues total, started with 65F bath, down to 62F. Finished with a quick cold shower... 57F! I didn't realized that it was so cold. It got me shiver a bit. My body warmed up quickly, within 10 minutes after I got out. My feet and hands are still cold and stiff, while I type this now, 15 minutes later.

But I loved it. 65F was easy to start with and although it felt cold when I got in, I felt fine within minutes. I had a full body immersion, up to my neck for more than half of the time.

I may do CT again on Friday. See if I want do it every day during the weekend. I think it's getting easier.

### youthfuliz — 2012-04-14T20:09:31+0300

### youthfuliz — 2012-04-19T14:52:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/youthfulizs-joyful-life-as-a-cavewoman.440/post-16546>

Today's CT:

40 minutes of cold shower, started at 72F, down to 65F. Took just a few minutes to get comfortable in the cold, then I turned down the temperature to 70F, 68F, then 65F. At the end 65F wasn't too cold and I felt glad to have the cold water hitting on my "warm" body. I thought that there must be oxytocin (or some other happy hormone) being released, and I see why cold showers can be addictive.

### youthfuliz — 2012-04-20T05:46:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/youthfulizs-joyful-life-as-a-cavewoman.440/post-16907>

Wearing my blueblocking sunglasses now... and almost feel it's dumb to be up at this moment. I see myself going to sleep at 10pm soon.  I felt so hot today. It was only 65F but felt like summer. I was in t-shirt and my feet were on fire the whole day. I wished I could walk barefoot in office. I had to take a quick cold shower to cool myself down when I got home. Tomorrow I'll be in skirt and sandals. I start to wonder how hot it will get this summer.

### youthfuliz — 2012-04-22T03:53:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/youthfulizs-joyful-life-as-a-cavewoman.440/post-17620>

Just finished today's CT:

52 minutes total. Cold bath followed by a quick cold shower. Started at 65F. Down to 59F. I was quite comfortable at 62F. Then 60F was fine too. It was the last 5 minutes at 59F got me shivering. But I wanted to feel "cold" so I did. After I towel dried myself and sat my naked butt on my leather chair, the chair felt warm! I walked barefoot and the floor felt warm!  Mind you, the room temperature is just 68F. Now I need to do some exercise so my feet warm up enough for me to fall asleep tonight!

### youthfuliz — 2012-04-22T04:00:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/youthfulizs-joyful-life-as-a-cavewoman.440/post-17623>

Hello Doc. Right now, after 7 minutes out of the bath, I feel fine. My body feels warm, hands and feet still cold, but I have stopped shivering... or almost stopped.  I'm glad that I tried 59F tonight, that means next time 60F is gonna be considered "easy".

### youthfuliz — 2012-04-22T15:39:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/youthfulizs-joyful-life-as-a-cavewoman.440/post-17695>

I did some leg lifts with ankle weights last night and got my feet "not cold" in a matter of minutes. They were not warm, but normal. Went bed at 11pm and although my legs/feet still felt cool to touch (my hands warmed up obviously) I had no problem feeling cozy and fell asleep. Woke up around 7:15am. I like to have 8 hours' sleep. Maybe I'll go to bed around 10:30pm and see if I wake up earlier.

I think my appetite is decreased lately. A few days ago I was in a hurry so just ate three extra large eggs cooked in co for breakfast. I didn't feel hungry until 4pm. I eat my dinner early, like 5pm, often and don't feel hungry before bed. I think I'll try to eat less - I'll keep the BAB, but eat less for dinner. Maybe just eggs instead of steak. Will see how this goes.

### youthfuliz — 2012-04-23T17:32:03+0300

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/youthfulizs-joyful-life-as-a-cavewoman.440/post-19705>

My appetite has been very good lately... I guess it's due to frequent CT. I have been very good at eating just the right food though. Stuffed myself with meat/fat/coconut manna/99 pencent chocolate today again. My face has been on fire for the whole day, while my feet have stayed cold. I guess I need to exercise a bit to warm my feet up for bed.

### youthfuliz — 2012-04-29T18:00:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/youthfulizs-joyful-life-as-a-cavewoman.440/post-19808>

Today's CT:

65 minutes total. Cold bath started at 61F, down to 56F. When it got to 58F I started to feel "cold and good", so I tried 56F for a second time. It didn't feel much different from 58F. Colder, yes, but my body didn't react much differently. Shivering a bit more, but nothing else. My tummy and inside of my thighs are still pink, now almost 20 minutes out of the bath. And my body has already warmed up, except for my hands and feet.

### youthfuliz — 2012-04-30T19:24:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/youthfulizs-joyful-life-as-a-cavewoman.440/post-20117>

I asked about the increased hunger here:

[http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?1248-CT-induced-hunger-when-will-it-pass](http://http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?1248-CT-induced-hunger-when-will-it-pass)

For now I think it should be due to estrogen, since I have seen smoother, more elastic skin, smoother hair, even fuller and firmer "girls"... which should all came from the same estrogen that made me hungry in the last few days. So here is the plan:

I'll eat 65g protein for breakfast. Continue to CT daily, but do not push for colder temperature this week. Let it stay in the range of 62F - 56F. Try 60F - 54F next week. Prepare to eat lunch and dinner. If I still feel hungry at night, the only thing allowed after 7pm is fried eggs in coconut oil.

### youthfuliz — 2012-05-03T00:55:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/youthfulizs-joyful-life-as-a-cavewoman.440/post-20847>

Posted this in another thread. [http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?524-How-was-your-CT-today&p=20920#post20920](http://http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?524-How-was-your-CT-today&p=20920#post20920)

> **Pamela;20865 wrote:** I've stepped up my next gradient to go for 1 hour soaks 4 times a week in about 55 degree water. Getting in and adapted has been much easier this past week, which is week 5 for me. I really feel ready for my soak when the day comes, I can feel it in my legs. They feel kind of swollen and then I do the soak and they feel good again. I'm working on getting my hormones fixed, will be doing blood this week for Dzugan program. Weight is staying steady while I eat a good keto paleolithic diet and enjoy all my fats. My next plan of action is to get the water a little colder, will probably have to go buy a bag of ice each day for that.

My heart beats faster when I prepare my cold bath in the morning.  I would fill the tub when I finish eating my BAB, check the water temperature, walk in the tub and let my feet feel it, then go away for 10 minutes - surf the internet, take my vitamins, fish oil, etc, drink my iced water... then wash my face in cold water, then go in the tub. This "ritual" is kinda like foreplay  that prepares me to greet the cold water with grace.

I don't multitask in the tub, I focus on the bath and make the most out of it. My arms and legs are always moving to circulate the water - it feels great when water moves against my body, almost feel like being massaged. Sometimes I use a washcloth to bring water onto my chest which doesn't always get under the water, or use the washcloth to massage my arms and legs - I figure as long as I'm in the tub let's do some scrubbing  And I bring water to my face too. It feels great to cool down my hot face with cold water. At the end I take a quick cold shower to wash off and let water drop on my face. Wonderful.

### youthfuliz — 2012-05-15T00:50:23+0300

This TOM is almost over. I feel great. Minimal cramps, no tiredness, excellent mood  I wish I had known CT since my teenage years, so many petty fights could have been spared! ### indigogirl — 2012-05-26T22:49:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/youthfulizs-joyful-life-as-a-cavewoman.440/post-27713>

Just curious...why CTing 2X a day?

### youthfuliz — 2012-05-26T23:00:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/youthfulizs-joyful-life-as-a-cavewoman.440/post-27715>

> **indigogirl;27811 wrote:** Just curious...why CTing 2X a day?
>
> Is your tap water 55F? Lucky you!

I feel unsatisfied since the tap water is warming up. It was 52F forever in the last three months and I have been slowly lowering the temperature of my bath... now I'm very comfortable with 55F, and can't get it colder since it's warming up. I really crave colder water.  I don't want to use ice since it's summer so I guess I can at least increase the time. I almost always only took cold bath in the morning since I need my feet to be warm to fall asleep. My recovery is very fast lately so I can take a cold bath in the evening and still have no problem falling asleep. I also hope the longer ct per day may speed up my weight loss a little bit.

### youthfuliz — 2012-06-25T01:47:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/youthfulizs-joyful-life-as-a-cavewoman.440/post-33375>

**OK, I'm going to challenge myself. I have a goal weight for 12/30/12... 132 pounds.**

I actually don't remember when I was 132 before, this may be too thin for me... well then if I get into all my skinny jeans (size 4 being the smallest) I will be satisfied too. I guess that means I should have a waist of 27"? Or if I see my abs...that'll be nice too. 6 months from now, to lose 20+ pounds. I have plenty of time. I'll make sure that I make progress every month. I'll reach my goal by doing the following:

1\. Cold shower first thing every morning

2\. Cold bath at least 3 times a week

3\. Eat ketogenic paleo

4\. Eat seafood at least 5 times a week

5\. Go to bed at 10pm ready to have the best recovering sleep

6\. Weigh-in and take measurements once a month and always make further plans at the end of the month

### youthfuliz — 2012-07-21T19:07:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/youthfulizs-joyful-life-as-a-cavewoman.440/post-40016>

I've been very busy this month. Dealing with graduation, moving from NY to PA, and starting a new job. Good thing is I have tons of energy. Well I need to thank iodine for that.

Long story short, I found a lump on my neck on July 4th. Went to the school health center on the 5th, did blood test and ultrasound. Before getting the results I did a lot of online search - I read "most thyroid problems are due to iodine deficiency" and watched video presentation by Dr. Brownstein and other iodine doctors - and started to supplement iodine right away. Seaweeds, brazil nuts, oysters, potassium iodide (in liquid form I found in local store) and I ordered iodoral, selenium and other cofactors online. I supplement about 50mg iodine+iodide starting from July 7th. The lump shrunk within the first day of iodine supplementation. When I saw my thyroid reacted so quickly to iodine I knew I was doing the right thing. On July 10th the results came back, normal thyroid hormones, ultrasound shows two nodules most likely benign. The doctor said no need for any treatment and he doesn't think iodine may help.  My lump had shrunk by 50% by then but that didn't interest the doctor. I asked him to give me an iodine deficiency test, he sadi there was no need. Iodine deficiency is history in the America. I asked him to give me another ultrasound, I can show him that my nodules have shrunk in a week due to iodine supplementation... he didn't want to do it. Most CW doctors are not trained to understand how iodine deficiency can lead to problems. I just need to find me an iodine literate doctor next time. I continue taking 50mg iodoral daily.

So it has been two weeks. I stopped checking the lump first thing in the morning... it's getting smaller but the change is not as obvious as when I first increased the iodine level in my body. I think my breast like the additional iodine too... they look and feel happy.  I have tons of energy as I mentioned earlier, have taken care of so many things this week, have been very resourceful and have good judgment... just super productive. I like it.

The rest of July and August will be very busy too. But I look forward to starting a new chapter of my life. Will continue to take 50mg of iodoral with the cofactors.

I talked about the thyroid issue here:

[http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?2417-Thyroid-Nodule-anyone-knows-something-about-it-please-chime-in](http://http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?2417-Thyroid-Nodule-anyone-knows-something-about-it-please-chime-in)!

Here is our iodine thread:

[http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?987-The-iodine-Thread](http://http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?987-The-iodine-Thread)!!!

There is also a MDA iodine thread that I gathered lots of information from.

### youthfuliz — 2012-11-11T21:13:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/youthfulizs-joyful-life-as-a-cavewoman.440/post-63675>

OK I'm back after a really busy three months... Graduated, Moved, Started a new job, finally my life goes back to normal (kinda).

I have been sleeping early (11pm the latest, and often 10pm) a lot, and have been avoiding artificial lights at night. Although I'm not perfect, I'm so much better than 1 year ago. Feel sleepy every night around 10pm and sleep well through the night to 7am.

I've been eating lots of fish/shellfish lately too. I think it's helping my hormones (observation from my last mentrual cycle). Lots of mushrooms and cilantro. Haven't been able to lose weight consistently which I blame on the high-carb food I had once awhile and having very little physical activities lately - I've been tied to my desk with work. Now that work is pretty much under control I'll have time for more walking, maybe some HIIT and I'll focus on my eating - I'll eat a big breakfast (which I have been doing), and a light/late lunch. Will not eat anything after 6pm. Go to bed at 10pm most nights.

### youthfuliz — 2012-11-11T21:54:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/youthfulizs-joyful-life-as-a-cavewoman.440/post-63680>

Forgot to update on the thyroid/iodine front: I've been taking 100mg/day in the last month or so - I take twice a day, 50mg each, and sometimes I forget my 2nd portion, sometimes I forget for the whole day.... but overall I think I take 500mg/week on average.

Thyroid nodule is much smaller though not completely gone. I haven't been to a doctor again since I'm too busy at work and I'm glad that iodine/and lots of seafood are working at reducing it. Good energy level although it can be better. Sleep well, and also my constipation problem is solved all by itself... so things are going toward the right direction.

### Destiny — 2012-11-14T15:12:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/youthfulizs-joyful-life-as-a-cavewoman.440/post-64233>

>

>
>
> I've been eating lots of fish/shellfish lately too. I think it's helping my hormones (observation from my last mentrual cycle). Lots of mushrooms and cilantro. Haven't been able to lose weight consistently which I blame on the high-carb food I had once awhile and having very little physical activities lately - I've been tied to my desk with work. Now that work is pretty much under control I'll have time for more walking, maybe some HIIT and I'll focus on my eating - I'll eat a big breakfast (which I have been doing), and a light/late lunch. Will not eat anything after 6pm. Go to bed at 10pm most nights.

You'll get there! Just keep going. I started with 152 lbs. I experienced a lot of aggravation in regards to losing weight. It was two step forward and one back for almost 9 months. I was stuck in the fat losing range of 7-11 lbs. I the past 4 months, things started to progress in the right direction weight wise. I am at 135 lbs now and I am aiming lower. Good luck.

### youthfuliz — 2012-11-18T16:17:56+0200

# Thread: Are there any Ice princesses?

**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 8

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-26T02:50:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-there-any-ice-princesses.441/post-6155>

Was thinking about the people who are doing the amazing human tricks are all men. Any leads on women that have gone full frost?

### Shijin13 — 2012-03-26T03:52:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-there-any-ice-princesses.441/post-6178>

Dara Torres. She puts less time in the pools& weights etc now that she's older & she keeps getting faster we'll see her at the Olympics as the oldest female athlete & the only women's swimmer who's been to 6 Olympic games.

CT has to have played some role w/her comeback in 2008. I read she spent a max of 1:30 min/day in the pool. Normal training time for elite swimmers is 2hrs 2x/day

### Jack Kruse — 2012-03-26T03:54:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-there-any-ice-princesses.441/post-6179>

There is a trainer on my FB list who is doing some big things but she wants to see how it goes longer term

### Birdy — 2012-03-26T04:15:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-there-any-ice-princesses.441/post-6191>

I probably was when I was younger. I am old enough to remember when pools weren't heated, and I was in them as much as I could, even in October if I could find one that was open. I didn't do super-human feats, but I was very thin and could run farther than any women I knew. When I started competitive swimming in a college heated pool, it probably wasn't much good, they keep it hot like a bathtub. I am working my way back towards ice princess. My goal is ocean swimming.

### agatha — 2012-03-26T18:41:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-there-any-ice-princesses.441/post-6424>

Oh yes, Birdy, ocean swimming was one of my big goals too - and I've done it twice in the last week after 3 weeks of cold baths at home. It's just wonderful. Wearing a hat and neoprene gloves really helped though i looked entirely bonkers.

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-26T20:41:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-there-any-ice-princesses.441/post-6482>

This reminds me that my mother was unknowingly raising my sibs and me to be ice princesses. When we were toddlers, she would always have us take our daily naps outdoors, in a bassinet or playpen. All year round! In the winter, she would layer blankets on us and she swore that we were all very healthy, no ear infections, colds. She did this on recommendation from her Irish grandmother. ALso same g-ma recommended cold dips in the ocean, as a cure-all for anything.

I did this with my daughter, my husband thought I was crazy, she would fall asleep in her stroller when I would take her for a walk, I would leave it in the back yard, under the kitchen window, and let her sleep in the cold. I did that til she was 4. She had 1 ear infection. No colds.

### hazyjane — 2012-03-26T23:07:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-there-any-ice-princesses.441/post-6576>

> **Teenie Leek;6518 wrote:** This reminds me that my mother was unknowingly raising my sibs and me to be ice princesses. When we were toddlers, she would always have us take our daily naps outdoors, in a bassinet or playpen. All year round! In the winter, she would layer blankets on us and she swore that we were all very healthy, no ear infections, colds. She did this on recommendation from her Irish grandmother. ALso same g-ma recommended cold dips in the ocean, as a cure-all for anything.
>
>
>
> I did this with my daughter, my husband thought I was crazy, she would fall asleep in her stroller when I would take her for a walk, I would leave it in the back yard, under the kitchen window, and let her sleep in the cold. I did that til she was 4. She had 1 ear infection. No colds.

I love this!!

I did have a lot of ear infections (probably due to being gluten intolerant) but I remember spending a lot of time as a kid playing in the snow in wet jeans and not being bothered by it or swimming in cold lakes or the river next to my house. I really didn't get sick much.- esp. compared to my friends' kids who really don't eat junk food, yet get sick every 6 weeks. I hardly had colds or coughs. I asked my mom about my siblings and I and she said "You kids rarely got sick."

### Birdy — 2012-03-27T04:05:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-there-any-ice-princesses.441/post-6743>

My mom was ALWAYS trying to get me out of the cold. I remember playing in the snow for hours, and would only come in when I was soaked to the core and turning blue. Then I would get a change of clothes and go out again. I just loved it, and spent most of that time alone because none of my friends liked to be outdoors that much.
