# Thread: Hello from Sweden
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-sweden.445/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### FF_BC — 2012-03-26T12:50:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-sweden.445/post-6282>

Hello everyone!

I'm a young man from Sweden, almost 22 years old

Height: 190cm

Starting weight (summer 2011): 109 kg

Present weight: 97 kg

Goal weight: Do not know

I've been low carb since mid-summer 2011, went from 109 kg to 102 kg in 3 months. Since about Januari I've been IF:ing (intermittent fasting) leangainsstyle while eating paleo/low carb, lost about another 7 kg in 3 months. The last month or so I've actually gained 1-2 kg partly because eating like a moron, though I suspect some muscle gain from squating and deadlifting.

Came across Dr Jack Kruse and CT while reading some blogs from paleopeople for about two weeks ago, read some of Dr JK:s blogpost and just could not stop thinking about CT for about a week. Ended up taking colder showers and dunking my face in cold water for a couple of days then thought: "wtf I'm Swedish, cold shouldn't be a proplem for a Viking like myself!". Did my first cold bath (not using ice, just the water from the tap at coldest setting) three days ago, the first 5 minutes I shivered like crazy and then it became oddly nice  Sat for 15 minutes my first session, 30 minutes the second and 35 minutes yesterday (third session).

Now I'm looking forward to my next cold bath this evening, aiming for 40 minutes! Also I'm very curious at the combination of CT and IF, someone else doing both IF and CT?

Have a nice day!

### .lina — 2012-04-03T12:34:17+0300

hejhej! fun to see more swedes

# Thread: Is Lyme a circadian disease, like fleas and parasites that go away in winter?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-lyme-a-circadian-disease-like-fleas-and-parasites-that-go-away-in-winter.448/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### chocolate — 2012-03-26T20:34:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-lyme-a-circadian-disease-like-fleas-and-parasites-that-go-away-in-winter.448/post-6475>

I am seeing that pyroluria is now thought to be LYME. Lyme is thought to be epigenetic as well as tick borne. Shoot, dogs catch worms and people can too.

I found Dr Kruse because my daughter was misdiagnosed with mental illness (unlike fat people with an inflammatory brain disease, hey, I'm ocd and like the company LOL)and one of my dear friends had RA/Lyme disease. I had a rheumatoid virus once and we were just comparing notes. He mother also had severe epilepsy brought on by the accidental death of her brother. The first seizure was at about 12 years old and she was standing in front of a class nervous and had a petite ma and wound up standing in a puddle of pee. (maybe pyroluria) She also had hashi's, along with all of the big boobied, full- lipped women in her family.

It seems that stress is the thing and weather triggers epilepsy, active ms, parkinsons, pyroluria, etc.

When you think about it, pyroluria cures the damage of lyme. You isolate and stop eating. You have an infection=depression=inflammation, the not eating starts growth and repair, and what grows? The bones. Lyme destroys the bones.

Also, my kid flew to the southern hemisphere for winter/our summer. She started feeling better on the way there and worse on the way back, twice. She didn't eat on the plane and barely drank. Her joints were in so much pain she could barely walk. I couldn't imagine the trip would have bound her up so much.

So then, during the sunspots, my lyme disease friend stayed off facebook and could barely get out of bed. She started antibiotics. Another friend of hers was diagnosed with ms after a few iffy years during the sunspots. I am wondering if anyone with ms,lyme,parkinsons, als, shizophrenia, or garden variety depression have been diagnosed with pyroluria?

### chocolate — 2012-03-27T00:06:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-lyme-a-circadian-disease-like-fleas-and-parasites-that-go-away-in-winter.448/post-6611>

Also, LYME disease and fat are not complementary. Pyrolurics tend to be muscular, even as women. If it was an epigenetic virus, maybe adaptations occurred during gestation.

[http://www.nutritional-healing.com.au/content/articles-content.php?heading=Pyroluria](http://http://www.nutritional-healing.com.au/content/articles-content.php?heading=Pyroluria)

### chocolate — 2012-03-27T03:15:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-lyme-a-circadian-disease-like-fleas-and-parasites-that-go-away-in-winter.448/post-6722>

[http://www.lymediseaseresource.com/wordpress/magnesium-friend-or-foe-to-chronic-lyme-disease/](http://http://www.lymediseaseresource.com/wordpress/magnesium-friend-or-foe-to-chronic-lyme-disease/)

lyme disease need magnesium for its biofilms, summer fruit and sugar release magnesium and mercury adheres in its place. Is mercury overload from lyme stealing the magnesium?

I know lyme has active times and I am sure summer must be it. Cold thermogenesis is the only way to get rid of mercury and repatriate the magnesium I am thinking. And then, defrost with anti biotics in hand? or at least defrost ready to test. The presoak has to be sugar free.... I am guessing good old cider vinegar for the malic acid.

### Destiny — 2012-03-27T03:22:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-lyme-a-circadian-disease-like-fleas-and-parasites-that-go-away-in-winter.448/post-6726>

> **chocolate;6758 wrote:** [http://www.lymediseaseresource.com/wordpress/magnesium-friend-or-foe-to-chronic-lyme-disease/](http://http://www.lymediseaseresource.com/wordpress/magnesium-friend-or-foe-to-chronic-lyme-disease/)
>
>
>
> lyme disease need magnesium for its biofilms, summer fruit and sugar release magnesium and mercury adheres in its place. Is mercury overload from lyme stealing the magnesium?

Great link! Thx!

### chocolate — 2012-04-24T18:14:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-lyme-a-circadian-disease-like-fleas-and-parasites-that-go-away-in-winter.448/post-18382>

### chocolate — 2012-05-01T03:06:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-lyme-a-circadian-disease-like-fleas-and-parasites-that-go-away-in-winter.448/post-20252>

[http://jada.ada.org/content/137/12/1643.1.full](http://http://jada.ada.org/content/137/12/1643.1.full)

MOLECULE THAT INDUCES BIOFILM DISPERSION DISCOVERED

A biologist at Binghamton University, Binghamton, N.Y., reportedly has discovered a molecule that induces the dispersion of biofilms.

David Davies, PhD, an associate professor of biology, says he has found and is in the process of synthesizing a compound that will cause biofilm colonies to disperse, leaving bacteria up to 1,000 times more susceptible to disinfectants, antibiotics and immune functions.

Biofilms are complex aggregations of bacteria marked by the excretion of a protective and adhesive matrix. They develop almost anywhere that water and solids or solids and gases meet, which means they are virtually everywhere. When traveling alone in planktonic form, most bacteria are of small consequence and generally easy to manage. However, when they form biofilms, bacteria seem to gain super powers.

The small molecule Dr. Davies is working with appears to be one of the few known examples in nature of a communication signal that remains effective across species, family and phyla.

### chocolate — 2012-05-01T03:13:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-lyme-a-circadian-disease-like-fleas-and-parasites-that-go-away-in-winter.448/post-20257>

[http://jb.asm.org/content/191/5/1393.abstract](http://http://jb.asm.org/content/191/5/1393.abstract)

A Fatty Acid Messenger Is Responsible for Inducing Dispersion in Microbial Biofilms▿

David G. Davies\* and

ClÃ¡udia N. H. Marques

\+ Author Affiliations

Department of Biological Sciences, State University of New York at Binghamton, Binghamton, New York 13902

ABSTRACT

It is well established that in nature, bacteria are found primarily as residents of surface-associated communities called biofilms. These structures form in a sequential process initiated by attachment of cells to a surface, followed by the formation of matrix-enmeshed microcolonies, and culminating in dispersion of the bacteria from the mature biofilm. **In the present study, we have demonstrated that, during growth, Pseudomonas aeruginosa produces an organic compound we have identified as cis-2-decenoic acid, which is capable of inducing the dispersion of established biofilms and of inhibiting biofilm development.** When added exogenously to P. aeruginosa PAO1 biofilms at a native concentration of 2.5 nM, cis-2-decenoic acid was shown to induce the dispersion of biofilm microcolonies. This molecule was also shown to induce dispersion of biofilms, formed by Escherichia coli, Klebsiella pneumoniae, Proteus mirabilis, Streptococcus pyogenes, Bacillus subtilis, Staphylococcus aureus, and the yeast Candida albicans. Active at nanomolar concentrations, cis-2-decenoic acid appears to be functionally and structurally related to the class of short-chain fatty acid signaling molecules such as diffusible signal factor, which act as cell-to-cell communication molecules in bacteria and fungi.

### AmyJ — 2012-05-02T02:15:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-lyme-a-circadian-disease-like-fleas-and-parasites-that-go-away-in-winter.448/post-20562>

I watched an interesting documentary on a few people with lyme disease on Netflix last weekend. "Under Our Skin". Pretty eye-opening. Counting my blessings, I don't have it so bad at all.

### chocolate — 2012-05-20T17:29:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-lyme-a-circadian-disease-like-fleas-and-parasites-that-go-away-in-winter.448/post-25693>

[http://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2009/02/090209205151.htm](http://http://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2009/02/090209205151.htm)

To establish infection, however, the bacterium also must acquire a number of essential nutrients, including metals like **manganese** from its mammalian and tick hosts. Until now, no metal transporter responsible for this acquisition had been identified in this bacterium.

Lead author Dr. Zhiming Ouyang, postdoctoral researcher in microbiology at UT Southwestern, said another newly discovered characteristic about the bacterium that causes Lyme disease is that it doesn't seem to require iron to function, something most other pathogens need to survive.

lithium and manganese are cofactors ....interesting

### Jack Kruse — 2012-05-20T18:11:46+0300

Because the spirochete uses the lymph and fat system as part of it life cycle CT is part of the solution but you still must clear the toxin........liberation of it is not enough for many. And yes I do think Lyme has a serious circadian component.

### chocolate — 2012-05-21T00:10:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-lyme-a-circadian-disease-like-fleas-and-parasites-that-go-away-in-winter.448/post-25835>

[http://www.samento.com.ec/sciencelib/4lyme/beyondantibiotics.html](http://http://www.samento.com.ec/sciencelib/4lyme/beyondantibiotics.html)

My buddy forwarded me this really well thought out list of things to try for lyme. I guess there is a lifecycle disruptor from Japan that looks like a tens machine. It sends off frequencies that vary and don't make for happy little spirochetes. Supposedly they lose their groove. the scheduling and frequency has to change a lot.

This is another protocol...

The KMT program #4 is designed for treatment of mycoplasma (6).

H. The spirochetes and their close relatives ( Bartonella, Rickettsia, Ehrlichiosis, Brucella abortis) are best treated last - with antimicrobial herbs or antibiotics., 1 tsp bid. We use an alternating course of teasel root tincture (15 drops 3 times per day) for 6 weeks and then TOS free cat’s claw tincture (10 drops tid). We also use Echinacea root tincture , 2 dropperfull 3 times/day. Organic freeze dried garlic sometimes has a profound effect on the spirochetes. Many other herbs have enormous potential in the treatment of chronic Lyme disease.

**Frequency #1 in the KMT TENS unit inhibits the microbial growth of spirochetes and Bartonella, simultaneously activates specific immune responses and aids the uptake of antimicrobial herbs.

Injected bee venom has long been my favorite during this phase of the treatment (22, 23). The peptide mellitin has strong antibiotic activity against all spirochetes (24). Bee venom also contains nerve growth factor, the very substance needed for healing, when everything else has been attended to.

For the psychiatric presentations of Lyme disease I use large doses of Niacin. Niacinamide and no-flush Niacin do not work. 3-6 gms in 3-4 divided doses often show amazing results. It appears that Niacin has tremendous antibiotic potential against all types of Borrelia (12). I suspect that our mentor and genius in orthomolecular psychiatry, Abraham Hoffer, MD discovered a treatment for

**

Bb long before Lyme-disease was known.

The current antibiotic protocols are discussed and listed elsewhere (10). My favorites include Zithromax and Minocycline (both work symbiotically by binding to separate regions of the bacterial 50s ribosomal nucleic acid and both inhibit the microbes from taking part in protein transcription). I also use Rifampin.

The Klinghardt Neurotoxin Elimination Protocol

Approved by:

American Academy of Neural Therapy and

Institute of Neurobiology (Bellevue, WA, USA)

Institute for Neurobiologie (Stuttgart, Germany)

Academy for Balanced NeuroBiology Ltd (London, United Kingdom)

This lecture was presented by Dietrich Klinghardt M.D., Ph.D. at the Jean Piaget Department at the University of Geneva, Switzerland Oct. 2002 to physicians and dentists from Europe, Israel, several Arab countries and Asia.

> The Basic Program
>
>
>
> High protein, mineral, fatty acid and fluid intake
>
>
>
> Rationale:
>
>
>
> Proteins provide the important precursors to the endogenous metal detox and shuttle agents, such as coeruloplasmin, metallothioneine, glutathione and others. **The branched-chain amino acids in cow and goat whey have valuable independent detox effects**.
>
> Metals attach themselves only in places that are programmed for attachment of metal ions. Mineral deficiency provides the opportunity for toxic metals to attach themselves to vacant binding sites. A healthy mineral base is a prerequisite for all metal detox attempts (selenium, zinc, manganese, germanium, molybdenum etc.). Substituting minerals can detoxify the body by itself. Just as important are electrolytes (sodium, potassium, calcium, magnesium), which help to transport toxic waste across the extra-cellular space towards the lymphatic and venous vessels.
>
> Lipids (made from fatty acids) make up 60â€“80% of the central nervous system and need to be constantly replenished. Deficiency makes the nervous system vulnerable to the fat soluble metals, such as metallic mercury constantly escaping as odorless and invisible vapor evaporating from the amalgam fillings.
>
> Without enough fluid intake the kidneys may become contaminated with metals. The basal membranes swell up and the kidneys can no longer efficiently filtrate toxins. Adding a balanced electrolyte solution in small amounts to water helps to restore intra-and extra-cellular fluid balance

He seems to think once the mercury is gone, the other metals fall nicely into line detoxing. You need to have the proper minerals on board to do it.

# Thread: Question to Dr. Kruse ...or anyone! HOW TO GET SOME OF THE FAT BACK???
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-to-dr-kruse-or-anyone-how-to-get-some-of-the-fat-back.453/>

### nyika — 2012-03-27T02:02:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-to-dr-kruse-or-anyone-how-to-get-some-of-the-fat-back.453/post-6681>

So, after losing 5 kg (11lbs on hhcg and thinking that I am at my optimal weight) I went fully Paleo and lost another 2kg/4.4lbs without wanting to.

I now find that I have too little fat around my used to be curves.

Not much cushioning under my skin. Bruising all the time

I would like to look a little more... youthful or healthy, not too skinny and not too surfboard like (being a girl, I want to look like a girl, I don't want the Madonna look ).

What could I eat to actually put a little more weight on, just enough to get my girly curves back? Or have a little more cushioning under my skin?

Who would have thought that this Paleo/Leptin reset works THIS well??

I find that I can't eat a heap any more.

2 meals are usually enough, although I make sure that I eat 3 meals with the kids.

I try to eat sweet potato and other high carb veggies with each meal, but there is only so much I can stuff into my stomach.

I seriously NEED to get some more body fat back to fill my skin.

WHAT TO EAT? When? How much?

HELP!

### Honey Crisp — 2012-04-11T13:51:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-to-dr-kruse-or-anyone-how-to-get-some-of-the-fat-back.453/post-13685>

I lost a lot weight on HCG protocol and kept losing after finishing three rounds. If there is such thing as orthorexia. I had it. I was being asked if I was sick or not feeling well. It wasn't until my teenage daughter pointed out that I looked like I was "carrying a load" and my husband said my face looked gaunt, I realized I lost to much weight. I gained 7 pounds by eating more protein, cutting back on cardio and strength training. (I think my eating included a few trips to DQ, not recommended.) The 7 pounds was enough to fill out both sets of cheeks.

# Thread: I have an important question for Dr. Kruse, a friend said he is a good person to ask
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-have-an-important-question-for-dr-kruse-a-friend-said-he-is-a-good-person-to-ask.457/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 19
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Ertarox — 2012-03-27T04:19:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-have-an-important-question-for-dr-kruse-a-friend-said-he-is-a-good-person-to-ask.457/post-6752>

Hi,

I have studied diet, nutrition, all sorts of health modalities, and on a mad pursuit to figure out what was wrong with me. I saw a lot of people reverse cancer and all sorts of problems using nutrition, and I know that diet and exercise are fundamental.

I suspected I had insulin-resistance, imbalanced hormones and systemic yeast/candida. I knew I had that, actually, and had some testing here and there.....but I had a harder then hell time following any one protocol for long and even when I did, results were marginal, nothing like others experience. I only felt better if I quit eating altogether, which seemed to balance me a bit. But I have abused that far too much, and of course it is devastating on the body.

I had a work injury, went through that nightmare for 9 months (about to finally have my knee scoped for it) and then got fired. Shortly thereafter had falling outs with my brother and father and of course most friends fell away....it was all a blessing as it simplified life and the real people that matter will value you unconditionally.

I basically made a very severe decision to use every penny of credit available to me to charge all the medical tests I could never ever afford, determined to figure this out (and I am VERY good at sourcing underlying causes of health issues for others at this point) or to finally just end my life. It's been decades of this.

I hired a top ND at The Tahoma Clinic, world pioneers in bio-identical hormones and practitioners of orthomolecular medicine, and I hired a psychic that a no-bullshit friend highly recommended. And then a really good psychologist.

From my organic acids test it was determined that my mitochondria are not converting ATP, I have severe neurotransmitter deficiency, it was one of the worst she has seen in her career. Major malabsorption issues to where she would put me on nutritional I.V., but she is in Washington, I am in IL, and that is likely a byproduct of my self-abusive coping mechanisms too.

While all the tests told me something, my hormone test was awful. My HGH was incredibly high (and I never slept so that made so sense). It was so high I had to test for a pituitary tumor, but had none. My progesterone and estrogen were more than just "low", and so I started on those and in a couple months I started sleeping for the first time in over 20 years. I never slept, which is just awful. DHEA was horribly low, adrenals low....

I couldn't believe I didn't have a pituitary tumor. That would explain so much. I started researching what would ruin my pituitary and nuerotransmitters and adrenals in such a way, and kept coming up on TRAUMA. Obviously, stress kills....but I started reading about PTSD, something I knew little about but knew of......and knew without a doubt that THIS WAS ME. But for as far back as I can remember. And it wreaks havoc on the H-P-A axis (and a few other key things).

I paid to see a very qualified psychologist with experience in trauma, and did all the tests, the oral history. I definitely have severe PTSD.  There was extreme emotional abuse from ages 11 - adulthood, but even then....why did I have these vision problems, spatial issues, balance problems, extreme moodswings, this....FEELING...in my head. My ND said she wasn't convinced there wasn't a brain issue either. I have a vision problem not stemming from my eyes AT ALL. But the MRI I did was "fine" but for a small lesion in the brainstem. The EEG the psychologist did was all over the place, so non-integrated it would be similar to that found in something more than PTSD.

And why did I have PTSD feelings and symptoms dated PRIOR to eleven years old? Things still were't checking out, but seeing as how I stumped my ND too, I didn't feel as bad. It's all just too strange. There's more symptoms too, but to tie this up, the only possible conclusion considering all of this, my kind of glum side even as a kid (my mom says), my entire life, my twin brother's legal blindness and all the tubes he had in his ears, things my dad said and how explosive he was, my mother's hollowness, as if traumatized herself....is that I had a brain injury. Very very early on. And from being shaken. Which is just devastating.

While I consciously forgot, my body remembered. PTSD. And brain injury. I have some kind of brain damage. And I believe the issue in my brainstem is neurovascular. The MRI I have is not the right kind of imaging for microtears or for my brainstem issue. They assumed it was just Capillary telangiectasias, which is generally benign. This is all but.

My question is not seeking medical advice or anything. I am able to schedule with a neurologist in about a month, I PRAY. It has to be done in a certain way, which really really sucks but that's the insurance world. I may not get to at all and cannot even think of that possibility. But my question is, is Capillary telangiectasias ever this disruptive? It's a 3mm lesion in the pons area, presumably that. Because you are a neurosurgeon, what else could I be researching in the meantime? I know it can be scar tissue, from a ligamental tear. It's right by the craniocervical junction and I FEEL IT. I pinch it and it offers some relief. I KNOW there is a injury there.

This part fascinates me, too:

Last September, I discovered that the xanax I was taking for sleep for the past 8 months (I finally resorted to scripts after averaging 4 hours sleep every 3 or 4 days, total), that I switched to due to the work injury, had accidentally become an addiction. The symptoms were disrupting my work injury symptoms, which is bad. I apparently was going through interdose withdrawal for months, not knowing it. I knew the risks but just cold-turkeyed it. I learned it was xanax addiction after accidentally withdrawing for about 48 hours once or twice before, then taking it. I could not believe alllll of that was xanax, I thought I was dying and you can die. I had paid attention, and I figured out fast that I needed to wear a neckbrace interestingly enough. Then the w/d went okay (as long as I stayed upright).

Why this is INTERESTING is that(I since learned that) in xanax withdrawal, it blows out (or something) all your calcium chloride. I am not a doctor, but I observed, then learned then researched it, that any tiny injury will be blown up to grotesque proportions when going under xanax w/d for this reason. My meniscus tear was sensitive, but I needed a brace and was limping profusely at night, versus could walk damn near fine during the day, when not going through withdrawal. This is key to me because the TWO sources of major dysfunction were my knee, where there was a known meniscus tear, and my neck. I felt it at the top of my neck (brainstem) and I could not hold my head up. I took a cab to the E.R where a friend is a doctor just to get a neck brace. I wore it for a week. It disabled my knee and my neck, grossly blowing the problem out of proportion. But my brainstem has been a source of frustration my whole life. So I am oddly grateful for the experience because I KNOW there's an issue. There IS an injury. And my vestibular-ocular (sp?) pathway is affected as

Can a ligament tear or scar tissue cause a neurovascular problem, or can Capillary telangiectasias be that disruptive?

And is the leptin diet key for neurotransmitters and/or pituitary health? I know I'll be doing orthomolecular doses of supplements, I have a lot right here. Linus Pauling style. And Dr. Perlmutter is applying nutritional science to neurogenesis and having success. Neuroplasticity is available to us but PTSD is a huge interference. Still, I try, and I educate myself as best I can. I plan to pursue neurofeedback for the PTSD as soon as I can rule out a neurovascular issue or have it corrected.

I've been trying to treat all this with wheatgrass and diet! Kinda funny to me, overall. LOL!  Gotta laugh at yourself.

Thanks so much. I know it's a tacky large forum post and many show up with huge life sagas and little interest in doing the research or work themselves. I tried to give a background and not a sob story. I hope I stuck to relevant issues only. I'm usually the guy coaching people in those situations. I just figured this out literally over the holidays, with the PTSD confirmation in Jan. Then I ran out of money and the depression set in. I've loved studying neuroscience the best I can, but it's a lot to digest. So it never hurts to ask!

### hazyjane — 2012-03-27T06:11:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-have-an-important-question-for-dr-kruse-a-friend-said-he-is-a-good-person-to-ask.457/post-6804>

Hi, Ertarox!

I'm not Dr. Kruse, but I have a suggestion for you:

I used to be a neurofeedback practitioner and I think you could benefit from Brain State Conditioning (a particular form of neurofeedback). It's very effective at reversing PTSD and opening up new neural networks to operate from. You can literally rewire your brain. As long as you are supporting your brain through neurotransmitter support and diet, I think your brain has a shot at making some good progress with Brain State.

I would suggest that if you do it, get the practitioner to consult with Lee Gerdes, the founder, on your case. It will be extra money, but it'll be money well spent, since Lee knows how to correct brain imbalances like nobody else.

[http://brainstatetech.com/](http://http://brainstatetech.com/)

### Ertarox — 2012-03-27T06:34:50+0300

THANK YOU!

I love neurofeedback. It gives me hope. I don't know what kind she was doing but my psychologist was very trauma-competent, and learned neurofeedback when she did BECAUSE it does the trick. I cannot afford it is the problem. And my only hesitation also is if there is an oxygen-supply problem in the brain.....I feel that should be addressed before I invest in rewiring my brain. Because it's like sweeping the deck during a tornado. Why bother? Lack of oxygen to the brain is forever going to affect it. Fortunately, I should be able to rule that out soon. The GOAL is to move to Eugene Oregon shortly, where cost of living is cheap and healthy living is easier, and there is a neurofeedback center there that focuses in PTSD even. I love the idea of Brain State Conditioning and consulting with Gerdes.

When I know a BIT more neurologically and obviously have the money, I'll go for it. According to the 'psychic' that helped me immensely, whatever is going on with me is going to be studied profusely by neuroscientists. I think that's interesting if it happens, but I was more concerned with getting answers, and that is what she primarily helped me with. I know I'll be following a heavy neurotransmitter protocol, to say the least. And anti-candida.

And Dr. Kruse, thank you! What is CT?

I saw this Nitric Balance product yesterday available in peppermint flavor....

BTW, HazyJane, it was your MDA post I found about the Nitric Balance and wondered if it would help, too, along with CT.

### ineedanLR — 2012-04-06T18:46:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-have-an-important-question-for-dr-kruse-a-friend-said-he-is-a-good-person-to-ask.457/post-12175>

I'm curious regarding significance of inflammatory responses relative to your health.......clearly they are underrated with respect to their importance to our overall health.

Have you stopped sugar (all sugar especially high fructose), gluten/grains/rice, milk protein (whey), nightshade family foods (potato, cayenne etc)? Whether you're overweight or not, I would think that eliminating the foods that cause gross inflammatory responses in the body would be beneficial to you (and to at least 99 percent of the population).

In the coming years, I think the diet and the treatment of inflammatory related medical issues that are currently at epidemic proportions will undergo serious rewriting and that a revolution is in the making.

Regards,

Art

### Pamela — 2012-04-06T21:42:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-have-an-important-question-for-dr-kruse-a-friend-said-he-is-a-good-person-to-ask.457/post-12235>

> **Ertarox;6788 wrote:** Hi,
>
>
>
> This part fascinates me, too:
>
> Last September, I discovered that the xanax I was taking for sleep for the past 8 months (I finally resorted to scripts after averaging 4 hours sleep every 3 or 4 days, total), that I switched to due to the work injury, had accidentally become an addiction. The symptoms were disrupting my work injury symptoms, which is bad. I apparently was going through interdose withdrawal for months, not knowing it. I knew the risks but just cold-turkeyed it. I learned it was xanax addiction after accidentally withdrawing for about 48 hours once or twice before, then taking it. I could not believe alllll of that was xanax, I thought I was dying and you can die. I had paid attention, and I figured out fast that I needed to wear a neckbrace interestingly enough. Then the w/d went okay (as long as I stayed upright).
>
>
>
> Why this is INTERESTING is that(I since learned that) in xanax withdrawal, it blows out (or something) all your calcium chloride. I am not a doctor, but I observed, then learned then researched it, that any tiny injury will be blown up to grotesque proportions when going under xanax w/d for this reason. My meniscus tear was sensitive, but I needed a brace and was limping profusely at night, versus could walk damn near fine during the day, when not going through withdrawal. This is key to me because the TWO sources of major dysfunction were my knee, where there was a known meniscus tear, and my neck. I felt it at the top of my neck (brainstem) and I could not hold my head up. I took a cab to the E.R where a friend is a doctor just to get a neck brace. I wore it for a week. It disabled my knee and my neck, grossly blowing the problem out of proportion. But my brainstem has been a source of frustration my whole life. So I am oddly grateful for the experience because I KNOW there's an issue. There IS an injury. And my vestibular-ocular (sp?) pathway is affected as

Xanax and any psychiatric drugs are deadly Don't take them. This forum gives you the answers and there are other self help methods than meds. Glad you are off them!

### healthnut — 2012-03-27T07:00:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/worrisome-lipids.459/post-6853>

My lipids have gone through the roof according this comprehensive health diagnostic labs reports:

total cholesterol- **318** ref ALA-**0.2% ref-0.1-0.4

DPA-**2.6% ref-0.6-4.1

EPA- **1.2% ref-0.1-2.5

DHA- **7.3% ref-0.1-8.4

omega 6 total-**27.3% ref 28.6-44.5

AA- **12.6%** ref 10.5-23.3

Linoleic- 10.9%** ref 4.6-21.3

cis monounsaturated total- 17.7%** ref 11.5-20.5

Saturated Total- 43.1** ref 36.6-42

Trans total-0.6%** ref 0.1-1.8

Hemoglobin A1c- 5.1** (drawn just prior to the above labs)

Fasting Blood Glucose- 98

### healthnut — 2012-03-27T16:10:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/worrisome-lipids.459/post-6976>

Anyone? Sure hoping for some input!

### L8F — 2012-03-27T16:16:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/worrisome-lipids.459/post-6980>

How long ago did you make the switch over to Paleo eating? There is an adjustment period while your liver makes the change over, and cholesterol goes up before settling in. That being said, your trigs are high. THis was fasting right? Also how much percent paleo are you? Are you low carb as well? Don't want to assume...

### healthnut — 2012-03-27T17:10:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/worrisome-lipids.459/post-7013>

It did not convert to Paleo recently. Let me give you a little background.........

I've been lowcarb since discovering metabolic syndrome in my early 30's, so about 20 years. Numbers were pretty well controlled until the past few years. They just seem to keep creeping higher. The trigs averaged around 5,000 in my thirties before I started lowcarb, shot as high as 7,000 when they put me on a no-fat program. I was a vegetarian from about age 18 through my twenties. It no doubt created metabolic syndrome. I also developed PCOS, IBS and scleroderma in my late 20's. The scleroderma went into remission about 5 years after I gave up vegetarianism and went lowcarb. IBS vanished immediatly after eliminating grains. I battled with PCOS unitl I was in my early 40's.

I have been very regimented with carbs for the past 20 year to keep hgbA1c in check, generally 20 net/day. Sometimes will cycle 50-75/net day if doing more activity. I track in cronometer. I have eaten mainly organic and grassfed raised animal products for the past 20 years, my husband and I were certified organic growers through the 80's. We grow a lot of our own veggies too. I try to eat both local and seasonal. When the term 'paleo' came along, it was easy to see we fit that description. I have not had grains in 20 years.

Several things have occurred that may have played a role in the lipids rising so dramatically. I stopped bodybuilding competitively a few years ago. Also, another player is age. I see the two biggies influencing my lipids as ApoE genotype and the hyperabsorption of fats. What worked to control lipids in my late 30's and through my 40's now seems to be backfiring. I also retired as a personal trainer about a year ago and have tried to change my workout fanaticism. I worked out with weights for an hour and a half plus at least an hour of hardcore biking/cardio per day. The leptin reset helped me recover adrenal function, leptin sensitivity and basal body temps (it freaked me out not working out at all for the first month). Since reducing exercise levels, I am battling lipids. I still lift 5 days per week and take the dogs for a walk every day. Trying to do exercise more in moderation. I am oc cousre tempted to begin using exercise again as a method to control numbers, because it does seem to keep lipids & blood pressure in check.

Most think of familial hypercholesterolemia when they see numbers like this, but Apo E4 E4 genotype will create a similar panel.....in spite of low carb. These however....are the worst my numbers have looked since my thirties. The sdLDL and particle size specifically are frightening. Very sobering.

### Shijin13 — 2012-03-27T17:58:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/worrisome-lipids.459/post-7043>

**healthnut;6889 wrote: My lipids have gone through the roof according this comprehensive health diagnostic labs reports:

total cholesterol- 318** ref11.3% ref-0.1-14.1

ALA-**0.2% ref-0.1-0.4

DPA-**2.6% ref-0.6-4.1

EPA- **1.2% ref-0.1-2.5

DHA- **7.3% ref-0.1-8.4

omega 6 total-**27.3% ref 28.6-44.5

AA- **12.6%** ref 10.5-23.3

Linoleic- 10.9%** ref 4.6-21.3

cis monounsaturated total- 17.7%** ref 11.5-20.5

Saturated Total- 43.1** ref 36.6-42

Trans total-0.6%** ref 0.1-1.8

Hemoglobin A1c- 5.1** (drawn just prior to the above labs)

Fasting Blood Glucose- 98

Ok I'm seeing 3 things here the first is Inflammation your HbA1C, homocystine, hsCRP are at the high end of normal. Dr K likes to see HbA1C below 4.5 and hsCRP as close .1 and below as possible. W/your history of autoimmune I'd avoid all nightshades, eggs, and dairy b/c you still have inflammation - CT will help w/the inflammation - push this as a tool!

2nd: your Fasting BG is High - given Dr K's and Dr Davis's views anything above 90 for fasting = diabetic. Get your self a cheap bg meter and check your bg before each meal, and for every 15mins until it returns to your pre-meal levels. you don't want to see more than a 10-15point spike during the 1hr or so after eating. Dairy can spike your bg

3rd: trigs and sdLDL & the APOE gene: How long have you been grain free? I didn't see anything in your elaboration that explained how long? my understanding is dairy (especially the casein protein) can stimulate trigs the same way in some ppl - based upon my conversations w/my pcp. ARe you dairy free? If not try going dairy free. .

you're having some oxidization going on... so that tells me you're body's not converting the LDL its making for the sex hormones... your hormones are probably off as well. Have you had a full hormone panel done recently.. you mention adrenal fatigue - I'm guessing you're still having issues w/conversion of LDL to pregnenolone - and instead you're still got some Hypothalmus- pitutary -Adrenal axis mismatch occuring... which is also impacting your Thyroid... but can't be sure unless you have more labs...

### BenG — 2012-03-27T19:02:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/worrisome-lipids.459/post-7118>

I recall Dr. Kruse saying no dairy till FBG is < 85. You might want to throw some anti-inflammatories in there too like tumeric, green tea (EGCG)(PQQ), etc. Read his top ten paleo supplements: [http://jackkruse.com/what-are-the-top-ten-paleo-supplements/](http://http://jackkruse.com/what-are-the-top-ten-paleo-supplements/) Homocystine..I'd probably add B-Complex supps. too. CT helps too. You're also lifting 5 days a week...Exercise is anti-inflammatory in moderation. Over training is pro-inflammatory though. How's your sleep? Dr. Kruse is anti-cardio...he'll tell you how it depletes your stem cells and causes early death. He only advocates Lifting and HIIT. (low intensity like walking is ok). If you have adrenal problems which Shijin mentioned (lousy sleep, low DHEA-S, High Cortisol \[overtraining causes that), you may have adrenal fatigue and should consider his Adrenal Rx, but a hormone panel would tell if you have pregnenolone steal syndrome.

### healthnut — 2012-03-27T19:19:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/worrisome-lipids.459/post-7144>

> **Shijin13;7080 wrote:** Ok I'm seeing 3 things here the first is Inflammation your HbA1C, homocystine, hsCRP are at the high end of normal. Dr K likes to see HbA1C below 4.5 and hsCRP as close .1 and below as possible. W/your history of autoimmune I'd avoid all nightshades, eggs, and dairy b/c you still have inflammation - CT will help w/the inflammation - push this as a tool!
>

> 2nd: your Fasting BG is High - given Dr K's and Dr Davis's views anything above 90 for fasting = diabetic. Get your self a cheap bg meter and check your bg before each meal, and for every 15mins until it returns to your pre-meal levels. you don't want to see more than a 10-15point spike during the 1hr or so after eating. Dairy can spike your bg
>

> 3rd: trigs and sdLDL & the APOE gene: How long have you been grain free? I didn't see anything in your elaboration that explained how long? my understanding is dairy (especially the casein protein) can stimulate trigs the same way in some ppl - based upon my conversations w/my pcp. ARe you dairy free? If not try going dairy free. .
>
> you're having some oxidization going on... so that tells me you're body's not converting the LDL its making for the sex hormones... your hormones are probably off as well. Have you had a full hormone panel done recently.. you mention adrenal fatigue - I'm guessing you're still having issues w/conversion of LDL to pregnenolone - and instead you're still got some Hypothalmus- pitutary -Adrenal axis mismatch occuring... which is also impacting your Thyroid... but can't be sure unless you have more labs...

Thanks for your in depth and thoughtful reply I agree, hemoglobin A1c is not where it should be. I have officially had diabetes since my 30's, is getting harder to control w/age.(I am 53). When I was bodybuilding & overexercising it stayed around 4.7-4.9...that's the lowest I ever was able to get it.

Unfortunately, the homocystiene and hsCRP have also risen in the last few years with the lipids, though not as dramatically.

I am not a big nightshade fan....but will steer clear. Peppers on occasion.

About the eggs and dairy. We raise chickens and goats. They are free range...they roam the woodland paths. The only concentrates they are fed are flax, mineral salt and kelp. I think the sulfur and choline (among many other nutrients) might be important in the eggs? Do you think eggs/dairy from clean, home raised, pastured animals would also cause inflammation?

I've been rationalizing having a cup of homemade goats milk Greek style yogurt every day. Goat milk has a very different composition than cow's milk. It contain A2 beta casein, the one that does not problematic as is A1 beta casein from cows milk. Goat milk provides medium chain triglycerides like coconut does, it is high in caprylic acid (this fatty acid is named after 'caprine' AKA-goat). I also feel the abundance of different strains of beneficial bacteria in home cultured, raw kefir provides large numbers of gut flora that lasts longer in the digestive system than anything that could be bought commercially. I culture the yogurt 24 hours, then strain it into a cheesecloth until it is very thick. It drains off most of the whey (lactose) and is similar to greek yogurt in carb count. What do you think? Still no good? I know Dr Davis speaks of the insuniogenic properties of milk. I don't see a post prandial rise, but maybe I am getting insulin to spike....maybe that is why I often see a post prandial drop instead of rise? Prolly thinking, won't render optimal results!

I am officially diabetic, and have been using a glucometer for maybe 8 years. I have controlled HgbA1c w/diet pretty well, but it is getting more difficult. The fasting blood glucose goes higher on keto levels of carbs. (dawn phenomenon). If on keto levels... my breakfast 1 hour post prandial drops, often 20-30 points. It's not really reactive hypoglycemia, since the drop doesn't normally go below 70's & 80's. But FBG can be as high as 120. If I get carbs up around 50grams/day, BG reacts more normally and fasting BG is lower.....but A1c rises. so, I try to look at a combination of A1c, FBG and 1 hour post-prandials. My 1 hour post prandials are rarely over 100.

Realy enjoying CT, I feel so relaxed afterwards.

I went off grains when I went lowcarb in my 30's, so about twenty years. I did re-introduce them for a brief period when I was bodybuilding, but it brought back IBS....so I cut them back out.

I am due for a complete hormone panel, I see my PCP next week to go over these results. I will post results. Last November, they were looking good and cortisol levels were near normal compared to where they were during the stage II adrenal failure. Pregnenolone was way higher too, but not too optimal. Still on some 7keto for that though.

### Dan in Utah — 2012-03-27T19:25:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/worrisome-lipids.459/post-7153>

I'm in a similar boat as you. I'm sorting my lipids out, too. We might learn from each other.

Dr. Kruse seems nonplussed about lipids in general.

My CRP isn't too high, but it's not optimal. Right now, I feel like I have the lit match (not optimal CRP) and the case of dynamite (high TC, high LDL, high LDLp, and high small LDLp). My lipids were okay prior to paleo/leptin/CT.

Mine really frightened me, too. I'm still formulating my plan and checking into a few more labs, but I'll post my ideas when I'm ready.

I'm sure Apo E4 is playing a big role in your case. Probably mine, too. (My test is still at the lab.)

Dr. Davis (sort of a paleo-cardiologist) thinks low carb is not so good for Apo E folks. Dr. Kruse seems to disagree. See my comments over here. ### BenG — 2012-03-27T19:37:20+0300

Last November, they were looking good and cortisol levels were near normal compared to where they were during the stage II adrenal failure. Pregnenolone was way higher too, but not too optimal. Still on some 7keto for that though.

If you have an autoimmune disease - which I believe you mentioned in your earlier write up - you need to avoid dairy, nightshades and eggs, follow the leptin reset and leaky gut rx at the same time... your inflammation is most likely in your gut - and transferring across everything else.

I'm off diary right now...at the request of my pcp - based upon a CIMT - which showed my arterial age of 51 - I'm 38! I go back to see her at the end of april beginning of may - and will have another set of labs run - if things are still high - out goes the eggs. I'm already night shade free - b/c I get wicked inflammation when I eat them - my joints swell, and turn red...

I'd skip the eggs and diary - even the A2 goats milk...

### L8F — 2012-03-27T20:18:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/worrisome-lipids.459/post-7230>

Robb Wolf's podcasts (my version of paleo university) have a lot about why AI folks should avoid dairy and eggs. He does not care if it is goat or cow's milk, it is highly insulinogenic and pro growth and inflammation in his book. As a diabetic this is going to be tough tough on your system. The eggs explanation is on the podcast from 12/21/09. It is not about quality of the eggs, it is more about the fact that they protect their reproductive material much like a seed or a grain does--->bad for AI especially. You might notice a dramatic difference if you worked on gut as suggested above along with _strict_ AI diet protocol. You have serious AI history with scleroderma, so don't mess around. Leaky gut stuff, hit it hard.

I find it hard psychologically to marry the nutritive values of certain foods with the potential downsides for certain populations, but it is what it is. Robb W knows his autoimmunity for sure though.

### healthnut — 2012-03-27T21:17:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/worrisome-lipids.459/post-7281>

I take it in the evening with regular L-tryptophan. I take 100mg 5HTP and 500mg L-tryptophan, plus 400-600mg mag glycinate & 200mg phosphatidyl serine. When I stopped the L-tryprophan and 5HTP, sleep went back to waking around 2-3am and headaches returned. I think I may be low in seratonin.

I stopped it because I take too many supps.....and my sleep was great even if I forgot to take it. The sleeplessness didn't kick back in until after I'd been off for a few weeks.

### BenG — 2012-03-27T22:00:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/worrisome-lipids.459/post-7315>

> **healthnut;7215 wrote:** _"Consider HIIT/Sprints. When I do them my FBG goes from 80-84 to 68-75. They can really bring it down. 5-6 bursts of maximum effort 2-3x a week is all you need."_
>
>
>
> I tried this a few times this winter, was fearfull it may elevate cortisol? I did the peak 8 sprints on cycle. 30 second sprint, 90 sec slow pace....8 cycles. I'll give it a go.

Your poor sleep suggests a cortisol problem. You probably should do salivary testing (day, evening, night etc) over 24 hours.

Studies have shown you can maintain your level of fitness by scaling back volume and intensity by 2/3rds as long as intensity stays the same. That said, if you have a cortisol/overtraining problem, I'd say workout no more than 1-2x a week. Often overtrained people, who cut volume back to 1-2x a week all the sudden see gains because it gives their hormones a chance to recover from chronically elevated cortisol/fatigue. Maybe 1 day of heavy weights and 1 day of sprints. The CT should improve recovery

See [http://jackkruse.com/primal-cortisol-response/](http://http://jackkruse.com/primal-cortisol-response/)

Adrenal Rx: [http://jackkruse.com/what-might-casey-anthony-and-oj-have-in-common/](http://http://jackkruse.com/what-might-casey-anthony-and-oj-have-in-common/)

On Leaky gut: [http://jackkruse.com/the-leaky-gut-prescription/](http://http://jackkruse.com/the-leaky-gut-prescription/)

and see if any apply to you, although it's hard to be sure without testing. Still, the bad sleep suggests a cortisol problem and low DHEA-S, sex steroids, Vitamin D and/or pregnenolone steal syndrome, so the links should help you fix. Going to and keeping artifical light to a minimum after dark is a huge help.

Yep, had overtraining adrenal/cortisol issues. I had tested 8 months prior to beginning LR. I was in stage two adrenal failure (did the salivary testing ZRT). Retested two months after beginning LR and cortisol levels were almost normal.

I have scaled back workouts a lot and am no longer a cardio addict. It's a hard habbit to break! Still hesitant to go whole hog on HIIT or sprint intervals. Especially since one knee has no miniscus left on either side. Yeh, maybe once or twice a week would work. The weights aren't stressfull now, stopped training like a bodybuilder prepping from competition. Much more relaxed. I still lift heavy, but not crazy heavy. I was squatting over 450# before my last knee surgery a few years ago. Now bench 175# for sets of 6. Used to rep 225#. We now work out at the local HS gym so I am not tempted to get competitive again.

Thanks for the links, I need to catch up on all the JK blogs, I am a greenhorn when it comes to much of the hormone talk. I overproduced hormones through my 30' & 40's. Just started to fall back last year. I take 10,000IU's of D and levels are hard to get/stay optimal. I had it up to 72 in the fall......now it fell back this spring. We live North of the 45th parallel.....not too much natural D production here in the deep woods. I have the glasses and the screen dimmer and turn lights down/off when it gets dark. The cold therapy helps w/sleep a lot too.

### healthnut — 2012-03-28T18:43:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/worrisome-lipids.459/post-7760>

I will attempt to post older labs today.

There are a few slamming me for not being on statins....or at least zetia.... or a bile sequestriant....

I thought you guys might find this analysis of my labs interesting. It is from a doctor who is on the track your plaque forum:

_"I want to point out all of the BEAUTIFUL parts of this test.

NO inflammation! Your myeloperoxidase (surrogate marker for dysfunctional HDL) is georgous. Your LPPLA2 has only a trivial elevation (marker for oxidized plaque) Your hscrp is excellent. Then, your omega 3 index is optimal,,,,so, maybe you do have a "build up" of plaque in an artery wall. It is not inflamed, (lppla2 almost normal), your hdl will work to help clear it out (myeloperoxidase normal), the fibrous protective "cap" over it is solid (omega 3 index optimal), Now even IF you were to have a plaque rupture, would the stuff just blow downstream and silently disburse, or would it "clot", form a "thrombus", cause an" event"? That depends on the characteristics of the blood rushing by....So your fibrinogen is nice and low! Fibrinogen is part of the clotting cascade so great! You don't have Factor V Leiden or the prothrombin gene (genetic tendencies of blood to clot more readily) You don't have Lp(a) which resembles plasminogen, another clot former. Your homocysteine is optimal despite C/T MTHFR genetics. Homocysteine elevation promotes clot formation. MTHFR (C/T)? This means that you can optimize probably only 70 % of the folate you eat. But then look at your RBC folate. (RBC folate is a 3 month folate, much better than a serum "3 minute" folate) Your dietary intake of folate is very high,AND 70% OF VERY HIGH is still excellent. Then what about your platelets? Are they ready to clump together and clot? No....your aspirinworks (Platelet clumping test) is very low.

So...high lipids, no oxidized HDL or LDL, no fibrous cap erosion, no increase in clotting factors, no abnormal platelet clumping.

Also BP parameters excellent (NTPRoBNP, Gallectin 3, urine for microalbumin)

Insulin, c-peptide excellent (no diabetes here)

Elevated nonesterified FFA? Thats your MCT oil.

F2 isoprostanes so low that they can't be measured? This says that you are super, super fit!!!_ "

Please tell me more about your CIMT, no radiation involved? I listened to a podcast recently that talked about the differences in diagnosing mens heart disease and womens. Diagnostic and therapeutic strategies \[for heart disease\], which had been developed in men, by men, for men weren't working so well for women. They said women tend to get more IM thickening as an early warning, so diagnosis methods typically used for men, do not detect a problem in women. I had an echo and a heart ultrasound last month, it did show some plaque. But I think by my age everyone has 'some' plaque. I plan to get CIMT and a heart scan to see exactly what's going on.

> HDL was in the 20's when I began battling metabolic syndrome. Raising HDL has been a long struggle.
>
>
>
> Would like to be in the optimal range with Trigs:HDL ratio, optimal is 1:1 (I believe). I am at 1.72
>
>
>
> To reach the total cholesterol to HDL optimal ratio of under 3, I have a ways to go. Currently at 4.29.
>
>
>
> Would you make any changes?

To raise HDL, some will use 1000mg of Vit B3 in the am and then another 1000mg in the evening. Slo Niacin seems to be a good choice for non flushing B-3 niacin. The ones that say "no-flush" seem to cause a flush rather badly. Slo Niacin avail at Wal-Mart for around $13 for 100 tablets of 500mg each.

# Thread: Chrystin's Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chrystins-journal.480/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 48
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### [email redacted] — 2012-03-28T02:09:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chrystins-journal.480/post-7437>

Ok, here goes...

It's hard to know where to start, isn't it? After a year and a half of hopelessly battling my 10 lb weight gain (on the heels of 35 years of being very fit) I stumbled upon the leptin rx. Not two weeks in, I had gained about 6 pounds! When I commented about my weight gain on the MDA forums, dr. K said that if you gain weight on the rx, your hormones are out of whack. My thought was "whatever. My hormones are fine.". And I kept going.

Then I decided to get some testing done-just to prove to myself that I really was healthy and that my weight gain and binge eating was because I was crazy, lazy and self indulgent. I was floored when my testing came back... Cortisol- low during the day, high at night. Estrogen and progesterone-low. T3 (and prob t4) - low. Dheas- low. Vit d-low.

After doing some research, I've finally realized that all of my symptoms over the past year or so are consistent with my messed up adrenals and thyroid. I've had minor sensitivity to noise, light, temperature, some hair loss, weak nails, dry skin. Those didn't grab my attention. The symptoms that have been most bothersome to me have been (obviously) the weight gain and the emotional ones.

I picked up the book "stop the thyroid madness". Wow. What an education. It was here that I was able to finally see my psychological issues in adrenal dysfunction and hypothyroid. Every symptom- rage, intolerance, complete lack of ability to deal with even the slightest stress, needing to be alone, ups and downs, depression, lack of joy (a few years ago I was the happiest, bubbliest, most energetic person you'd ever meet) completely characterized my every day existence. I just though I was an awful mom and wife. My mom was really demanding, perfectionistic, stressed and high strung and I thought I was just carrying on in her example. To think that all this has been a chemical imbalance.....

Well, I'll see my naturopath next week. I expect her to do some further testing- iron, electrolytes, rt3, maybe b12, iodine, gluten, hormones. Then we'll see where to go from there...

That's why I decided to start this journal. I'm not sure where this journey will take me. I've been unable to stay on the leptin rx for longer than a couple of weeks before I'm overtaken by bingeing. Restrictive programs always set me off... But I keep on keepin on and I'm religiously doing my cold baths every day.

### Birdy — 2012-03-28T02:44:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chrystins-journal.480/post-7458>

Good luck, and I am looking forward to reading more.

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-31T13:49:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chrystins-journal.480/post-9166>

Haven't posted in few days. I've been extremely moody. Rage, tears, depression. And cravings. I think I looked like one of those movie schizophrenics yesterday after lunch, fighting my craving for sugar. Ugh. This is how I'm wasting the precious seconds and minutes and hours of my life? It is shameful. Really really shameful.

Started B complex, C complex, E and Magnesium supplementation yesterday (from Wilson's "Adrenal Fatigue") ahead of my naturopath appt on Tuesday. I have to say that I think the Magnesium helped. I had to take it after dinner (he says to take it near food, although after 8 is best- those two obviously do not coincide on the Rx). I slept much better than the past few nights. I think I may have woken up during the night, but I don't recall.

I will return to CT today. Baths, that is. I've iced the past two days because I just couldn't make myself get in the tub (TOM-2 weeks late and very heavy). The icing is just too easy to give any results, IMHO, LOL. I also plan to lift some weights this afternoon.

I was reading on another forum (about paleo/primals with sugar cravings)...This one girl got rid of her sugar cravings by drinking a can of coco milk. Got me thinking again about bulletproof drinks... My plan is to have one after each meal. (coffee at breakfast, until naturopath I imagine will tell me to stop coffee) and green tea at lunchtime and dinner. I had coco oil in green tea immediately following dinner last night and it \*may\* have helped. I'll continue that experiment.

...and I continue to track my meals on a spreadsheet. Oh, and I'm waking up very thirsty every morning. I don't think I'm getting enough water. Maybe that will help me flush all of these moody crappy feelings out better...

### [email redacted] — 2012-04-04T14:08:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chrystins-journal.480/post-11210>

Quick check in while i make my kids' breakfast... Saw naturopath last night. Not \*quite\* as knowledgeable (read:in agreement with me, LOL) as I had hoped. She asked if the "diet" I was on had more fat. Long sigh...... Anyway, got a full thyroid panel ordered, dhea-s at least and will be doing a whole month of saliva testing and another draw on day 21 for hormones.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-04-04T14:25:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chrystins-journal.480/post-11219>

Welcome to the road to optimal.....

### [email redacted] — 2012-04-04T15:55:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chrystins-journal.480/post-11240>

Love from the master...nice.

So, to expand for anyone who's listening or interested or, hell, just for posterity...

ME: I think my problem is my thyroid- possibly hashimoto's (kids have sub clinical gluten allergy by IgG and IgE-I suspect I may have it too, but I haven't been tested yet), screwing up everything else down the line (adrenals and hormones).

HER: She thinks my problem is solely hormonal-specifically low progesterone. She's undecided about the thyroid, wants more testing. She didn't really see too much of a cortisol issue.

ME: What I saw were cortisol numbers "in range" for morning noon and evening, but in the completely wrong part of the range (lower than optimal) and then high out of range for the night levels. Thyroid similar with T3 at the rock bottom of the range. BUT, I totally agree with her that one day's salivary cortisol testing and blood spot thyroid test is insufficient to move forward with.

HER: She thinks I may be oversupplementing with Vit D (I take 8000 IU/day after my level came back at 50).

ME: I would like my VitD levels to be higher.

I did not give her details about the Leptin Rx. I didn't feel she would be receptive and I sure as hell didn't show her my lipid results. I really didn't think she would feel warm and fuzzy about them, even though I think they're ok. She offered to put me on a "shake detox" and offered me access to the infrared sauna (to "burn extra calories", even bigger sigh), but I must have had quite a look on my face because she didn't bring it up again.

SO, had my thyroid drawn last night and will very impatiently wait for next Wednesday, when we have a phone appt to discuss the results. What i'm hoping for, honestly, is for my suspicions about my thyroid to be borne out in my lab results. To me, that would best explain my symptoms and the gradual decline of my emotional and physical health and well being over the past 3 or so years.

### [email redacted] — 2012-04-11T20:13:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chrystins-journal.480/post-13823>

Just shoot me. Full thyroid panel. totally normal. HS crp 0.6. Me-honest to god the fattest I've ever been outside of pregnancy. Totally out of control. Doing month long salivary hormone testing now. Testosterone was accidentally added to my last lab req and was low.

I feel totally, utterly helpless and hopeless. My obsession with my weight and food continues to grow... I was so hoping that my emotional issues were caused by an underfunctioning thyroid.

### [email redacted] — 2012-08-13T22:22:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chrystins-journal.480/post-44759>

Im baaaack...four months later. I am tempted to default to the phrase"off the wagon" but I prefer to think that this has been the path that I've needed to take. I've been working with my naturopath, reading (hormones, adrenals, thyroid, as well as almost everything martha beck has written. After much testing that revealed not much (to my naturopath, anyway) and talks with her about my desire to treat my significant hormonal deficiency symptoms, rather than my numbers and her attempts to treat me homeopathically, supplementally and pretty much every other manner _except_ BIHT...it would seem that I've gotten nowhere. But, again, I don't wish to frame it that way. Its more like when we tell laboring moms (after hours of active labor) that they are still at the same dilation they were hours before...a LOT has been happening, changing and progressing. The baby has been turning, preparing, secreting hormones, readying itself for the journey. Measuring progress by dilation alone is a mistake, albeit the only one we are able to accomplish.

I have tried the following over the course of the last few months...

T3, vitex, maca, adrenal support, sepia (homeopathic), pregnenolone, inaddition to supplementing with mag citrate, b complex, c, krill oil, vit E, a women's multi...

My naturopath is hesitant to pull the trigger on hormones...despite my low progesterone and absent testosterone and encouragement that I AM NOT AFRAID, CONCERNED OR WORRIED ABOUT GOING ON BIHT FOR THE REST OF MY DAYS!!!!

### [email redacted] — 2012-08-13T22:37:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chrystins-journal.480/post-44763>

Today!

BAB. 7:30 AM. Not within 30 minutes of waking. Need to get up b4 my kids for that! 4 eggs w greens and onions in CO, 3.5 slices niman ranch pepper bacon. 1 slice cantaloupe, 1 cherry. cup coffee w half and half (until i get cream in the house)

CT in tub up to ribs about 54 degree water for 20 minutes. Not bad considering I haven't done it in many months. Although, I have been taking cold showers a couple of time a week.

2PM kombucha

Am feeling hungry late afternoon, but will wait until dinner--scallops!!

Dinner5:30. SCallops, greens, guacamole, couple slices of watermelon and cantaloupe, coffee

Im baaaack...four months later. I am tempted to default to the phrase "off the wagon" but I prefer to think that this has been the path that I've needed to take. I've been working with my naturopath, reading (hormones, adrenals, thyroid, as well as almost everything martha beck has written. After much testing that revealed not much (to my naturopath, anyway) and talks with her about my desire to treat my significant hormonal deficiency symptoms, rather than my numbers and her attempts to treat me homeopathically, supplementally and pretty much every other manner except_ BIHT...it would seem that I've gotten nowhere. But, again, I don't wish to frame it that way. Its more like when we tell laboring moms (after hours of active labor) that they are still at the same dilation they were hours before...a LOT has been happening, changing and progressing. The baby has been turning, preparing, secreting hormones, readying itself for the journey. Measuring progress by dilation alone is a mistake, albeit the only one we are able to accomplish.

I have tried the following over the course of the last few months...

T3, vitex, maca, adrenal support, sepia (homeopathic), pregnenolone, in addition to supplementing with mag citrate, b complex, c, krill oil, vit E, a women's multi...

My naturopath is hesitant to pull the trigger on hormones...despite my low progesterone and absent testosterone and encouragement that I AM NOT AFRAID, CONCERNED OR WORRIED ABOUT GOING ON BIHT FOR THE REST OF MY DAYS!!!!

So, my next step (after I read Wiley's book and Ageless-I've already read both Uzzi Reiss books) is to try out this local doc who is part of the "anti aging" crew, if you will. Its in Woburn, MA---called BodyLogic. If anyone has any experience with these guys, please let me know. It is about 2 hours away and I am done wasting my time and, of course, money!! OMG!! I just looked this guy up and, guess what????? He is paleo!!!!!!

Moving on, I am back in the tub. And the whole seafood/seaweed thing intrigues me. SO I'm back in. And I've read up on all of the blogs I've missed.

As someone with disordered eating issues (as much as I detest labelling myself that way, I'm not sure how else to say it), I was wary of participating in a program that was so limiting, being that I felt that was how my issues began. But, now I'm thinking that those issues began along with and as part of my hormonal cluster \*\*\*\* and were not caused directly by my food limiting.

### [email redacted] — 2012-08-15T20:18:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chrystins-journal.480/post-45046>

Crap, thought I posted, but it isn't here! Oh well. Body Logic graciously let me back out and is refunding my money....Now just waiting for the OBGYN listed on the Wiley site to call me back. Says she's certified antiaging. I'm all for that.

If anyone in New England wants to contribute any paleo/primal/Kruse friendly docs/professionals/businesses, I'd like to create a resource for newbies--less legwork and trial and error for those just getting on board. Let's face it, they have enough to occupy their time and energy...CT, epi-paleo, reading the blogs, reading the forum, sleeping, lights out.....

Back to all important me, LOL...

I did very well after lunch yesterday. Loaded up for lunch, expecting to skip dinner because I was working. Total success, didn't even think about food and went right to bed when I got home at 9:45ish.

This morning 5:30 BAB. Scrambled eggs with leftover pork chops and greens that were cooked in butter and CO and coated with [sea shakes](http://http://www.injoynow.com/seashakes/index.html). Love that stuff. Sea salt and seaweed seasoning. Also coffee, a ramekin of watermelon cubes.

12:30 lunchish....I made a blueberry (we picked em this morning-doesn't get more seasonal than that!) coconut flour "cake" for my kids. Really simple, coco flour, salt, baking soda, blueberries, vanilla, eggs and maple syrup (naughty) Had a few spoons of that with . Not total compliance, there, but I thought I'd take that opportunity to have another dose of magnesium and pregnenolone and some kombucha maybe. Moving on...

Dinner will be sockeye salmon ceviche! I'm going to top it with a caponata-style salad- red onion, garlic, tomato, green and black olives. Really excited 'bout that.

I've been dumping tons of water the past two days. Amazing how fast the water leaves once clean eating commences, especially after a week of vacation filled with crap. We made a lot of awesome food, too, but it was accompanied by lots of crap.

### DarleenMB — 2012-08-15T20:40:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chrystins-journal.480/post-45052>

> **kathylu;45292 wrote:** Darlene, do you have a period with the Wiley protocol? Why did you decide to do this rather than other BHRT methods? Thanks!

No periods because I don't have any parts. And I chose this because after I read Wiley's book I realized that conventional BHRT would NOT be of much help. It just makes so much sense to me that if you want the protection your hormones provide you HAVE to have them at the levels where they gave you the most protection, i.e., in your glorious 20's when you were at your childbearing peak years.

I swear I was already showing symptoms of early dementia before I got on the WP. I could not remember ANYthing and my reading comprehension was down to about nothing. I was walking through each day in a total mental fog. It's much better now but I don't know if I will ever get back to full mental functioning. It's extremely frustrating. So when I say they can have my BHRT/WP when they can pry it from my cold, dead hands I am NOT kidding.

### [email redacted] — 2012-08-16T01:40:31+0300

Yeah. That's what I'm talking about. Round it off with a tad pinot grigio and a mixed blueberry watermelon cantaloupe salad. Ahhh. Next up coffee and that's all folks! Did my CT late this afternoon. About 25 minutes to the navel of 53 or so degree water.

Thanks, ABM. Haven't scheduled the consult yet. Want to get my labs from my naturopath. But I just finished writing up my healh hx, so, 1 step closer.

Something else I realized when i was writing up my hx... Since starting reset again, my desire for coffee has gone waaaay down. Like 1 cup a day is enough. I was never a really massive coffee drinker and I never drank it because i felt I "needed" it to wake up. I just enjoyed it. Max 4 cups/day. But now, not so much....interesting...

Also, may I say, that EVERYONE should read Wiley's book "Sex, lies and menopause". Male, female, old, young, whatever. Amazing. Such a great explanation of circadian biology, how we've moved away from how we were optimally designed to live and why we need hormones if we want to live a long, healthy life. To sum up...you cannot alter natural, biologic processes without consequences. We have f\*\*\*\*d with mother nature. Thanks so much to those of you who suggested reading it.

### [email redacted] — 2012-08-27T17:06:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chrystins-journal.480/post-47485>

In the tub this am...first time I've been back to update journal since consult. Super busy weekend and soooooo much to think about. First off, consult was mind blowing. Glad I recorded it as some suggested. If you do a consult you HAVE to find a way to record it. Even if you have to buy something to do it! I just used garage band. Thanks to dr k, I can no longer live in denial. My problems are fixable, but if I don't fix them I will be one sick puppy! My main issues are 1. Light and 2. Hormones. I was told the the only thing probably keeping me from being worse off than I am is my diet. Scary.

So, morning light in my eyes 3-5 minutes (I'm apparently wicked sensitive to light), Bab or hab, 4-5 hrs btwn last meal and bed. Orgasm morning and nite. Need to get my estrogenrogesterone flipped. Start taking d again. One of the most interesting parts was when he said (paraphrasing). " you think that your diet is what's making you fat...it's really the light issues that are making you fat."

He also said if I did a FBg, it would be over 88.

It was 89.

Totally totally totally worth it.

Issues that remain...

I work at nite often (like 6-9:30) in an brightly lit retail store. I have no problem eating b4 work and leaving sufficient time between meal and bed. But what about the light?

I am holding out hope that this new doc will give me everything I need, accept some of the recommendations I received from dr k and help me get back to optimal. I am waiting for the lab reqs to come. Had new est, prog, dhea, testosterone drawn on Saturday for the naturopath. Maybe I'll take the consult to her first...... I just don't know

### [email redacted] — 2012-08-30T20:17:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chrystins-journal.480/post-48412>

Ugh. Back and forth, back and forth. I'd like this relationship with my naturopath to continue. She is a very caring practitioner, she just needs some more knowledge. In attempts to school her, I paid about ten bucks to have my consult transcribed online and it is a disaster. SO much for that. Will have to take another approach, I guess! I don't want to waste anymore time. The Wiley doc wants me to do a 24 hour urine and a thyroid panel that she wants sent to a special lab. The 24 hour urine cant be done at TOM (currently) and must be eight days away from any supps. So I'm looking at a week b4 I can do that. They thyroid draw must be sent spun and frozen, which the lab by her will do for me. It is 2 hours away. Just for a blood draw. I really would like my naturopath to get on board. My next step is to just send her the recording....And the Suzanne Somers book.

Interesting update....

So, I've had this gnawing sensation (almost like hunger-goes away if I eat) that flares up from time to time. Been to the ER, talked to the naturopath. Everyone's \*\*\*\*ing clueless about it. Listening to my consult again, Dr. K mentioned that, with my cravings he wondered if I might have a passenger in my gut-namely candida or H pylori. How come no one else ever thought that with my flare ups!! Jeez! Its not like H pylori is uncommon...at all! I'm going to demand that my naturopath test me for it. I'm having a flare up now. And I'm eating ice cream (where's the smiley for shame?).

Waiting for naturopath to call me back as my labs were in yesterday. My approach is going to be that I really want to continue being treated by her, but in order to do so, I need her to start me on BHRT...stat! She's been trying everything else since April. That's 6 months _just since i've been seeking her treatment. I was suffering for years before I went to see her_.
